Sining Bulilit 2015
Grade School Building
UP Integrated School (UPIS)
10:30 a.m.-12:30 p.m.
Sining Bulilit is an Art Enrichment Program that caters to two age groups : 6-8 years old and 9-11 years old. Young pupils are taught various art activities by Art Education graduate students of the College of Education.
Abril 13-Mayo 22
(Lunes-Miyerkules-Biyernes)
Reading Link : Beginning Reading Program
UPIS Grade 3 to 6 Building
8-10:30 a.m. 
The Beginning Reading Program of Reading Link is designed for incoming Kindergarten (5.6 years old) to Grade 2 pupils who need more assistance to prepare them for reading. The program is handled by the faculty and graduate students of the Reading Area of the College of Education. 
Abril 13-Mayo 22
(Lunes-Miyerkules-Biyernes)
Reading Link : Reading Enrichment Program
UPIS Grade 3 to 6 Building
8-10:30 a.m.
The Reading Enrichment Program of Reading Link is meant for incoming Grades 3 to 7 students. The program is facilitated by the faculty and graduate students of the Reading Area of the College of Education. 
Abril 13-17
Mining, Metallurgical and Materials (MMM) Engineering Days 2015
The MMM Days 2015 is the most anticipated week-long celebration of the 78th anniversary of the Department of Mining, Metallurgical and Materials Engineering (DMMME). This year's celebration also showcases DMMME's contribution to the society and promotes the department by organizing fun-filled activites. 
Abril 13-17
Civil Engineering (CE) Days
The CE Days is the biggest and the most awaited annual institute-wide event attended by UP's civil engineering students and faculty. It is where students, instructors and professors are able to showcase their individual talents and where camaraderie and unity among the institute's constituents are built. 
Abril 14
Weak or Strong State : The Implications of Self-organization in a Disaster-Prone Community in the Philippines
College of Mass Communication A101 
3:30 - 5 p.m.
The paper is an offshoot of an earlier study on the manifestations of self-organization in the community of Banaba, San Mateo, Rizal in their response to Tropical Storm Ketsana (local name Ondoy). 
Abril 14-17
Electrical and Electronics Engineering Institute Days
The Electrical and Electronics Engineering Institute (EEEI) Days is a week-long event designed to promote camaraderie and community spirit among the EEEI student body, faculty and staff. Every year, the EEEI Day draws hundreds of EEEI students and faculty to participate in its unique and enjoyable events and gatherings. 
Abril 14-17
2015 Summer Training-Seminar for Social Science Teachers 
CSSP Audio-Visual Room
Palma Hall
The College of Social Sciences and Philosophy (CSSP) and the Social Science and Philosophy Research Foundation, Inc. (SSPRF) will be holding the 2015 Summer Training-Seminar for Social Science Teachers. 
Abril 15
CLRG@50 Lecture Series : ICTs in Local Governments by Dr. Remigio D. Ocenar
Assembly Hall
National College of Public Administration and Governance (NCPAG)
2 p.m.
The CLRG@50 Lecture Series is held in celebration of the 50th anniversary of the NCPAG Center for Local and Regional Governance (CLRG). 
Abril 16
Myanmar Planning : First-Hand Impressions (SURP 50th Anniversary Public Lecture Series)
Cariño Multi-Purpose Hall
School of Urban and Regional Planning (SURP)
6 p.m. 
The talk is the fourth in the SURP 50th Anniversary Public Lecture Series and will be presented by Cesar B. Umali Jr. 
Abril 16-17
ISKOpreneur 2015 : Iskasaysayan
UP Clark Courtyard
UPD Extension Program in Pampanga 
ISKOpreneur is an annual fair that showcases the entrepreneurial skills and innovative products of the senior Business Management students of UP Diliman Extension Program in Pampanga. 
Abril 17-Mayo 20
(Lunes-Miyerkules-Biyernes)
UP EDTEG Summer Fun
Classes 2015
K-12 Building
Kinder : 8-11 a.m.
Grades 1-3 : 9 a.m. - 12 p.m.
The summer workshop for kids caters to Kindergarten (5 years old) to incoming Grade 3 kids who will be engaged in integrated developmentally appropriate literacy, arts, numeracy, science, music and physical activities facilitated by the faculty and graduate students of the Teaching in the Early Grades (TEG) area of the College of Education.
Abril 17-Hulyo 17
(Lahat ng Biyernes)
Management Development Program for Coops, NGOs and Trade Unions 
School of Labor and Industrial Relations (SOLAIR) 
9 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The certificate course in Management Development Program is designed to build the managerial, leadership and entrepreneurial skills primarily for cooperatives, NGOs and trade unions. 
Abril 17, 20-24
Speech and Theatre Fest 2015: SPECTRUM
A SPECTRUM is a group of colors that can be separated from red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo and violet. These colors can be a byproduct of a prism. Just like a prism, the Department of Speech Communication and Theatre Arts produces different kinds of students passionate in different disciplines. This year's Speech and Theatre Fest aims to showcase what these disciplines are. 
Abril 18
Social Studies Education and the K to 12 Curriculum : Trends and Challenges 
Benitez Theater 
College of Education
9 a.m. - 4 p.m. 
The conference provides a venue to share best practices and present developments and results of research projects in Social Studies Education. It aims to promote collaboration and connection among scholars. educators and administrators in the field of Social Studies. 
Abril 20
Ayala Corporation - UP School of Economics Forum (AC-UPSE Economic Forum) : A Case for the Philippine Competition Law
Ayala Museum, Makati City
3 p.m.
The AC-UPSE Economic Forum is a quarterly forum organized by the UPSE with support from a grant by the Ayala Corporation. The program encourages and publicly disseminates applied economic research on factors that promote sustained economic growth in the Philippines. Presentations involve a variety of topics affecting the Philippines and the world economy. 
Abril 20-25
Computer Science (CS) Week
A week-long event for the CS students, faculty and staff. 
Abril 21
Korean War Films : From Unending War to the Peace Movement 
College of Mass Communication Auditorium 
10:30 a.m. - 12 noon
The paper represents the current research of Dr. John R. Eperjesi on the Korean War in both US and South Korean culture. 
Abril 21 
Ang Pagbabagong-bihis ng mga Aklat Pambata
Panayam ni Eugene Y. Evasco
Pulungang Claro M. Recto, Bulwagang Rizal
Kolehiyo ng Arte at Literatura
1-2:30 p.m. 
Sa panayam na ito ni Dr. Eugene Y. Evasco, tatangkaing suriin ang mga bagong panitikang pambata sa anyo ng ebooks o kaya'y picture book applications. Interactivity ang pangunahing pagtutuunan ng pansin sa mga tekstong ito. Tatalakayin dito ang natatanging uri ng mga "aklat na may teknolohiya." Ibig sabihin, nakinabang ang mga aklat pambata sa mga pagbabagong dulot ng teknolohiya gaya ng animation, touch screen technology, musika at games. Sa huli, maglalatag ng tanong at hamon ang pag-aaral kung may tulong pa ang mga pagbabagong ito upang makapanghikayat ng mga mambabasa sa hanay ng kabataan. 
Abril 21
Alimbukay : An Acoustic Cafe
Palma Hall Rm. 400
1-4 p.m. 
Guests will be served with free coffee and tea while Original Pilipino Music and Japanese cover bands entertain them with popular acoustic tunes. 
Abril 21-24
Engaging in Advocacy Work : An Essential Skill for Social Development 
CSWCD Seminar Room 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
A College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD) through the Research and Extension for Development Office (REDO) initiaterd training. 
Abril 24
UPSE 50th Anniversary Fellowship Night : Bond for Gold 
School of Economics (SE) Auditorium
6 p.m. 
A gathering of the UPSE alumni, students, faculty, staff and friends for a night of fellowship and renewal of camaraderie. This is held in celebration of UPSE's 50th anniversary. 
Abril 24
Uli-Uli : A Cultura Night
Sunken Garden
6-9 p.m. 
An evening of cultural performances and traditional games of Philippines and Japan. 
Abril 25
"Impact of Transatlantic Investment Treaties on Labor Relations"
School of Labor and Industrial Relations (SOLAIR) 
1-4 p.m.
SOLAIR hosts the Annual Lecture Series featuring national and international experts from the academe, the government and the industry sectors. 
The lecture impact of Transatlantic Investment Treaties on Labor Relations focuses on the extent to which corporations make use of the dispute settlement process of international investment treaties to challenge specific aspects of labor law (e.g. minimum wage).
Mayo 4
Parada ng Parangal
Academic Oval
5 p.m.
Hudyat ng pagsisimula ng Linggo ng Parangal sa pamamagitan ng parada ng magpapatingkad sa mga tagumpay at karangalang inihatid ng iba't ibang yunit ng UP Diliman sa nakalipas na taon, habang sama-samang sasalubong nang buong-sigla at sigasig sa mga darating pang panahon. 
Mayo 5
Chancellor's International Reception
UP Theater Lobby 
5:30 p.m.
An annual event that brings together UPD officials and members of its international community, comprising of visiting students, faculty and researchers. With some members of the diplomatic corps in attendance, the event will also be the opportunity for UPD to promote its academic, research and extension programs. Highlights of the event are the performances by foreign students from Asia, Europe and the Americas. To be featured too are cultural presentations by UPD students. 
Mayo 6
Parangal sa mga Mag-aaral
GT-Toyota Asian Center 
2 p.m. 
Isang pagbibigay-pugay at pagkilala sa mga natatanging mag-aaral na nagpamalas ng katalinuhan at kagalingan sa kanilang mga gawaing pangmag-aaral. 
Mayo 7
Parangal sa mga Retirado at Gawad Paglilingkod 
GT-Toyota Asian Center 
1 p.m.
Bilang pagpapahalaga at pasasalamat sa mga retirado at mga kawaning lubusang nag-aalay ng kanilang buong kakayahan sa pagtataguyod ng adhikain ng Unibersidad. 
Mayo 8
Gawad Tsanselor
Institute of Biology Auditorium 
2 p.m. 
Isang hapon ng pagdiriwang at pagkilala sa kahusayan, tagumpay at serbisyo ng kaguruan, REPS, kawaning administratibo at mag-aaral ng Unibersidad ng Pilipinas. Pinakatampok na araw sa Linggo ng Parangal. 
Mayo 4
UP Diliman-wide Extension Colloquium
Bulwagang Tandang Sora
College of Social Work and Community Development
11 a.m.-5 p.m.
A sharing of extension work and practices, perspectives, structrure and organization among UPD units to generate and enrich discussions for policy recommendations in line with the public thrust of the University.
Mayo 5-6
Academe Meets the Unions : Enhancing Labor Protection in Asia's Global Production Networks
University Hotel
9 a.m.
A workshop of leading Asian academics and researchers in the field of transnational global production networks (GPNs) in the Asia-Pacific region and the leading Asian labor voices advocating for social protection and labor rights throughout the GPN chain of production, particularly in the automotive, electronics/semiconductors and garments/textiles industries. 
Mayo 7
Evolution of the Philippine Working Class Struggle and Labor Movement 
Bonifacio Hall
School of Labor and Industrial Relations (SOLAIR) 
9 a.m. 
A forum in honor of Crisanto Evangelista organized by SOLAIR. 
The forum gathers academics, labor historians and labor movement leaders to analyze and discuss the evolution of the Philippine working class struggle and labor movement. 
Mayo 10-17
The 54th UP National Writers Workshop 
Baguio City
An annual literary workshop held every summer in Baguio City by the Likhaan : University of the Philippines Institute of Creative Writing (UP ICW). The fellows, associates and advisers of the UP ICW select 12 writers in mid-career to participate in this workshop.
Mayo 11-15
A Basic Course in Urban and Regional Planning
School of Urban and Regional Planning (SURP)
The SURP Training and Extension Division holds its second run of the training program A Basic Course in Urban and Regional Planning (ABC in URP). The training program is an introductory course on the basic concepts, processes and methodologies of urban and regional planning and provides updates on the latest planning approaches, tools and techniques. 
Mayo 13
CLRG@50 Lecture Series : Devolution by Nestor N. Pilar
Assembly Hall
National College of Public Administration and Governance 
10 a.m. 
The CLRG@50 Lecture Series is held in celebration of the 50th anniversary of the Center for Local and Regional Governance (CLRG) of the National College of Public Administration and Governance (NCPAG).
The lecture series aims to revisit the past issues and challenges on local governance as it relates to the present. Featured speakers are CLRG's past and present directors.
Mayo 14
Learning Mathematics Through Origami
Dolores F. Hernandez Hall
Science Teacher Training Center, NISMED, 2 p.m. 
Learning Mathematics Through Origami is a public lecture by Patsy Wang-Iverson, Vice President for Special Projects at the Gabriella & Paul Rosenbaum Foundation. Her work focuses on improving mathematics and science education by learning from practices around the world, including jugyokenkyuu (translated as lesson study) and origami. 
Mayo 15 
Paliwanagan sa UP Diliman : OVCRD Colloquium Series 
Quezon Hall
3-7 p.m. 
A bi-annual activity of the Office of the Vice Chancellor for Research and Development (OVCRD).
The colloquium aims to create greater public awareness and appreciation of UPD's research and development initiatives and programs. 
Mayo 20
Need for and Principle Contents of a National Transport Policy Act 
(SURP 50th Anniversary Public Lecture Series) 
Cariño Multi-Purpose Hall
School of Urban and Regional Planning (SURP) 
6 p.m. 
Presented by Dr. Primitivo C. Cal, it is the fifth in the SURP 50th Anniversary Public Lecture Series which commenced in January and will continue until October 2015. 
Mayo 26-29
Enhancing the Supervisor's Capability in Mentoring and Coaching 
CSWCD Seminar Room
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The 4-day, live-out training aims to enhance the knowledge, skills and attitudes of middle level supervisors in providing the needed support to improve the functioning of the supervisees. The focus is on making them proactive in intervening when there are performance gaps among the supervisees and systematically and purposively providing opportunities for their growth in their work. 
Mayo 26-29
Data Analysis Using SPSS 
(Beginner's Course)
Computer Laboratory
UP Population Institute (UPPI)
Room 334, Palma Hall 
The course is designed to provide an intensive introduction to the latest version of the Statistical Package for the Social Sciences (SPSS), now known as IBM SPSS Statistics. SPSS is a user-friendly Windows-based statistical software and a powerful and versatile tool for data analysis. The training combines lecture and hands-on laboratory sessions and involves an analysis of a subset of a large dataset. 
Mayo 27-29
2nd Training Workshop on Legal Research and Library Services Made Ready
UP Law Library 
Organized by the UP Law Library and the AASDI (Asosasyon ng Aklatan at Sinupan ng Diliman), the 3-day training workshop on legal research and library services uses informative and interactive discussions, networking sesssions and activities to allow participants to interact, inspire and learn from one another.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 1, Blg 3 (5 copies)
column7:
Academic clusters organize faculty workshops on UPD GE framework
Hunyo 10
Arts and Letters cluster
9 a.m. - 12 p.m.
Science and Technology cluster
NEC Audiovisual Room
1-5 p.m.
Hunyo 11
Management and Economics cluster
AIT Seminar Room
8 a.m. - 12 p.m. 
Social Sciences and Law cluster
Palma Hall 207
1 - 5 p.m. 
In each workshop, the academic units concerned are expected to review and revise the General Education (GE) framework. This will entail looking at the context/rationale for revising the GE program, the philosophy/definition of general education, the pedagogy and different modes of inquiry for teaching GE courses, the capabilities that the GE program should develop among students, the system for classifying GE courses and the implementation scheme for the revised GE program. 
Hunyo 15
The State of Penal Administration in the Philippines : Prospects for Reforms 
NCPAG Assembly Hall
2 p.m.
Anniversary Week
In celebration of the 63rd anniversary of the National College of Public Administration and Governance (NCPAG), the college is hosting a lecture "The State of Penal Administration in the Philippines : Prospects for Reforms" by Dr. Raymund E. Narag. Narag is an NCPAG alumnus and currently a visiting professor at the college. He obtained his Ph.D. from Michigan State University School of Criminal Justice in the United States as a Fulbright Scholar. 
Hunyo 15-18
Handling Children at Risk in UP Campus by UPD Security Personnel
CSWCD Seminar Room
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The training is in line with the university's mandate of being a public service university providing various forms of public and volunteer service. This is also in support of UPD's vision for a safe, nurturing, shared, connected and sustainable spaces for its constituents. 
Hunyo 17-19
Worker's Institute on Labor Laws
Bonifacio Hall, SOLAIR
9 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The 3-day seminar covers a wide range of topics including : (a) legal rights of workers and management prerogatives; (b) allowable job contracting; (c) collective bargaining concepts, practices and issues; (d) unfair labor practices; (e) labor-management conflict resolution; (f) hours and conditions of work; (g) wages and wage-related benefits; (h) social welfare benefits; (i) just causes of termination of employment; and (j) resignation and retirement.
Hunyo 18
Pag-inog at Pagsulong : Ika-107 Kaarawan ng UP
University Theater
7 p.m.
Isang gabi ng pagtatanghal ng mga mag-aaral, guro at kawani ng Unibersidad ng Pilipinas-Diliman, sa direksyon ni Prop. Josefina Estrella.
Ang UP ay umiinog sa tatlong saligang adhikain : pagtuturo, pananaliksik at gawaing ekstensyon. Ang tatlong ito ang bumubuo sa kaibuturan ng tungkulin ng UP sa paglilingkod sa sambayanan. Kung kaya't marapat lamang na sa tuwing sumasapit ang kaarawan ng pagkakatatag ng Unibersidad ay balikan, pagnilayan at ipagbunyi ang ambag ng UP sa ikauunlad ng bansang Pilipinas. 
Hunyo 18
"Nagsimula sa Puso" digitally restored and re-mastered
Cine Adarna
7 p.m.
The restored feature, shot in UP Diliman, premieres at the Cine Adarna of the UPFI Film Center in UPD at 7 p.m. on June 18, most fittingly as UP marks its 107th Foundation Day. 
Hunyo 18
Twelve Hundred Miles
College of Mass Communication Auditorium 
3-5 p.m. 
Twelve Hundred Miles (2015, 44 minutes) is an experimental documentary by Joni Gutierrez which juxtaposes the footage from Beijing shot on June 4, 2014 with the Occupy Central protests in Hong Kong (HK) in the same year. It exhibits a perspective of a foreign student faced with an overwhelming need to understand the intricacies of HK-mainland relations. 
Hunyo 25
Capacity Building Seminar on Scholarly Research and Publication
3/F UP College of Law Library
Espiritu Hall
7:30 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
Research is integral for the growth of any profession as it anchors the established principles and serves to strengthen its foundations. It enforces, challenges, or in some cases, refutes established dogma and theories from which professional practice arises. 
The seminar, organized by the UP Law Library and the AASDI (Asosasyon ng Aklatan at Sinupan ng Diliman), is designed and developed to assist Library Information System (LIS) professionals in conducting, writing and publishing their research. The (LIS) profession, like any other discipline, benefits from research as it learns which theories, principles and methodologies work, or do not. 
Hunyo 26
ICT Skills and Innovations for Libraries 
3/F UP College of Law Library
Espiritu Hall
7:30 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Rapidly changing technology and evolving user needs and habits compel Library Information System (LIS) professionals to always be at the forefront of these developments to better understand and serve the library users. Improving the LIS professionals' skills in the Information and Communication Technology (ICT) allows them to have more confidence in exploring the vast landscape the technology offers, while viewpoints from outside the profession enables them to have a fresh take on how the library and its services are seen externally. This renewed perspective, coupled with the improved ICT skills, help LIS professionals be more creative in designing and creating programs to improve the users experience in the library. 
UPD academic units' recognition rites schedule
Hunyo 25
Hunyo 26
Hunyo 27
Hunyo 28
104th UPD General Commencement Exercises
"Pag-uugat, Pag-uugnay, Pagyabong"
UPD Amphitheater
7:30 a.m. 
Speaker : Bro. Armin A. Luistro, FSC
Secretary, Department of Education
Hunyo 28
Hunyo 29
Hunyo 30
Call for papers for The 16th Biennial Symposium on Literature and Culture in the Asia-Pacific Region
While all literatures explore the forbidden, this Symposium scheduled in November seeks to investigate its implications specifically for the literatures, cultures and perspectives of the Asia-Pacific region seen by scholars on and in the region. It invites research n impropriety in all its forms, from mischief and rule-breaking to crime, sin and subversion. It also celebrates writing itself as a dangerous or deadly habit, a form of deviant behavior, infiltration or transgression.
Hunyo 30 - Hulyo 3
Enhancing the Supervisor's Capability in Mentoring and Coaching
CSWCD Seminar Room
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The training is intended for supervisors of organizations involved in social development. The training course is designed to enhance the knowledge, attitude and skills of supervisors in providing the necessary support to improve the performance of their supervisees. It also aims to make the participants to be more proactive in intervening whenever there are performance gaps among their staff, thereby providing guidance and motivation for improvement of their work. 
Hulyo 24
Talasalitaan : Ulat sa CHED Memo Blg. 20 s2013 at Istatus ng Implementasyon ng Programang K-12 ng DepEd
3/F SURP Building
1-4 p.m.
Isang panayam hinggil sa pinakabagong pangyayari sa implementasyon ng Commission on Higher Education (CHED) Memo Blg. 20 s2013 at ng Programang K-12 ng Department of Education. 
Hulyo 24-28
2015 National Science and Technology Week
SMX Convention Center, Mall of Asia, Pasay City, 11 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
UP Diliman, through the Research Dissemination and Utilization Office of the Vice Chancellor for Research and Development (RDUO-OVCRD), participates in the 2015 National Science and Technology Week (NSTW) organized by the Department of Science and Technology's Technology Application and Promotion Institute (DOST-TAPI).

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 1, Blg 4 (3 copies)
column7:
Agosto 1-31
Buwan ng Wika
Wikasabihan at Bugtungan sa Diliman
Pagpaskil ng mga kasabihang Filipino at mga bugtong sa palibot ng Academic Oval. Mayroong timpalak para sa mga mag-aaral ng UP Diliman na kailangang sagutin ang bugtong sa pamamagitan ng Facebook at text message. 
Aklatang Bayan 
Pagligid ng Aklatang Bayan ng Sentro ng Wikang Filipino (SWF) sa mga piling institusyon at kolehiyo sa UP Diliman para magbenta ng mga publikasyon ng SWF sa mababang halaga. 
Palihan ng mga Komite sa Wika
Palihan at kumustahan ng mga kinatawan ng Komite sa Wika sa iba't ibang kolehiyo sa UP Diliman
Talastagisan 
Modernong balagtasan tampok ang mga pangkat ng Fliptoppers ng Tanghalang Bayan ng Kulturang Kalye (TABAKK) at mga pagtatangghal ng mga kultural na grupong mag-aaral ng UP Diliman. 
Agosto 1
College of Human Kinetics (CHK) Community Recreation Program 
University Gym
The Community Recreation Program is a sports and wellness extension class offering of the College per semester, where CHK faculty members and students share their expertise with the whole UP community.
Agosto 5
Dulaang UP's 40th Theatre Season Launch
20/20 Vision : Clarity, Originality, Perspective 
Wilfrido Ma. Guerrero Theater
2nd floor, Palma Hall
2 p.m.
Agosto 5
14 Silent Comics
3/F South Wing Gallery, Vargas Museum 
4 p.m.
14 Silent Comics is Manuel "Manix" Abrera's solo exhibition that presents silent comics (comics without dialogue). Characters from Philippine mythology like the manananggal, white lady, duwende and kapre are portrayed in a different light through stories that cast them as protagonists. They are either funny or philosophical, caught up in circumstances where they meet different creatures, mortal and otherwise. 
Agosto 5
Jorge Vargas : A Collecting Life : Opening of a new Permanent Exhibition 
Library and Archives, Vargas Museum
4 p.m.
The exhibition presents three central themes in Jorge Vargas's practices of collecting : Kawilihan (Fascination), Kasaysayan (History) and Kasanayan (Discipline). 
Agosto 5
Breeding Ground
1F West Wing Gallery, Vargas Museum 
4 p.m.
Breeding Ground focuses on the contested nature of land and how it is represented and appropriated. It presents the works of Fernando Amorsolo, Adjani Arumpac, Mark Maestro, Dominic Mangila and Jorge Vargas.
Agosto 5
Hand Made Violence
1F Lobby, Vargas Museum
4 p.m. 
Hand Made Violence is Bembol Dela Cruz's solo exhibition that presents confrontation with guns in two forms : first, in metal sheet with inscriptions in Braille; second, in painting. By asking users of unlicensed guns to describe their guns, Dela Cruz develops a "cartographic sketch" that makes the gun acquire a criminal face. 
Agosto 11
A Nonparametric Option Pricing Model Using High Moments
School of Statistics Auditorium 
4 p.m. 
The colloquium presents the paper of Prof. Peter Julian Cayton on nonparametric model that includes non-Gaussian characteristics of skewness and kurtosis. The model is proposed based on the cubic market capital asset pricing model. 
Agosto 13
Kaligtasan, Kabuhayan, Karapatan : Mga Batang Manggagawa sa Tubuhan
Bulwagang Tandang Sora
College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD)
8 a.m. - 3 p.m.
Organized by the UP Social Action for Research and Development Foundation, Inc. (UPSARDFI) of the CSWCD, the forum aims to disseminate the highlights of the research findings from the studies undertaken by the ABK3-UPSARDFI and University of St. La Salle Bacolod; gather feedback from the various stakeholders regarding implications of the research results; and identify recommendations for possible policy and program reforms to improve child labor conditions in sugarcane industry.  
Agosto 13
Comparative Conundrum Between Nation-State and Muslim Governance
A Public Lecture by Nassef Adiong, PhD
Conference Room
Institute of Islamic Studies
9 a.m. - 12 p.m.
The study finds compelling points of contentions in comparing both nation-state and Muslim governance in terms of the 1933 Montevideo criteria (territoriality, citizenry, authority and sovereignty) including elements of history, constitution, Hallaq's moral predicament with modernity and difficulties with democracy, secularism, capitalism and nationalism. To go beyond this comparative conundrum, the study recommends to theoretically look into selected worldviews such as Alexander Wendt's proposal of a World State theory and various Islamicate civilizational analyses (i.e. from Khaldunian sociological perspective to Senturk's Open Civilization thesis) as alternative world orders in order to encompass an inclusive pluralist understanding of units of analysis in the international system/society (nassef.info).
Agosto 22-Oktubre 10
Pottery Workshop
(7 sessions, every Saturday)
College of Fine Arts 
9 a.m. - 12 p.m. / 1:30 p.m. - 4 :30 p.m. 
Agosto 24-Oktubre 19
Pottery Workshop Batch 1
(7 sessions, every Monday)
College of Fine Arts
9 a.m.-12 p.m. / 1:30 p.m. - 4:30 p.m.
Facilitated by Prof. Romanlito Austria, the workshops aim to introduce students to basic hand-building technique in forming works and in using the wheel. 
Agosto 24-Oktubre 19
Ceramic Sculpture Workshop
(7 sessions, every Monday)
College of Fine Arts
9 a.m. - 12 p.m. / 1:30 p.m. - 4:30 p.m. 
This workshop familiarizes students with the nature, possibilities and processes involved in working with stoneware clay as a medium to create sculptural forms. Facilitated by Prof. Rita Gudiño, the workshop aims to provide opportunities for students to build skills and techniques in hand-building clay sculptures. 
Agosto 24
International Humanitarian Law and Islamic Law on Armed Conflict and Humanitarian Affair
Conference Room
Institute of Islamic Studies 
9 a.m. - 12 p.m.
Organized by the Institute of Islamic Studies with speakers from the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC), the public lecture discusses the Law on Armed Conflict with reference to the International Humanitarian Law and Islamic Law. The lecture explores both the simple and complex attributes of the law for better understanding of lawyers and non-lawyers alike. 
Agosto 24-Nobyembre 23 (Weekday Classes)
Agosto 29-Nobyembre 14 (Saturday Classses) 
UPCM Extension Program
The UP College of Music Extension Program is a non-degree program of study that offers a set of music courses with the goal of providing comprehensive musicianship for youth, adults, musicians, and music educators who are not officially enrolled in the College of Music degree offerings. 
Agosto 25-28
Mandatory Continuing Legal Education
UP Law Center
8 a.m.
The Institute for the Administration of Justice is a research and training center for the improvement of the administration of justice. It actively formulates and conducts Continuing Legal Education programs (MCLE) for a member of the bar to hone their professional competence. It develops and conducts training programs on special areas of law on its own initiative or under special arrangements with other local and international organizations. 
Agosto 26-Setyembre 13
#R</3J : A Multimedial Hallucination on William Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet 
Wilfrido Ma. Guerrero Theater 
2nd floor, Palma Hall
Weekday shows at 7 p.m.
Weekend shows at 10 a.m., 3 p.m. and 8 p.m. 
#R</3J retells the star-crossed lovers' narrative transforming Verona into a concrete jungle at the heart of Metro Manila - teeming with corruption, revolt, advertisements, sex, social media and condominium units. Destroy the city, the family, the government - even Shakespeare - but never destroy the will to love. 
Agosto 26-28
The College of Social Sciences and Philosophy and the Archaeological Studies Program
Call for Participants
Conference on Cultural Heritage : Multidisciplinary Perspectives on Theory and Practice 
August 26-28, 2015
Palma Hall, UP Diliman
Setyembre 3-5
Occupation and Liberation : An International Conference on the Pacific War in the Philippines
GT-Toyota Auditorium
8 a.m. 
The conference aims to bring together scholars of World War II from both national and international communities in order to revisit and re-assess past studies done on the Pacific War in the Philippines; introduce new topics and approaches to the study of World War II; and develop an increased awareness and deeper appreciation for this particular period in Philippine history. 
Setyembre 5
UAAP Season 78
Opening Ceremonies 
Smart-Araneta Coliseum
1 p.m.
The University of the Philippines (UP) is hosting the 78th season of the University Athletic Association of the Philippines (UAAP) for school year 2015-2016 with the theme "Tumitindig, Sumusulong."
Setyembre 5-18
Combined Foundation and Advanced Certificate Course of the Paralegal Training Program on the Philippine Legal System and the Comprehensive Agrarian Reform Program, Laws, Rules, and Procedures
Malcolm Hall
9 a.m. - 6 p.m. 
The UP Law Center, in coordination with the Department of Agrarian Reform, launches the first combined foundation and advanced certificate course of the Paralegal Training Program which focuses and specializes on the fundamentals of law in the Philippines and agrarian reform laws, rules and regulations. 
Setyembre 12-Nobyembre 28
Asian Languages Extramural Classes 2015 Cycle 3 
Setyembre 15-18
Participatory Monitoring and Evaluation of Social Development Projects
Semimar Room
College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD)
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The four-day, live-out seminar workshop aims to equip social development practitioners with knowledge and skills to strengthen people's involvement, enhance their capacities and increase their confidence in the decision-making process. 
The training also aims to enhance the understanding and capabilities of participants in developing appropriate participatory monitoring and evaluation systems for their respective community projects and programs. 
Setyembre 28-29
Diliman-wide GE Conference 
8 a.m. 
The outputs of the cluster workshops held in June and July this year will be presented and deliberated in the 2015 UPD GE Conference. 
The conference aims to consolidate UPD's stand on the conceptual and operational design of the GE Program to be implemented alongside curricular changes in the respective degree programs of the different academic units.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 1, Blg 5 (2 copies)
column7:
Oktubre 1
DEL Extramural Classes
4th cycle 2015
The 4th cycle of the Department of European Languages (DEL) Extramural Classes will run from October to December 2015. Classes will be held on weekdays (2 hrs./session) and Saturdays (3 hrs./session).
Oktubre 2 
Sampling Design for Survey of Enterprises in the Philippines
School of Statistics Auditorium 
4 p.m.
The colloquium presents the paper of Dr. Erniel Barrios on business surveys. 
Business surveys, according to Barrios's study, are very challenging for two reasons : the organizational structures of businesses are too complicated, thus it is often difficult to locate where the data is; and the distribution of some target variables like revenue is not normal but rather severely skewed. 
Oktubre 2 
Pag-uugat at Pagsasanga : Antropolohiya Katambal ang Iba't ibang Larangang Akademiko
Pulungang Claro M. Recto 
Bulwagang Rizal (Faculty Center) 
1-5 p.m.
Sa ikalawang handog na colloquium ng Departamento ng Antropolohiya, tampok ang mga piling gurong tagapagsalita, nilalayong baybayin ang ugnayan at kasaysayan ng disiplina ng Antropolohiya sa iba't ibang larangang akademiko at gawain, partikular sa Agham, Sosyolohiya at sa paglapat ng mga aplikasyon nito. 
Oktubre 6
#chooseright : Empowering Voters for Elections 2016 A public forum on voter's education
NCPAG Assembly Hall
1:30 - 4 p.m.
The forum is the first activity of the National College of Public Administration and Governance's (NCPAG) Center for Leadership, Citizenship and Democracy (NCPAG-CLCD) Election Series Project. NCPAG Dean Maria Fe Mendoza, Institute of Political and Electoral Reform Executive Director Ramon Casiple, and UP Political Science Prof. Grace Gorospe-Jamon of the UP Political Science Department will serve as resource persons. 
The forum aims to contribute in facilitating discourse and analyses on national issues affecting the 2016 elections, provide a platform for discussion and dissemination of analyses to a wider audience, and help build awareness and capacity to make informed choices among the electorate especially the first time voters and the youth. 
Oktubre 7
Democratization Advocates in Vietnam, 1990s-2014 
A public lecture by Dr. Benedict J. Tria Kervliet
Seminar Room, Hall of Wisdom, Asian Center 1:30-4 p.m.
Dr. Benedict J. Tria Kervliet will discuss how, "beginning in the mid-1990s, public criticism of the Communist Party government in Vietnam spread to the point that by 2014 it became a prominent feature of the country's political scene. He is Professor Emeritus at the Department of Political and Social Change, School of International, Political and Strategic Studies, Australian National University. 
Oktubre 7-25
King Lear / Haring Lear
Wilfrido Ma. Guerrero Theater
2/F Palma Hall
Weekday shows at 7 p.m. 
Weekend shows at 10 a.m. and 3 p.m. 
The play is set in the waning years of a Southeast Asian kingdom replete with appropriate music, movement, costumes and properties of the region without losing the themes, images and poetry of the playwright's supreme achievement. King Lear / Haring Lear is the epitome of how deceit and the love for the superficial can tragically bring families to ruin and great kingdoms to ashes. 
Oktubre 8
Indirect Fertility Estimation in light of International Labor Migration : Differentials across Selected Variables 
School of Statistics Auditorium
4 p.m.
The colloquium presents the study of Michael Dominique del Mundo on the relationship of Migration and Fertility in the Philippines by focusing on the effect of spousal separation due to international labor migration on fertility. 
Oktubre 8
Josefino Chino Toledo's Misa Lingua Sama-Sama
Abelardo Hall Auditorium 
College of Music
7 p.m.
First complete performance of Toledo's Misa Lingua Sama-Sama, a concert mass in Latin and Filipino featuring the UP Dance Company, UP Madrigal Singers, Aleron, UP Los Baños Choral Ensemble Technological Institute of the Philippines Choral Society, St. Scholastica's College Chamber Choir / Ateneo Glee Club Alumni, UP Manila Chorale, AUIT Chamber Vocal Ensemble. 
Oktubre 8-9
Future Perfect : Cities at the Forefront of Change and Development 
Eastwood Richmonde Hotel, Quezon City 
8 a.m. - 6 p.m. 
The international conference showcases innovative approaches and reforms to city administration. It is organized by the UP National College of Public Administration and Governance in partnership with the Quezon City Government. 
Oktubre 16
The Link between Expenditure on Contraceptives and Number of Young Dependents in the Philippines 
School of Statistics Auditorium
4 p.m.
Prof. Michael Daniel Lucagbo used the Family Income and Expenditure Survey (FIES) 2009 data to examine the factors affecting the number of young dependents in a household, and focuses in particular on the household's level of contraceptive expenditure. 
Oktubre 16
Music Culture Workshop with Dr. David Urrows
College of Music
3-5 p.m.
This event is part of the music colloquium series of the College of Music. The target audiences of this music culture workshop are doctoral students. Prior to the workshop, attendees will read two to three of the visiting scholar's most recently published research, and one or two discussants from the College may formally respond to them before the open forum. 
Oktubre 17-18, 24-25 at 27-29
UAAP Table Tennis Event 
Season 78
Ninoy Aquino Stadium, Rizal Memorial Sports Complex, Malate, Manila
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The University Athletic Association of the Philippines (UAAP) Table Tennis Competition will be participated by the eight UAAP member universities. Both the UP men and women's team will try to regain the championships this season. The UP women's team was the UAAP Season 76 champions. 
Oktubre 19
Grants, Gigs and Gimmicks 2015
Aldaba Recital Hall
University Theater Complex 
2-4 p.m. and 4-6 p.m. 
The UP Diliman Office for Initiatives in Culture and the Arts (UPD-OICA) invites registered UP Diliman student organizations (college-based or university-wide) to the third edition of the annual Grants, Gigs and Gimmicks 2015, a free orientation and workshop regarding the Financial Grants Program. 
Oktubre 19-23
The 36th Asian Conference on Remote Sensing (ACRS 2015)
Crowne Plaza Galleria, Quezon City
The Asian Conference on Remote Sensing (ACRS) is an annual international scientific conference in Asian cities with voluntary participation of the host country and researchers, educators, professionals and students from all over the world. This is the second time that the Philippines was chosen to host the ACRS, the first time was in 1988. 
Oktubre 20-23
Enhancing the Supervisor's Capability in Mentoring and Coaching 
Seminar Room
College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD)
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The seminar-workshop is an introductory skill-building course designed for program/project managers, senior community development workers, social workers and other social development practitioners with supervisory responsibilities. The content focuses on the concepts, principles and processes of supervision with special focus on mentoring and coaching functions utilizing appropriate methods and tools. 
Oktubre 22
UP SURP's 50th Anniversary Celebration and General Alumni Homecoming
Ang Bahay ng Alumni 
5:30 p.m. 
In celebration of the 50th year of the UP School of Urban and Regional Planning (UP SURP), a series of activities will be held such as parade around the campus and groundbreaking of the new SURP building. This will be capped by the 50th anniversary celebration and general alumni homecoming at the Ang Bahay ng Alumni. 
Oktubre 22-24
National Conference on Research in Teacher Education 
Benitez Hall (College of Education)
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The National Conference on Research in Teacher Education (NCRTE) is conducted biennially by the UP College of Education since 1997. It has served as a venue for discussions on educational issues based on research findings. 
Oktubre 23 
Constructing Leading Economic Indicators for the Philippine Economy Using Dynamic Factor Models
School of Statistics Auditorium 
4 p.m. 
The study presents an alternative, and perhaps better, procedure to the Leading Economic Indicators System (LEIS) in nowcasting the movements of the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) using the Dynamic Factor Models (DFM). This paper used 30 monthly economic indicators in capturing a common factor to nowcast movements of GDP via the DFM. 
Oktubre 30
Call for submission for UP Press Critical Essay Writing Contest 
Oktubre 30 
Demographic Sweet Spot and Dividend in the Philippines : The Window of Opportunity is Closing Fast
School of Statistics Auditorium 
4 p.m. 
Dr. Dennis S. Mapa's paper looks at the population structure of the country from 2010 to 2100, using actual census data from the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) and projections on future population from the United Nations (UN), to estimate the period when the country will experience the demographic window of opportunity. 
Nobyembre 9
Likha-Likas : Reconfiguring Music, Nature and Myth : A Symphonic Band Concert
Abelardo Hall Auditorium 
College of Music 
8 p.m. 
This is the UP leg of the weeklong 33rd Asian Composers League (ACL) Internationalo Festival and Conference. 
The concert features the UP Symphonic Band under the baton of Prof. Rodney Ambat. The UP Symphonic Band will play new compositions of ACL member countries. 
Nobyembre 10 
(Re) Present! A Public Presentatio of Communication Research Student Papers 
College of Mass Communication
9 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
This whole-day event organized by the Department of Communication Research of the UPD College of Mass Communication showcases the research works of students and recent alumni of the Department. The morning session presents the research output of the Data Interpretation and Reporting class, while the afternoon session features student research papers that have been presented in international conferences. Topics covered by the student papers include health and wellness, leisure, film, politics, poverty, disaster, diaspora and the Filipino family.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 1, Blg 6 (2 copies)
column7:
Disyembre 1
"Paano Lumakas ang Loob ng Mahiyaing Manok"
Hall of Wisdom
GT-Toyota Asian Cultural Center
2:30 - 4 p.m. 
A public forum about "Ang Mahiyaing Manok," a children's book that bagged the Encouragement Prize at the 2000 Noma Concours for Children's Picture Book Illustrations in Tokyo, Japan. The forum is free and open to the public. 
Disyembre 7
Piano Music from Romantic Manila 
Abelardo Hall Auditorium 
6:30 p.m.
An evening of Filipino piano music from the 1860s to 1960s, to be performed by Israeli-American pianist Sally Pinkas, professor of music and pianist-in-residence at the Hopkins Center of Dartmouth College, Hanover, New Hampshire. 
Disyembre 10
KRIZZMAZZ JAZZ at the Carillon
The Carillon Plaza
6 p.m. 
The UP Alumni Association inaugurates the Carillon Concerts Series at the Carillon Plaza with a concert by The UP Jazz Band, with conductor Prof. Raymundo Maigue and artistic director Dr. Ramon Acoymo. 
Disyembre 11
Tanglaw 2015 : Isang Pamaskong Konsyerto
UP Theater
3 p.m. and 7 p.m. 
Ang konsyerto ay bahagi ng pagdiriwang ng ika-80 taong anibersaryo ng UP Filipiniana Dance Group (UPFDG). Lalahukan ito ng mga kasalukuyan at dating direktor at miyembro ng UPFDG. 
Disyembre 14
Parada ng mga Parol 2015
Academic Oval
5 p.m.
Ito ang paligsahan ng pinakamagagandang parol ng mga akademiko at di-akademikong yunit ayon sa temang "Dingas : Adhikaing Diliman, Adhikaing Bayan." Ipaparada ang mga parol paikot sa Academic Oval at magkakaroon ng palatuntunan sa Amphitheater. Ito ay tatampukan ng fireworks display. 
Disyembre 14
Maskipaps : The Crossover
Sunken Garden
9 p.m.
Isang pangkomunidad na pagdiriwang ng UP sa pangunguna ng Beta Epsilon na dadaluhan din ng mga mag-aaral mula sa iba't-ibang pamantasan. 
Enero 8-9
For the Love of Death : Human Osteoarchaeology in Southeast Asia and the Pacific 
Archaeological Studies Program 
The 2-day international conference aims to create an opportunity for researchers to present and discuss their latest work on archaeological human remains assemblages from across Southeast Asia and the Pacific. 
Enero 8-9
Wagas na Pag-aaruga : The Yaya Training Workshop
Audio Visual Room
College of Social Work and Community Development 
8:30 a.m. - 4 p.m. 
This 2-day event aims to provie the Yayas the basics of child development that will enable them to facilitate appropriate activities as they interact with the children. Specifically, it focuses on how to : keep children stimulated through play; help children become more independent ; and deal with challenging behaviors of children. 
Enero 26-29
Enhancing the Supervisor's Capability in Mentoring and Coaching
Bulwagang Tandang Sora
College of Social Work and Community Development 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
This seminar-workshops is an introductory skill-building course designed for program/project managers, senior community development workers, social workers and other social development practitioners with supervisory responsibilities. The content focuses on the concepts, principles and processes of supervision with special focus on mentoring and coaching functions utilizing appropriate methods and tools. 
Enero 28
Hommage a' l'enfant : Isang Pagtatanghal sa Pista ng Sto. Niño
Abelardo Hall Auditorium, College of Music 6-7:30 p.m.
The third week of January is significant to a number of communities because of the feast of the Holy Child. This event is commemorated at the University through an offering of music for and inspired by children.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 1, Blg 1 (3 copies) UP Diliman Month 2016
column7:
Pebrero 1
Bodabil sa Kampus : Seremonya ng Pagbubukas ng Buwan ng UP Diliman
University Amphitheater
6 p.m.
Halaw mula sa bodabil, isang engrandeng programa ang isasaentablado sa may ampiteatro ng UP Diliman. Layunin ng seremonya ang ipagbunyi ang mga artista ng bayan hindi lamang bilang manlilikha ng obra maestra kundi bilang mga iskolar ng sining. 
Pebrero 1-6
Pasalubong Festival (Pasafest) 2016
Palma Hall and Kalayaan Residence Hall
An annual event celebrated in the Kalayaan Residence Hall, Pasafest showcases the dormers respective cultural identity through cuisines, cultural performances and innovative booths. 
Pebrero 2-23
Usapang Pambansang Alagad ng Sining
Bulwagang Recto
2:30 p.m.
Ito ay serye ng lektura na katatampukan ng mga tala ukol sa buhay nila Lamberto Avellana, Manuel Conde, NVM Gonzalez at Severino Montano. Tatalakayin din sa mga lektura ang mga kabuluhan ng kanilang mga gawa sa kasalukuyang lipunang Pilipino.
Pebrero 2-5
BIYABIT : Isang Pagtatampok ng Lahing Ala-eh sa Diliman
AS Lobby and Tambayan Complex, Vinzons Hall
A 2-part event which celebrates the culture and traditions of the province of Batangas : Tan-aw and Mual. 
Tan-aw, a Batangueño word for glimpse, is a weeklong exhibit on the essence of the province where the largest balisong in the country, local handicrafts and some major products will be showcased. 
Pebrero 3
Conde sa Dap-ay : Señorito
College of Mass Communication Dap-ay
7 p.m. 
"Señorito," isang pelikula ni Manuel Conde. 
Ito ay tungkol sa isang ama na umupa ng isang babae upang magpanggap bilang isang mayamang tao at gamitin ang kanyang karisma para baguhin ang maling pamumuhay ng kanyang lalaking anak. Habang nagtatagumpay ang plano ng ama, ang kanyang anak at ang inupahang babae ay tunay na nagka-ibigan, bagay na taliwas sa kanilang naging kasunduan. 
Pebrero 4
Conde sa Dap-ay : Genghis Khan
College of Mass Communication Dap-ay 
7 p.m.
"Genghis Khan," isang pelikula ni Manuel Conde.
Ito ay nagsasalaysay kung paanong gumawa si Temujin ng isang hukbo at napagtagumpayan ang pagkamit ng maraming lupain upang hirangin o bansagan na Genghis Khan, ibig sabihin ay "haring walang katumbas." Sa kabila ng kanyang tagumpay, naging alipin siya ng kanyang pag-ibig sa anak na babae ng kanyang kalaban.
Pebrero 5
Pagpupugay : A Tribute to the National Artists
Pulungang C.M. Recto, Bulwagang Rizal 
5 p.m.
UP Press, in partnership with the UPD College of Arts and Letters, re-launche the works of 13 National Artists of the Philippines. 
Pebrero 6-20
Basic Drawing and Painting Workshop 2016
(3-sessions, every Saturday)
College of Fine Arts
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
Pebrero 7
UPIS Centennial Fun Run : Bagong Siglo, Bagong Sigla : UPIS Takbo Pa!
Academic Oval
4:10 a.m. 
The fun run is a fund-raising activity of the UP Integrated School Parent-Teacher Association for the construction of the UPIS gymnasium. Participants get a chance to win an iPad Mini. 
The activity is also in line with the centennial celebration of UPIS. 
Pebrero 7-21
Himigsikan
Carillon Plaza
5 p.m.
Ang Himigsikan ay ang taunang serye ng mga konsyertong handog ng UP Diliman sa komunidad sa bawat araw ng Linggo tuwing Pebrero. Sa taong ito, itatampok ang musika ng mga grupong hinubog o nakilala sa UPD. 
Pebrero 10
Avellana sa Arki : Badjao
College of Architecture Amphitheater
7 p.m. 
"Badjao," isang pelikula ni Lamberto Avellana.
Kinikilala bilang "pinakaunang mahusay na pelikula na naglarawan ng tradisyunal na kaugalian ng isang grupong minorya," tinalakay ng pelikulang "Badjao" kung paanong ang dalawang grupong naglalaban, ang mga Badjao at ang mga Moro, ay nakamit ang isang mapayapang kasunduan sa pamamagitan ng karanasan ng bagong kasal na pinuno ng mga Badjao at ng isang Moro na pinaglaban ang kanilang relasyon sa kabila ng mga pagtutol ng parehong grupo. 
Pebrero 10-28
Tisoy Brown : Hari ng Wala
Wilfrido Ma. Guerrero Theater
2/F Palma Hall
Weekday shows at 7 p.m.
Weekend shows at 10 a.m. and 3 p.m. 
Tisoy Brown : Hari ng Wala is Dulaang UP's third offering for its 40th Theater Season. Jose Estrella directs the play. 
Pebrero 11
Tugs of War : Local Governments, National Government
PCED Auditorium, School of Economics 
3 p.m.
The Ayala-UP School of Economics Forum on Tugs of War : Local Governments, National Government offers an economic perspective on the jurisdictional or functional conflicts among local governments or between local governments and national government under decentralization. The causes, consequences and conclusions of these conflicts will also be discussed. 
Pebrero 11
Avellana sa Arki : A Portrait of the Artist as Filipino
College of Architecture Amphitheater 
7 p.m.
"A Portrait of the Artist as Filipino," isang pelikula ni Lamberto Avellana.
Ito ay base sa akda ni Nick Joaquin na itinuturing na "pinakamahalagang dulang Pilipino sa English." Si Joaquin ay isang Pambansang Alagad ng Sining para sa Literatura. 
Pebrero 13
Elevate 2016 : Utak at Puso
University Theater 
3 and 7 p.m.
A dance concert of the UP Varsity Pep Squad, the project aims to present the different dance genres that the UP Pep Squad can do as well as to showcase the talents and creativity of the dancers and drummers of the squad. The concert also promotes school spirit. 
Pebrero 15
TUKLAS : A Look into Landscape Architecture
NISMED Auditorium 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The seminar/workshop aims to give students more ideas on what landscape architecture is all about and how the profession can contribute in a community in terms of aesthetic qualities and how it introduces solutions to sustainable environment development through design. 
Pebrero 15
ASEAN Youth Cultural Forum
GT Toyota Auditorium 
9 a.m. - 4 p.m.
With a theme "Locating the Filipino : Advancing Philippine Culture in the ASEAN," the forum seeks to address the current issue of the ASEAN integration and its implications on culture and the arts among UPD students, artists and cultural workers. 
Pebrero 16-17
Montano sa UP Theater Stage : The Ladies and the Senator
University Theater Stage
3 and 7 p.m.
Isang komedya ni Severino Montano tungkol sa pagbisita ni Anthony Maripal, isang Pilipinong senador na nasa Estados Unidos. Pinaghahandaan ng senador ang isang 'welcome party' ng mga opisyal ng Filipino Women's Club ng Washington D.C. Ngunit bago pa man dumating ang senador, pinag-uusapan na ng kababaihan kung papaano pa nito nagagawang makapag-relaks samantalang sa Pilipinas ay may nakabinbin na kasong 'plunder' laban dito. 
Pebrero 16-19
Carovana 2016
UP Arki Buildings 1 and 2 and Amphitheater
The weeklong arts and music festival that include exhibit of student artworks, daily performances of ARKAIRA members and applicants, and a culminating activity with special performances of local bands. 
Pebrero 19
Bawat Bata, Artista : Konsyerto ng UP Cherubim and Seraphim
University Theater Stage
3 and 7 p.m.
The concert aims to showcase composition and arrangement of Philippine classics by well-known composers as well as works from the contemporary and popular repertoire. The concert will also feature works of National Artists for Music Lucio San Pedro and Ramon P. Santos.
Pebrero 20
Speech Cup 2016 PAMANA : Boses ng Pinalaya, Boses na Magpapalaya
UP Film Institute 
1-5 p.m. 
Speech Cup is an annual speech competition open to high school and college students nationwide. It serves as a venue for students to express themselves and their beliefs and principles through choral competition and oral interpretation. 
Pebrero 20
Haranafest
Abelardo Hall Auditorium, College of Music
3 p.m.
Haranafest is an inter-college choral competition for students organized by the UPD Office of the Vice Chancellor for Student Affairs (OVCSA) and the University Student Council.
The theme of the inaugural Haranefest is "Awit sa Pelikula." The official contest piece is "Anak Dalita," theme song of the movie of the same title directed by National Artist for Film Lamberto Avellana. The choice of songs of the participating college choirs will be drawn from the theme songs of Filipino movies from the 1940s to the present. 
Pebrero 22
Pagpapasinaya ng Selyo ni N.V.M. Gonzalez
University Theater, Lobby
2 p.m.
Kasama ang National Commission for Culture and the Arts, pormal na pasisinayan ang espesyal na selyo tampok si NVM Gonzalez, Pambansang Alagad ng Sining Para sa Panitikan. 
Pebrero 22
Wanderer in the Night of the World : A Dance Concert featuring the UP Dance Company
University Theater Stage
4 and 7:30 p.m.
Ito ay isang konsyerto tampok ang UP Dance Company upang bigyang buhay ang mga tula ni NVM Gonzalez sa pamamagitan ng galaw at sayaw.  
Ang konsyerto ay sa direksiyon ni Angela Lawenko-Baguilat.
Pebrero 24
N.V.M. Gonzalez's The Bread of Salt
Bulwagan ng Dangal
3 and 7 p.m.
Handog ng Teatrong Mulat ng Pilipinas ang isang palabas na papet na "The Bread of Salt."
Sa direksiyon ni Amihan Bonifacio-Ramolete, dekana ng Kolehiyo ng Arte at Literatura, at mga disenyong papet ni Sig Pecho, layunin ng palabas na mas mapalapit sa mga batang manonood ang yaman ng wika at kultura ng Pilipinas na ipinamalas ni NVM Gonzalez sa kanyang akda. Isa rin itong pagkakataong makilalang maigi ng kabataan ang mahalagang teksto sa kasaysayan ng panitikan ng Pilipinas. 
Pebrero 26
Seremonya ng Pagwawakas : Panahon ng Luwalhati
University Theater
7 p.m. 
Isang simple ngunit makabuluhang konsyertong katatampukan ng sayaw, poesiya at awitin ng mga kontemporaneong artista at obra maestra na sumisimbolo sa pangunguna ng UP sa pagpapatuloy ng mayaman at malalim na sining ng bayan.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 2, Blg 3 (4 copies) UP Diliman 2016
column7:
Abril 3
The CentEEEnnial Run
UP Academic Oval
5 a.m.
The CentEEEnnial Run is a fund-raising activity of the UP Electrical and Electronics Engineering Alumni Association, Inc. The activity is also in line with the centennial celebration of the UP EEEI.
Abril 4-15
Teaching Preschoolers and Early Graders in the Context of the K to 12 Curriculum 
UP College of Education
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
A 10-day training workshop intended for administrators and professors who are handling courses in preschool and the early grades and teacher-practitioners of preschool and primary classes. 
Abril 6
The Dressing Room/Ang Dressing Room That Which Flows Away Ultimately Becomes Nostalgia/Ang Dressing Room Kung Saan Lubusang Pangungulila ang Dulot ng Panahon
Wilfrido Ma. Guerrero Theater
2/F Palma Hall
Weekday shows at 7 p.m.
Weekend shows at 10 a.m. and 3 p.m.
The Dressing Room/Ang Dressing Room is a tender and humorous drama about actors, the theatre, aging, surviving and moving on. Dr. Alexander Cortez directs the play. 
Abril 8
The Philippines and Thailand : A Reversal of Fortune? A Public Forum on Comparative Politics and Economic Development
Seminar Room, GT-Toyota Asian Cultural Center, Asian Center
2:30-4 p.m.
Using political economy, state-society and cultural lenses, the public forum examines how political factors link up to economic development. It also interrogates the narratives of economic growth and decline from different perspectives. 
Abril 8-13 at 15
Gawad Pangulo art and design competitions Awarding ceremony and exhibition of works
Gallery and Open Court 
UP College of Fine Arts (CFA) 
6 p.m.
The Gawad Pangulo held UP System-wide art competitions this February : the On-Your-Spot art competition, the Products UPGrade design competition and the Multimedia Competition. 
Abril 10-17 at 24; Mayo 8, 15 at 22
Basic or Advance Drawing and Painting Workshop
UP College of Fine Arts
8 a.m.- 5 p.m. 
Facilitated by Prof. Yasmin Almonte, the 3-day workshop offers participants the chance to hone their skills in basic drawing and their choice of painting formats (portrait, still life, landscape, figure drawing, or abstract art).
Abril 11-Hunyo 11
UP Ceramic Studio Pottery and Ceramic Sculpture Workshops
UP College of Fine Arts
The UP Ceramic Studio Extension Program offers 7-session workshops in ceramic arts to participants of all ages. 
Abril 11-15 
Tambulan sa Diliman XI
UP College of Arts and Letters Grounds
The weeklong event serves as a venue for indigeneous peoples to share to the public their everyday realities and to shed light on pressing issues, such as on ancestral domains and cultural preservation, among others. Through creative workshops, cultural performances and a film showing activity, Kontra-GaPi hopes to surface the challenges that majority of these local communities experienced or are still experiencing and to inspire the general public to proactively respect and protect the rights of indigenous peoples. 
Abril 16
3K UP: Takbo Para sa Kabataan, Kalikasan, Kinabukasan
UP Academic Oval
6 a.m.
A run for a cause organized by the College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD) administrative staff and faculty, together with Tatak CSWCD scholars, the UP CSWCD Student Council, UP CD Circle and JSWAP. 
Abril 16-Hunyo 18
Department of European Languages Extramural Languages Extramural Courses 2nd cycle 2016
College of Arts and Letters Building
The 2nd cycle 2016 of the Department of European Languages (DEL) Extramural Courses will run from 16 April to 18 June 2016. Classes will be held on weekdays (2 hrs./session) and Saturdays (3 hrs./session) 
Abril 18-Mayo 20
Summer Fun Classes 2016
Summer Fun is the annual summer program offered by the Teaching in the Early Grades Area of the College of Education. 
Abril 18-Mayo 14
Continuing Education for Music Teachers (CEMT)
UP College of Music
8:30 a.m.-5 p.m.
The training is a post-baccalaureate summer certificate program (3 levels) offered by the UP College of Music Extension Program designed to address the need for music teachers to enhance their musical growth and teaching competencies. 
Abril 18
The Role of UP EEEI in the Philippine Innovation Ecosystem
PLDT MLH (VLC), EEEI Bldg. I 
2:00 PM
The year-long lecture series aims to highlight the accomplishments of the EEE alumni, especially those who have been successful in their career in the Philippines. In addition, the lecture aims to instill the idea to undergraduate and graduate students that one does not need to find work opportunities overseas to have a successful career. 
Abril 19-30
Index 2016 : An Industrial Design Portfolio Exhibit
Corredor and Foyer
UP College of Fine Arts (CFA) 
5 p.m. 
This annual exhibition showcases portfolio works by senior Industrial Design students from the D197 (Special Topics in Industrial Design) class of Dante Cruz. 
Abril 20
Balik-tanaw sa mga Ambag ni Conkin sa Antropolohiya
H. Otley Beyer Museum, Gusaling Palma 
2-5 p.m.
Bilang pagpupugay sa naging buhay ni Harold Conklin, ang Departamento ng Antropolohiya ay magkakaroon ng isang porum na magbabalik tanaw sa kanyang mga naging ambag sa Antropolohiya sa Pilipinas. 
Abril 20
Pelikula, Kasaysayan, at Lipunan : Pagmamapa at Kritika sa Tunguhin ng Sineng Filipino 
Panayam nina Prof. Nick Deocampo, Prof. Patrick Campos, at Choy Pangilinan
College of Mass Communication Auditorium 
1-4 p.m. 
Layon ng panayam na talakayin at suriin ang kasalukuyang lagay ng sineng Filipino na may pagbibigay diin sa hindi maitatatwang hugpungan ng pelikula at lipunan. Sasalingin ng tatlong papel ng mga tagapagsalita ang mga usapin ukol sa pelikula, representasyon at bayani, kasaysayan ng pelikulang alternatibo, at dokumentaryo, kolektibong alaala at sineng politikal.  
The 2016 UP CMC Graduate Student Conference on Communication and Media Studies
Plaridel Hall
8:30 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The conference serves as a venue for graduate students who are interested in communication and media, to learn from each other through the presentation and discussion of their research papers. It also provides a space for students to share their experiences in graduate school and learn how to publish their papers. 
Abril 25
Balangkas : a Symposium on Intensifying International Engagement
3/F Melchor Hall
9 a.m. - 5:30 p.m.
The Symposium on Internalization is intended to continue recognizing institutions, as well as individuals, who have been instrumental in moving forward the programs and activities that are in line with UPD's pursuit of international engagement. 
Abril 25-26
UPD International Fair
2/F Lobby, Melchor Hall
9 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The UPD International Fair is intended to continue recognizing institutions, as well as individuals, who have been instrumental in moving forward the programs and activities that are in line with UPD's pursuit of international engagement. 
Abril 26
Digital Vigilantes : Online Shaming and Surveillance Culture in Top Gear Philippines' Facebook Page
College of Mass Communication Auditorium 
1-2:30 p.m. 
The paper argues that the Top Gear Philippines' Facebook page has transformed into a radically new site of "crowdsourced" forms of social norms and punishment for civic deviance and transgressions, and introduce coming greater capacities for negotiation, resistance, power and control in the digitally mediated surveillance society. 
Abril 26
Digital Vigilantes : Online Shaming and Surveillance Culture in Top Gear Philippines' Facebook Page
College of Mass Communication Auditorium 
1-2:30 p.m. 
The paper argues that the Top Gear Philippines' Facebook page has transformed into a radically new site of "crowdsourced" forms of social norms and punishment for civic deviance and transgressions, and introduce coming greater capacities for negotiation, resistance, power and control in the digitally mediated surveillance society. 
Abril 26
Family picture : a study on the influence of news media on the voting public's perceived image of political dynasties in the province of Nueva Ecija. 
College of Mass Communication Auditorium 
3 - 4:30 p.m.
The paper aims to discover how the news media contribute to the voting public's formation of their perceived image regarding political dynasties in Nueva Ecija. 
Mayo 14-Hulyo 16
Asian Languages Extramural Classes (Cycle 2)
UP Department of Linguistics 
8 a.m.- 5 p.m. 
In line with the UP Department of Linguistics Extension Agenda, the Department undertakes extension activities with its goal of integrating linguistic knowledge into language pedagogy that focuses on the teaching and promotion of Asian Languages. 
Mayo 16-20 
Linguistics for Language Teachers 
UP Department of Linguistics 
9 a.m.-12 noon; 1-4 p.m. 
A one-week seminar based on a continuing linguistic research into the differences between unrelated languages (e.g., English and Filipino), and between language varieties (e.g., Philippine English and International English). It serves to make the participants appreciate that learning of a second language is, first and foremost, exposure to exemplars of that language. 
Mayo 22-29
55th UP National Writers Workshop
UP Los Baños
Twelve writers from different genres of poetry, fiction, creative non-fiction and drama present their works in progress, and talk about their craft, in this year's UP National Writers Workshop (UP NWW). The UP NWW is for advanced writers only. The fellows are accepted not only on the basis for their publications and awards but also for the potential of their current literary projects. 
Mayo 26-28
SEALS26
The Annual Meeting of the Southeast Asian Linguistics Society
Century Park Hotel, Manila 
As a primary venue for exchange of linguistic ideas, the Southeast Asian Linguistics Society (SEALS) conference focuses on the language and linguistics of Southeast Asia, including the Austroasiatic, Austronesian, Hmong-Mien, Tibeto-Burman and Tai-Kadai families, and provides a venue for scholars to share their research. The SEALS conference regularly circumnavigates the globe and has met not only in the United States, but also Southeast Asia, Australia and Europe. 
Mayo 31
Call for Session Proposals for the SIMSEA Regional Symposium 2016
The SIMSEA Regional Symposium 2016 will focus on sub-themes and areas of research to meet the goals of sustainable development such as ocean health, productivity of marine ecosystems and sustainable livelihood; climate change and variability; weather extremes; disaster management and adaptation in the coastal zones; socio ecological dynamics of sustainable use of oceans, seas and coastal resources; coastal pollution, degradation and biodiversity loss; ocean policy and trans-boundary flows.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 2, Blg 4 (7 copies) UP Diliman 2016
column7:
Hunyo 1 - Agosto 15
Gawad Rogelio Sicat 2016 : Timpalak sa Pagsulat ng Tula, Maikling Kuwento at Sanaysay
UP Departmento ng Filipino at Panitikan ng Pilipinas
Ang Gawad Rogelio Sicat 2016 ay naglalayon na himukin ang mga mag-aaral na magpasa ng kanilang mga malikhaing akda at bigyang-gawad ang nagsisimulang manunulat na nagpapakita ng kahusayan sa pagsulat sa wikang Filipino. 
Hunyo 1-3
ASIALEX 2016
Advancing Language Teaching with Lexicography and Corpus-Building Century Park Hotel, Manila 
The 10th International Conference of the Asian Association for Lexicography (ASIALEX) aims to present the current trends and practices in lexicography and corpus-building, promote the use of lexicography and corpora in language learning and English Language Teaching (ELT), and to bring together lexicographers, linguists and language educators from different parts of Asia and the rest of the world. 
Hunyo 6 - Hulyo 5
2016 Ramadhan Program
UP Institute of Islamic Studies (UPII) 
3:30-7:30 p.m.
The celebration of the holy month of Ramadhan is an annual program of the IIS that commenced in 2014. Every day from dawn to dusk during the month, more or less 1.6 billion Muslims around the world spend complete fast abstaining from food, drink and sex. Other malevolent acts are also prohibited during this month in order to avoid the inadvertent nullification of fasting. 
Hunyo 7
Inclusiveness and Competitiveness : the Challenge for the Philippines
Room Ruby B, Crowne Plaza Manila Galleria, Ortigas Center, QC
9 a.m. - 4 p.m.
The forum features the result of a 5-year project of an international research consortium working on a research programme about the phenomenon of the "middle-income country trap." The heart of the research deals with factors that drive the success of firms who are able to survive, thrive in and withstands the competitive pressures of globalization despite the handicap of operating in high-cost environments. 
Hunyo 9
CHE Inauguration and Groundbreaking 
Inauguration of the newly renovated CHE Gusali 2 and Ground Breaking of CHE Museum and the Department of Family Life and Child Development Center
Alonso Hall, College of Home Economics 
8 a.m. - 12 noon
The UP College of Home Economics (CHE) will inaugurate the newly renovated Gusali 2 Building that houses five classrooms that could be turned into a multi-purpose hall. There will also be ground breaking of two new facilities, the CHE Museum and the Department of Family Life and Child Development with the Child Development Center. The newly designed and created CHE website will be launched on the same day. 
Hunyo 14 - Disyembre
(tuwing Martes at Huwebes)
Integrated Japanese 1
Asian Languages Extramural Classes
UP Department of Linguistics
5:30 - 7:30 p.m. 
The Integrated Japanese 1 (IJI) module is composed of the first 3 modules of the Japanese language series (1,2 and 3) offered in the UP Department of Linguistics Asian Languages Extramural Classes. 
Hunyo 21
Rizal as a natural scientist
Room 104 Old IB Zoology Building 
3 p.m. 
In celebration of the UP Natural Sciences and Research Institute's (NSRI) 52nd foundation day, UP's 108th foundation day and of the national hero's 155th birth anniversary, NSRI presents a lecture about Dr. Jose Rizal. 
Hunyo 23
PARA (!) SA LAHAT : Dahil sa Pag-arangkada para sa Karapatan, Walang Dapat Naiiwan
Paglulunsad ng PRIDE Sticker Campaign sa mga Pampasaherong Dyip sa UP
Quezon Hall Lobby
8:30-9:30 a.m.
Bilang paggunita sa makasaysayang Stonewall Riots noong Hunyo 28, 1969 na naging batayan ng taunang Pride March, ilulunsad ng UP Center for Women's and Gender Studies ang PRIDE Sticker Campaign sa mga pampasaherong dyip sa UP Diliman. 
Hunyo 26
Ika-105 Pangkalahatang Pagtatapos
Ampiteatro ng Unibersidad 
7 a.m.
Ang tema sa taong ito ay "Pumailanlang."
Ang pangunahing tagapagsalita ay si Pangulong Alfredo E. Pascual.
Hulyo 20-22
PKSS-Korean Studies Workshop 2016
Microtel UP Technohub
Diliman, Quezon City
With the purpose of encouraging Korean Studies scholars in the Philippines to produce research in the field, the workshop aims to help Filipino Korean Studies graduate-level students, researchers and scholars to develop their ongoing research works into publishable manuscripts for submission to journals in the country and/or abroad. The organizers hope that these research works would deepen the Filipino academicians' awareness, understanding and knowledge of Korea and Korean Studies, and encourage them to challenge or integrate the concepts and ideas promoted by these researches as they develop their own Korean Studies curriculum and produce comparative research in their own institutions.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 2, Blg 5 (7 copies) UP Diliman 2016
column7:
Agosto 5
Global Liquidity and Financial Vulnerability
Room 303, UP School of Economics 
4 p.m. 
The study by Dr. Maria Socorro Gochoco-Bautista attempts to make a modest contribution to the literature on the relationship between global liquidity and financial vulnerability. It conducts a dynamic conditional correlation analysis to examine the relationship between global credit and risk, and regression analysis to examine how global liquidity, as seen in cross-border claims to the Asia-Pacific region or capital flows to the Philippines as a share of GDP interacts with the global factor, VIX, and affects stock market returns, bank leverage and the growth of domestic credit to the private sector in the Philippines. 
Agosto 8 - Setyembre 2 (Lunes-Biyernes)
"The Journey of Mankind : First Humans Out of Africa-The Hominids of Dmanisi, Georgia"
Albert Hall Lobby 
9 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The exhibit is about a human evolution that took place 1.77 million years ago in Dmanisi, Georgia. The Dmanisi hominids are believed to be the first humans to have successfully left their native land and their story is one of adaptation, subsistence, survival and even compassion. 
Agosto 11
Evaluating Fun and Enjoyment in Language Learning : New Trend in ELT Research
Benitez Theatre
College of Education
10-11:30 a.m. 
Thomas Lloyd, British Council Deputy Director for English for Education Systems East Asia, will be delivering the talk about fun and enjoyment in language teaching. 
Agosto 11 - Setyembre 15
Dancing The Shrimp
(The Tactical Improvisation of Post Colonial Space Mix) 
1F Galleries, Vargas Museum
4 p.m. 
The exhibition features Jon Cuyson's new works of fragmented installations that combine painting, sculpture, sound, photography, performance and scenography. Cuyson transforms the entire ground floor of the Vargas Museum into a space in flux, slipping between abstraction, figuration, ready made, still life and movement. 
Agosto 12 - Setyembre 3
Left Wing - Right Wing : An exhibition of works by UP College of Fine Arts faculty
Corredor and Foyer, Barlett Hall 
5 p.m. (Exhibit Opening)
The UP College of Fine Arts (UPCFA) holds its annual exhibit entitled "Left Wing-Right Wing : An Exhibition of Works by the UPCFA Faculty." The title of the exhibit is a play on words alluding to the architectural location of the College's different departments as well as the wide spectrum and overlapping of diverse disciplines, fields and affinities within the College itself. 
Agosto 13-Oktubre 3
(Tuwing Sabado at Lunes)
UP Ceramic Studio Pottery and Ceramic Sculpture Workshops
UP College of Fine Arts
The UP Ceramic Studio Extension Program offers 7-session workshops in ceramic arts to participants of all ages. 
Agosto 22
Pingkian : Student Colloquium on Public Service Initiatives
College of Engineering Theater
8 a.m.- 5 p.m. 
A conference of UPD student organizations that conduct public service progams in partnership with the different sectors of society such as people's organizations, non-government organizations, and basic and higher education institutions. 
Its objectives are to generate a general profile/typology of the public service work done by student organizations and to disseminate their best practices to the student population. The conference is open only for student organizations which have pre-registered at the UPD Office of the Vice Chancellor for Student Affairs. 
Agosto 24
UP Gawad Plaridel 2016 Awarding and Lecture
UP Film Center
2-4 p.m.
The UP Gawad Plaridel is the sole award in UP given to outstanding Filipino media practitioners who have excelled in media (print, film, radio, television and new media) and have performed with the highest level of professional integrity in the interest of public service. 
Agosto 26
Tatak CSWCD : 50 Taon ng Wagas na Paglilingkod at Pakikilahok
Bulwagang Tandang Sora, CSWCD 
3-10 p.m.
The UP College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD) is celebrating its 50th anniversary in August 2017. As a kick-off to the celebration, CSWCD will launch and announce, on August 26, the series of events and activities for the year-long golden anniversary celebration of the College. 
Setyembre 1-3
9th International Conference on Teacher Education
Theme : Teacher Agency and Educational Reform : Articulations, Conditions, Possibilities 
GT Toyota Auditorium, Asian Center 
The International Conference on Teacher Education (ICTED) is a biennial event sponsored by the UPD College of Education. This year's conference foregrounds the theme of teacher agency, defined as the exercise of individual and collective power of teachers in the midst of challenging situations. It aims to create a forum for educationists, social scientists, policymakers and students to discuss the central question : In what ways can educational reforms support or constrain teacher agency, for what purpose, under what conditions and with what consequences?
Agosto 30 - Setyembre 1
Wikang Filipino : Lunan ng Kaalamang Bayan, Lunsaran ng Makabayang Kamalayan.
Balay Kalinaw
Ang UPD Sentro ng Wikang Filipino (SWF) sa pakikipagtulungan ng Opisina ng Tsanselor at ng iba pang organisasyon sa loob at labas ng Unibersidad ay ipagdiriwang ang Buwan ng Wika 2016.
Setyembre 4
UP College of Music 
100th Anniversary Grand Alumni Homecoming 
Isang Daang Taong Himig at Tinig!
Abelardo Hall Auditorium 
1-5 p.m. (General Assembly and Cocktails),
5:30 p.m. (Anniversary Concert)
The UP College of Music (UPCM) is celebrating its centennial year on Saturday, Sept. 4, 2016. The homecoming will begin with a registration followed by a General Assembly. The event will also reminisce the past 100 years of UPCM and a concert will cap the occasion. 
Setyembre 8-30
A Visual Diary : Impressions and Reflections
First one-man exhibition of Prof. Emmanuel Garalde
Corredor and Foyer, Bartlett Hall
6 p.m.
The exhibit marks Garalde's final year in serving UP. He has taught at the UP College of Fine Arts for over 43 years, dedicating his life to honing young artists by teaching and developing core courses and photography subjects. He served as College Secretary, Chairperson of the Department of Visual Communication and was a proponent of the CFA's Industrial Design program. He retired in 2013 and continued teaching as a Professorial Lecturer since then. 
Setyembre 8
2nd Intercollegiate Voice Summit
Abelardo Hall Auditorium
7 p.m.
The most outstanding classical singers of the top music schools, conservatories and colleges in Metro Manila perform on the stage of the Abelardo Hall, in a repertoire of immortal operatic, sacred and art song ensembles. 
Setyembre 22-23
Voices and Noises : Communication and Integration within and across ASEAN Communities
2016 Communication Research International Conference
Cine Adarna, UP Film Center and UP College of Mass Communication
The 2-day international conference aims to create greater public awareness of the objectives, benefits, and challenges of the ASEAN integration and develop a regional platform for open discussion and sharing of information. 
Setyembre 23
UP Carillon Concert Series
Inspirations in Literature and Music : An evening of great poetry and song
Carillon Plaza
6:30 p.m.
The UP Alumni Association will hold the third Carillon Concert Series with an evening of great poetry and song by immortal writers and composers. 
Setyembre 25
23rd Inception Anniversary Fun Run
UP Academic Oval
5 a.m.
The College of Social Sciences and Philosophy (CSSP) Pahinungod and UP Manila Ugnayan ng Pahinungod will hold a fun run to celebrate the 23rd anniversary of the Ugnayan ng Pahinungod Program.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 2, Blg 6 (5 copies) UP Diliman 2016
column7:
Oktubre-Disyembre
The 3rd UP Gawad Melchora "Tandang Sora" Aquino 
Call for Nominations
The UP Gawad Melchora "Tandang Sora" Aquino is the award bestowed to outstanding social development academics and/or practitioners or social development organizations.
