It honors individuals/organizations who/which have excelled in social work, community development or women development. The UPD College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD) administers the award. Individuals/organizations who/which have excelled and provided exemplary service in social work, community development or women and development may be nominated. 
Oktubre 3
17th Faculty-Student Conference on Statistical Sciences
Shangri-la Hotel, EDSA
The Faculty-Student Conference on Statistical Sciences serves as a medium for the exchange of ideas between budding young researchers and seasoned academics in the field of statistical sciences, and as a venue for statistics students and faculty to disseminate their researches. It is an opportunity to explore different applications and areas of statistics, and experience limitless developments and discoveries in statistical sciences. 
Oktubre 4
Book Launching
October 4, 2016 
1:00 - 4:00 pm
UP College of Home Economics Tearoom 
Diliman, Quezon City 
Soft Book Launching of the Introduction to Food Technology by the Food Science and Nutrition Department of the College of Home Economics
This event will launch the book "Introduction to Food Technology" written by Dr. Miflora Gatchalian, Dr. Sonia Y. De Leon and their co-authors. The two volumes of this book will be discussed in the event. There will also be a brief open forum during the launch. 
Oktubre 5
Call for proposals for the Southeast Asian Studies in Asia Conference 
The Consortium for Southeast Asian Studies in Asia (SEASIA), of which the UP Asian Center is a member, will be holding the 2nd Southeast Asian Studies in Asia Conference (SEASIA 2017) on Dec. 16-17, 2017 in Chulalongkorn University, Bangkok, Thailand. 
Oktubre 7-28
Spatial Halo-Halo 
An Exhibition of works by UPCFA, Department of Visual Communication
Corredor, UPCFA Gallery, Bartlett Hall 
6 p.m.
The exhibit showcases art and design works of faculty members from the College of Fine Arts (CFA) Department of Visual Communication. Over the years, the exhibition has become a department tradition to showcase different creative practices and interests of the faculty of the Visual Communication and Industrial Design programs. More than 30 artists will be contributing their recent works, including ceramics, design, drawings, illustrations, painting, photographs, prints and sculptures. 
Oktubre 10
Innovating the Philippines
The UP EEEI Centennial Lecture Series
PLDT Multimedia Lecture Hall (MLH/VLC) 
2 p.m.
The yearlong lecture series aims to highlight the accomplishments of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Institute (EEEI) alumni, especially those who have been successful in their career in the Philippines. The lectures are also geared to inspire students in pursuing their careers in the country. 
Oktubre 10 
Fast Fashion : The Dark Sides of Fashion
Main Exhibition Hall, Bulwagan ng Dangal 
Gonzalez Hall
6 p.m.
The UP College of Home Economics through the Department of Clothing, Textiles, and Interior Design in collaboration with Goethe Institute is holding an exhibit entitled Fast Fashion : The Dark Side of Fashion. First set up in Hamburg, Germany, the exhibit's main objective is to present the issues and concerns that emanate from the fast paced industyr of garments and textiles. It promotes the green concept and sustainability in fashion. 
Oktubre 12-30
Distrito de Molo
Three one-act plays of Prof. Leoncio Deriada, directed by Prof. Tony Mabesa
Wilfrido Ma. Guerrero Theater
2nd Floor, Palma Hall
7 p.m. (weekdays)
10 a.m. and 3 p.m. (weekends)
Tres Hermanas de Molo - three old maids and their nanny call on the ghosts of the past and pray for repentance for thie freedom. Atlas de Molo - a statue of Atlas comes to life and changes the lives of two newly-weds in their ancestral home in Molo, Iloilo. Venus de Molo - a sculptor and a cruel patron recreates a famous statue but is shocked when they are visited by a local goddess of Molo. 
Oktubre 13
Forum on the selection of the next UP President
UP Cine Adarna
9 a.m.
The Search Committee for the selection of the next UP President will hold a Forum on October 13 at the Cine Adarna, UP DIliman. 
Ten nominees are officially vying for the position. They are Dr. Consolacion R. Alaras, Prof. Danilo L. Concepcion, Prof. Gisela P. Concepcion, J. Prospero E. De Vera III, Prof. Rowena Cristina L. Guevara, Dr. Orlando S. Mercado, Prof. Benito M. Pacheco, Dr. Roger D. Posadas, Prof. Caesar A. Saloma and Prof. Michael L. Tan. 
Oktubre 14
Laboratory Animal Workshop
UP Natural Sciences and Research Institute 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The Biological Research and Services Laboratory of the UP Natural Sciences and Research Institute will be holding a workshop via a short lecture and hands-on session on the care and proper handling of mice, techniques in blood collection and drug administration, and sample collection. 
Oktubre 14-17
Amelia Lapeña-Bonifacio Writers Workshop
UP Institute of Creative Writing Workshop on Speculative Fiction
Microtel, UP Technohub
8 a.m.- 5 p.m.
The first Amelia Lapeña-Bonifacio Writers Workshop (ALBWW) will focus on Speculative Fiction. The works of 12 accepted fellows would be discussed and critiqued during four days of workshop sessions. The ALBWW is meant to complement the annual UP Writers Workshop, which has evolved to accomodate and mentor publised writers in mid-career. 
Oktubre 17-21
Special Course on Urban Transport Planning and Management (SCOUT)
A basic course in transport planning
Cariño Multipurpose Hall
3/F School of Urban and Regional Planning Bldg.
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The SCOUT is a 5-day program which provides an overview of transport planning, its systems, and how it is effectively assessed and managed. 
The program is aimed at capacitating stakeholders in the local and national institutions in understanding transport planning and management in the Philippine context; likewise seeking to help improve physical and social machineries for the benefit of the community. In addition, the program identifies new challenges, directions and trends in transport planning, mobility management strategies, traffic impact assessment and transit-oriented development, among others. 
Oktubre 20-22
National Electrochemical Energy Storage Workshop (CHED-PCARI Program)
Small Auditorium
National Institute of Physics
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
A 3-day workshop where concepts on energy storage and electrochemistry will be discussed such as how batteries and fuel cells work, how to improve their performance and how they are key in the world of renewable energy. 
Oktubre 22
Maglasig-lasig kitabi! : Peste Igal 2016
GT Toyota Auditorium
2 p.m. and 6 p.m.
The festival-concert enjoins audiences to partake of the rich performance traditions of the Sama-Bajau people of the Southern Philippines. It features igal and pansak, Sama-Bajau's traditional dances, and kulintangan ensemble music pieces from four performing groups from Semporna, Malaysia; Bongao, Tawi-Tawi; Apalit, Pampanga; and San Andres, Manila. 
Oktubre 22
IEmagineering for Nation-Building 2016 UP Industrial Engineering Alumni Association (UPIEAA) Symposium
Engineering Theater
Melchor Hall
2 p.m.
The conference aims to fuel the nation-builder among engineers by tackling critical issues and unlocking opportunities in emerging technologies and to rediscover the engineer's role in transforming society. 
Oktubre 22
Street bEats : The 51st UPSE Alumni Homecoming
School of Economics grounds
4-10 p.m.
The University of the Philippines School of Economics (UPSE) will be holding its 51st grand alumni homecoming with a theme Street bEats. There will be live performances by Juan Miguel Salvador of the Authority Band and DJ Galo Marquez. 
Oktubre 24
UPAA SURP Chapter General Assembly and Election
UP School of Urban and Regional Planning 
6-9 p.m.
The UP Alumni Association (UPAA) School of Urban and Regional Planning (SURP) chapter general assembly and election is in line with the upcoming 51st founding anniversary of the UP SURP. 
The UPAA SURP Chapter calls on its alumni to participate in the school's festivities. New members will be inducted into the chapter and honorary membership will be conferred to the individuals who served as deans of the School. A new set of officers will be elected during the assembly. Capping off the night will be a dinner celebration. 
Oktubre 25-28
Seminar Workshop : Participatory Monitoring and Evaluation of Development Projects
Seminar Room
College of Social Work and Community Development
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The training course aims to enhance the understanding and capabilities of participants in developing appropriate monitoring and evaluation systems for their respective community projects and programs. 
Nobyembre 3-25
Beyond My Body
A solo exhibition of new works by Ambie Abaño
Corredor, UP CFA Gallery, Bartlett Hall 
Opening reception November 8, 4:00 p.m. 
Visual artist and UP College of Fine Arts (CFA) faculty member Ambie Abaño remounts her solo exhibition, Beyond My Body, at the Corredor Gallery. Featuring large-scale prints on translucent textile and works on engraved Tanguile wood, the exhibition reflects on the temporal nature of the body and ponders on the essence of eternal selfhood lying within the deeper self : the spirit. In this exhibition, Abaño wields material binaries to represent dual states of being : images in engraved wood present the body in its various stages of deterioration, while images produced from layers of translucent tulle exemplify the ethereal qualities of the spirit. 
Nobyembre 7
The Engineer's Advantage : Becoming a Visionary Entrepreneur
The UP EEEI Centennial Lecture Series
PLDT Multimedia Lecture Hall (MLH/VLC) 
EEEI Bldg. I
2 p.m.
The yearlong lecture series aims to highlight the accomplishments of Electrical and Electronics Engineering Institute (EEEI) alumni, especially those who have been successful in their career in the Philippines. The lectures are also geared to inspire students in pursuing their careers in the country. 
Nobyembre 14-18
Special Course on Urban and Regional Planning : A Basic Course in Urban and Regional Planning (SCURP : ABC in URP)
Cariño Multipurpose Hall
3/F School of Urban and Regional Planning Bldg. 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The Special Course on Urban and Regional Planning : A Basic Course in Urban and Regional Planning (SCURP : ABC in URP) is a 5-day program that serves as an orientation-cum-introductory course on the basic concepts, processes and methodologies of urban and regional planning. 
Nobyembre 15-18
Training on Community Organizing
UP CSWCD Research and Extension for Development Office 
Short Courses
Seminar Room
College of Social Work and and Community Development
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The training is a 4-day course that aims to provide the participants with an overview of concepts, principles, processes and approaches in community organizing in the context of current realities. The phases of community organizing will be discussed as well as the essential skills needed. The course will facilitate the preparation of re-entry plans of the participants that they will use in their respective organizations. 
Nobyembre 24
Empowering Entrepreneurs : The ISSI Journey
A symposium in celebration of UP ISSI's 50th anniversary
UP Institute of Small-Scale Industries (ISSI) 
1-4:30 p.m.
The event will showcase UP ISSI's 50-years worth of experience in providing training, consultancy, research and information towards the development and promotion of entrepreneurship in the country. It will also serve as a venue for information on business and on assistance that is available to entrepreneurs. 
Nobyembre 25
"Kristiyanong Misyon at Antropolohiyang Pilipino"
Palma Hall 400
1-5 p.m.
Ang Round Table Discussion 2016 ay ikatlong beses na paghahandog ng UP Departamento ng Antropolohiya bilang paghahanda nito sa nalalapit na sentenaryo sa taong 2017. 
Ang tema ngayong taon, "Kristiyanong Misyon at Antropolohiyang Pilipino" ay naglalayon na talakayin ang naging ugnayan ng relihiyon at antropolohiyang Pilipino, partikular sa kung anong naging ambag ng mga Kristiyanong misyon sa pag-unlad ng disiplina ng antropolohiya sa Pilipinas. Ang mga tagapagsalita ay sina Dr. Melba Maggay, Dr. Hector Guazon at Dr. Prospero Covar. 
Nobyembre 29
TugSayAwit : Pagpupugay sa OPM mula sa Laybraryang Pinoy
Bulwagan ng Dangal, Gonzalez Hall 
6 p.m.
A tribute to Original Pilipino Music (OPM), the concert will feature live performances by groups composed of The University Library staff. The event is staged in celebration of the Library and Information Services Month.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 2, Blg 7 (14 copies)
column7:
Disyembre 1-3
5th International Conference on Asian Network for GIS-based Historical Studies (ANGIS)
Balay Kalinaw Conference Hall 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Focusing on Asian history and aiming to promote multidisciplinary approaches in exploring historical processes, the upcoming ANGIS Conference will serve as a venue for popularizing innovative methods such as digital visualization, analysis of data using time information system, and the use of Geographic Information Systems (GIS) in analyzing and interpreting historical data that are being undertaken by both academics and practitioners in the fields of history, geography, heritage studies, spatial analysis and modelling, visualization and GIS.
Disyembre 2 
"Himig ng Diliman"
Pag-iilaw 2016
Oblation Plaza
6 n.g.
Ang seremonya ng pag-iilaw ngayong taon ay may temang "Himig ng Diliman." Ang tema ay naglalarawan sa iba't ibang tunog at huni ng mga yunit ng kampus na kapag pinagsama-sama ay bumubuo ng isang magara at makabuluhang awitin. 
Disyembre 2
Madrigal-Gonzalez Awards/Likhaan Journal 10 Launch/UP Writers Night 2016
UP Institute of Creative Writing's Annual Writers Night Celebrations
GT-Toyota Center
4-10 p.m.
LIKHAAN : The UP Institute of Creative Writing (UP ICW) celebrates Philippine writers with three consecutive activities. First is the awarding ceremony for the annual Madrigal-Gonzalez Best First Book Award. This year, the award will be given to a writer in Filipino who has just written his or her first book. This is followed by the launch of Likhaan Journal 10, an annual anthology of Philippine literature published by the UP ICW. This year's editor-in-chief is UP ICW fellow and UP Press director Dr. J. Neil Garcia. 
Disyembre 3 - 16
SPATIAL TERRITORIES : Artistic form in the production of space 
Bulwagan ng Dangal Museum
South Wing Basement, University Library
Opening cocktails on December 3
4 p.m.
The UP College of Fine Arts' MFA Program, under Prof. Katti Sta. Ana's FA 215 Class : Production of Space in Art Production, will hold an exhibit "Spatial Territories."
The exhibit gathers 10 contemporary artists to produce spaces in the artists' particular themes and mediums. All of them are forays into experiences of space - of miniature universes, virtual connections, memories, empowerment, disempowerment, grief, delight and confusion. 
Disyembre 10
'Sang Siglong Sigla! 
UP High, UP Elem, UP Prep, UPIS Grand Alumni Homecoming
UPIS Grades 7-12 Quadrangle 
4 p.m.
This is the annual alumni homecoming of the UP Integrated School (UPIS) and all its progenitor schools - UP Elementary  School, UP Preparatory School and UP High School. This year's homecoming marks the centennial celebratio of UP High School which was established in 1916. UPIS Batch 1991 will be hosting the event.
The event is open to all alumni, teachers, former teachers and friends of UPIS, UP Elem, UP Prep and UP High. 
Disyembre 12
KAPPanimad-on Round Table Discussion (RTD) Serye 2 : Sampaksaan sa Domestikong Problema at Panlabas ng Polisiya sa Ilalim ng Administrasyong Duterte
Pagtalakay sa mga Pananaw, Pag-unawa at Pagtaya ng Agham Panlipunan at Pilosopiya
Pavilion 2 Conference Room, Palma Hall 
8:30 a.m. - 12 : 30 p.m.
Layunin ng KAPPanimad-on RTD 2 na mabigyan ng pagkakataon ang mga larang mula sa Kolehiyo ng Agham Panlipunan at Pilosopya (KAPP) na makapagbigay ng kanilang pananaw at gampanin sa administrasyong Duterte. Sa pagkamit ng layuning ito, magiging mas malinaw ang hangarin ng Kolehiyong mamulat, makapag-ambag at makisangkot sa mga lokal at pandaigdigang usapin. 
Ang mga magiging tagapagsalita ay mula sa iba't ibang disiplina tulad ng Agham Pampulitika, Sosyolohiya, Pilosopiya at Antropolohiya. 
Ang aktibidad na ito ay inorganisa ng Komite sa Indihinesasyon ng KAPP. 
Disyembre 14
Dreams Turned to Reality 
UPIS Field Demonstration / Street Dance Competition 2016
University Amphitheater 
6:30 - 11 a.m. 
The event showcases the unity, creativity and dancing prowess of UPIS students : from Kindergarten to Grade 6 via a field demonstration; and the Grades 7-10 students via a street dance competition. The competition will be preceded by a parade of participants in full costume from the UPIS grounds to the amphitheater. 
Disyembre 14
Singkwenta Filipino : Gabi ng Parangal at Paglulunsad ng 50 Aklat
Balay Kalinaw, UP Diliman
6 -9 n.g.
Ang gabing ito ay kulminasyon ng pagdiriwang ng ikalimampung taon ng pagkakatatag ng UP Departamento ng Filipino at Panitikan ng Pilipinas (DFPP). Magtitipon-tipon ang kaguruan ng DFPP, nakaraan at kasalukuyan, upang sariwain ang pagpupunyagi ng Departamento sa pagsusulong ng wika, panitikan, malikhaing pagsulat at araling Pilipino sa Unibersidad. 
Disyembre 15
Pagsulong ng Kabataan sa Katarungan para sa Karapatang Pantao (KKK) 
Pagdiriwang ng Araw ng Karapatang Pantao 2016 ng UPIS
UPIS Grade 7-12 Building
9 n.u. - 12 n.t.
Sa kauna-unahang pagdiriwang ng Araw ng Karapatang Pantao, layunin na imulat ang buong paaralan sa mga usapin ng karapatang pantao hindi lamang sa kasalukuyan kundi sa naging pag-unlad nito sa kasaysayan. 
Sa diwa ng Katipunan na nagsulong ng pambansang kasarinlan, nilalayon ng Departamento ng Araling Panlipunan at ng Kilusang Araling Panlipunan na buhayin ang pagsusuri at pakikisangkot ng mga mag-aaral ng UPIS sa mainit na usaping pambansa. 
Ang tema ng pagdiriwang na ito ay "Pagsusulong ng Kabataan sa Katarungan para sa Karapatang Pantao" o KKK. Magkakaroon ng eksibit, tagis at talino, at Bayani ng Karapatang Pantao, mga patimpalak na sasalihan ng mga mag-aaral ng UPIS. Ang mga aktibidad para sa KKK ay bukas sa publiko. 
Disyembre 15
UPIS Battle of the Bands 2016
UPIS Grades 7-12 Quadrangle 
3 - 6 p.m.
Battle of the Bands is a talent competition among bands whose members are all current UPIS students from grades 6 to 10. Bands are required to perform two songs. One of which must be an original composition based on a theme provided by the UPIS Student Council (a.k.a. Kamag Aral or KA). 
Disyembre 16
"Himig ng Diliman, Hagkan ang Gabi"
UPD Parada ng mga Parol 2016
Academic Oval 
4 p.m.
Mga akademikong yunit lamang ng Unibersidad ang kalahok sa Paligsahan ng mga Parol bagamat maaring gumawa at magparada ng parol ang anumang yunit at organisasyon sa Unibersidad. 
Sa pamamagitan ng mga parol na ipaparada ng mga yunit, layuning ipaala-ala ang mga hamong pinagtagumpayan ng ating pamayanan at ang mga pagbubunying kalakip nito. Para patingkarin pa ang tema, binibigyan ng espesyal na halaga ang aspeto ng malikhaing paggamit ng ilaw sa mga parol. 
Enero 20-21
UP ICW Teachers Seminar on Creative Writing and Malikhaing Pagsulat
Microtel, UP Technohub, Diliman, Quezon City
9 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
LIKHAAN : The UP Institute of Creative Writing (ICW) will be holding a two-day teachers' training and seminar on Creative Writing and Malikhaing Pagsulat as a response to developments brought about by the implementation of the K-12 program.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 3, Blg 1 (7 copies)
column7:
Pebrero 1-28
Sansinukob : Isang Installation na Eksibit
UP Lagoon and CMC Hill 
6 p.m. (Pasinaya) 
Ang eksibit ay isang paglalahad ng pinagmulan ng sansinukob (universe) sang-ayon sa mga umiral at umiiral na kuwentong-bayan at alamat ng mga etnolinggwistikong pangkat ng Pilipinas. Sa pamamagitan ng eksibit mamamalas ang sinaunang etnoastronomiya at kosmolohiya bilang kaalamang-bayan ng mga kulturang Pilipino. ASng mga kaalamang-bayang ito ay hindi lamang humahanmgga sa kagyat na kapaligiran gaya ng lupa at tubig, kundi lumalagos din sa kalangitan at kabilang-buhay. 
Pebrero 1 
Seremonya ng Pagbubukas : K'wentong Bayan, Kaalamang Bayan
Harap ng Faculty Center 
6 p.m.
Ang mga k'wentong bayan ay mga naratibong pasalita na nagpasalin-salin sa maraming henerasyon. Naririnig ang mga ito sa mga pangkomunidad na seremonya at ritwal at sa pang-araw-araw na paghuhuntahan at pagkukuwentuhan ng mga mamamayan. 
Madalas ang mga k'wentong bayan ang nagpapaliwanag ng iba't ibang aspekto ng tradisyon, mga kalakaran at pati na rin kasaysayan. 
Sa pagdiriwang ng Pambansang Buwan ng Sining, sasariwain ng UP Diliman ang mga k'wentong bayan na kinalalakipan ng iba't ibang saysay, pukaw at kaalaman. 
Pebrero 1
2017 World Hijab Day
"UP-IIS Hijab Day and Interfaith Forum 2017 : Celebrating Women's Right in the Light of the Islam"
UP Balay Kalinaw
1-5 p.m.
The event highlights the significance of hijab in the life of a Muslim woman as well as her rights in the light of Islamic teaching. The interfaith component of the program promotes respect and tolerance among people of different faiths. 
Pebrero 2
Maceda 100 : Cassettes 100
Restaging of the "Cassettes 100" Event
Vargas Museum
7 p.m. 
In 1971, Cassettes 100 was performed at the lobby of the Cultural Center of the Philippines, where one hundred 'musicians' weaved their way through an 'audience' strolling around, each carrying a cassette player playing one of the hundred pre-recorded tracks of various Philippine instruments. 
This restaging is a collaborative effort between the UP College of Music and the UP College of Engineering. 
Pebrero 4, 11, 18 at 25
Mandatory Continuing Legal Education Saturday Course
2/F Conference Room, Law Center 
8 a.m. - 6 p.m.
Members of the Integrated Bar of the Philippines not exempt under Rule 7 of Bar Matter 850 shall complete every three (3) years at least thirty-six (36) hours of continuing legal education activities approved by the MCLE Office. 
Pebrero 7-9
K'wentong Bayan sa Pelikula
UP Cine Adarna
5 p.m. and 7 p.m.
Pebrero 7
5 p.m. Ang Panggagahasa kay Fe
7 p.m. Ploning
Pebrero 8
5 p.m. K'na the Dreamweaver
7 p.m. Sayaw ng Dalawang Kaliwang Paa
Pebrero 9
5 p.m. Hinulid
7 p.m. Tuos
Pebrero 9-10
The Third World Studies Center in its 40th Year : An International Conference 
GT-Toyota Asian Center Auditorium, Asian Center
8:30 a.m. - 6 p.m.
The Third World Studies Center (TWSC) gathers in this multidisciplinary conference its vast and resilient network of scholars and public intellectuals, academics and activists, advocates and bearers of unorthodox views from different parts of the globe. Dr. Alfred W. McCoy, Harrington Professor of History at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, is the keynote speaker and former TWSC directors will be featured in one of the plenary sessionsm while former TWSC visiting research fellows, contributors to Kasarinlan : Philippine Journal of Third World Studies, and members of the TWSC network will be presenting papers in 11 panels. Documentaries produced by student interns of TWSC and the University of Montreal will also be screened.
Pebrero 9
Diwata ng Bayan
Isang Dulang-Awit ng Ating Kasaysayan Tampok ang Natatanging Pagtatanghal ng Samahang Teatro ng Sinilangan
Tanghalang Nicanor Abelardo 
(CCP Main Theater)
8 p.m.
Isinulat bilang papuri sa alaala nina Gat Andres Bonifacio at Gregoria de Jesus, ang Dakilang Supremo at mandirigmang Lakambini ng Kataastaasan Kagalanggalangang Katipunan (KKK) ng mga Anak ng Bayan; at ng mandudulang 'sedisyoso' na si Aurelio Tolentino ng KKK, gayundin sa kanyang mga kapanalig at kapwa alagad ng sining sa 'teatrong rebolusyonaryo' noong digmaang Pilipino-Amerikano, at sa lahat ng mga bayani, bathala at bathaluman sa ating kasaysayan, mitolohiya at mga kuwentong-bayan. 
Pebrero 10
The 5th UP Electrical and Electronics Engineers Alumni Association, Inc. (UPEEEAAI) Golf Classic
Ayala Greenfield Golf and Leisure Club, Calamba, Laguna
8 a.m.
The UP EEEAAI invites all golf enthusiasts, UP alumni or otherwise, to join the 5th edition of the UPEEEAAI Golf Classic - the culminating activity of the UP EEE Institute centennial celebration and a fund raiser to help support the continuing scholarship and research programs of the UPEEEAAI. 
Pebrero 10 at 24
Himigsikan : Mga K'wentong Bayan
UP Carillon Plaza
6 p.m.
Mga awiting-bayan na bibigyan ng bagong anyo sa pamamagitan ng kontemporaryong musika at interpretasyon. 
Gaya ng nakagawiang anyo ng libangan tuwing hapon sa lilim ng mga puno, ito ay pagsasariwa ng mga awiting kinagisnan ng ating mga magulang at ninuno at sasaliwan ng makabagong tugtugan at areglo bilang pagpapamalas na maging ang mga awiting-bayan ay may kakayahang lumagos sa mga salinlahi at umagapay sa nagbabagong panahon. 
Ang mga palabas ay libre at bukas para sa lahat
Pebrero 10 - Noel Cabangon
Pebrero 24 - Bayang Barrios 
Pebrero 12 
COEXIST 2017 : Help Rebuild Libraries and Communities
Melchor Hall/Academic Oval
5 a.m.
The UP College of Engineering Library will be holding a Fun Run event to raise funds for the benefit of the Library's outreach program which will support a chosen beneficiary community. The activity intends to help rebuild libraries in the country that were affected by natural disasters. Moreover, it aims to promote the Library's "I Love My Library Campaign" to raise awareness on the importance of involving the community with the libraries. 
Pebrero 15-28
Si Faust at ang Dalumat ng Kasamaan : Isang Adaptasyon ng Akda ni Goethe
Wilfrido Ma. Guerrero Theater, Palma Hall
Weekdays shows at 7 p.m.
Weekends shows at 10 a.m. and 3 p.m. 
For its 41st Theater Season, Dulaang UP (DUP) presents Rody Vera's adaptation of Faust. The play opens in heaven where the devil, Mephisto, wagers with God that he can corrupt his most virtuous servant Faust. What follows in the play is a series of events that questions the preconceived notions of justice, rationality and morality. 
Pebrero 15
Sagunja and Minhwa : Korean Traditional Painting Workshop
Asian Center, Philippine and China Hall
10 a.m. - 1 p.m. and 2-5 p.m. 
The UP Korean Research Center (UP KRC), in partnership with the Korean Cultural Center (KCC) in the Philippines and UP Asian Center, organizes the "Sagunja and Minhwa : Korean Traditional Painting Workshop".
Sagunja, also called "The Four Gracious Plants," is comprised of four elements - plum, orchid, chrystanthemum and bamboo. Minhwa is a Korean folk art that illustrates common people's freedom of expression and hope in carefree unconventionality. 
Pebrero 17
5th ERDT Congress
SMX Convention Center, Pasay City
9 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The Engineering Research and Development for Technology (ERDT) Congress is a yearly event where researchers, ERDT scholars, visiting professors, industry, government, and other experts gather and get inspired by plenary lectures of highly distinguished professors and experts all over the world. The Congress also serves as a platform in setting directions for research and development. 
Pebrero 17
A Multidisciplinary Symposium on the Roots of Transcultural Philippine Music
Abelardo Hall Auditorium
1-4 p.m.
The articulation of Filipino modernities in the arts had two tendencies : 1) the middle-class elevation of the folk materials to "high culture" (e.g., use of balitao and kundiman in Western classical-art forms), and 2) the channelization of the Filipino folk-popular to wider audiences via the mass media, which was not isolated from the first tendency. 
This symposium looks into these two tendencies, but give more space to the realm of the second in which vernacular Filipino literature, folk dances, Filipinized bodabil, and radio songs got broadcast audiences attuned to the emerging "alternative Filipino modernities." This strand emerged during the formative years when capitalism and flow of images and ideas accelerated between 1898 and 1941.
Pebrero 18-19
Sita & Rama : Papet Ramayana
Institute of Biology Auditorium
10 a.m. and 3 p.m. 
Ito ay istorya ni Prinsipe Rama at ang pagpapalayas sa kaniya sa kaharian ng kaniyang amang si Haring Dashrata. Isinasaad din nito ang paglalakbay ni Rama sa kagubatan ng India kasama ang kaniyang asawang si Sita at ang kapatid na si Laksmana, pati ang kaniyang pakikidigma kay Haring Ravana dahil sa pagdukot nito sa asawa niyang si Sita; hanggang sa kaniyang pagbabalik sa kanilang kaharian matapos ang labing-apat na taon at pagkahirang bilang hari. 
Pebrero 20-25
CSSP Week
Agham Panlipunan at Pilosopiya : Mapanuring Gabay sa Bayang Pilipino
Palma Hall 
8 a.m. (everyday) 
The College of Social Sciences and Philosophy (CSSP) Week celebrates the 33rd annual founding of the College since 1983. The celebration features a series of exhibits, workshops, roundtable discussions, panel discussions, public lectures and recognition events, together with a symposium, a faculty colloquium, a conference, a Zumba program, and an open house, led by the various offices, departments, institutes and centers of the College. 
Pebrero 22 
Late 19th Century Terremotos in Luzon : Scientific Researches, Post-Disaster Rehabilitation, and the Colonial State
A Public Lecture by Kerby C. Alvarez 
CSSP Audio-Visual Room (Palma Hall 207) 2-4 p.m. 
The lecture will present a historical study of earthquakes that devastated the island of Luzon in the late 19th century. It attempts to present a narrative of state-subject relations in Spanish-Philippines through the lens of hazards and disaster response. The public lecture is part of UP CSSP Week and presented by the UP Department of History. 
Pebrero 22-28
UP Engineering Week 2017
Engineering Week or Eng'g Week is the biggest and most awaited event of the College of Engineering. It is composed of night and day events organized by different organizations in the college. Over five thousand engineering students gather to participate in a variety of events ranging from simple games of chance to grand performances of talents and skills. 
Pebrero 22
Silakbo : Smoker Night 2017
UP Film Institute 
7 p.m. - 12 a.m. 
Continuing its tradition of promoting creativity and critical thinking, SILAKBO : Smoker Night 2017 aims to attack, tackle, and criticize relevant social issues through a comedic play. This night event features Engineering-based organizations as they exhibit unique interpretations of their stands on the issues. This event also aims to showcase the holistic upbringing of UP engineering students who are not only excellent in their fields but are also proactive members of the society. As the first student-participated night even of the Engineering Week, Smoker Night propels the fighting spirit of each engineering student. 
Pebrero 24
Sidlak : Tanghalan 2017
Engineering Theater
Melchor Hall
5:30 - 8:30 p.m. 
Now on its second year, the Engineering Student Council brings SIDLAK : TANGHALAN 2017, an event where our faculty, administration, and staff can showcase their talents to the Engineering community and where the students can show their utmost appreciation outside of the classroom setup and have an avenue to celebrate them. With the theme of the Giant Lantern Festival, the event will present home-grown performers as they are the ones who truly light up the lives inside the college. This year, the event aims to be even more inclusive by welcoming unaffiliated engineering students to perform as well. 
Pebrero 25
Jammeng'g
TBA
6 p.m. - 12 a.m.
Jammeng'g is a themed battle of the bands where different amateur bands hailing from various engineering organizations fight it out in a battle royal of musicality and artistic skills. Engineering students jam with them as they rock it out with all their hearts, eyeing the elusive prize. Bands are given the liberty to choose their songs of preference, as long as they comply with the theme of the year. On stage, they perform back-to-back with popular musicians of the day in front of a live audience in a raging party full of music and free-flowing drinks. 
Pebrero 27
Awitan
Henry Lee Irwin Theater
Ateneo de Manila University
6 p.m. - 12 m.n.
Awitan is a prestigious university-wide choral competition open to all amateur choral groups of the different student organizations in UP Diliman. Since its conception in 1965, Awitan has serenaded the engineering community with a wide variety of Christmas and Themed chorale pieces. This year's pre-judging system is geared towards a bias-free evaluation of the participating groups via blind judging. Participating groups would have the opportunity to hear the comments and suggestions of the judges after their pre-judging performance.  
Pebrero 28
Indakan
UP Film Institute
6 p.m. - 12 a.m.
Indakan is a theme dance competition which aims to showcase the talents and creativity of amateur groups from various engineering organizations. Performances shall highlight the given theme through their dance choreograhy and music. This year, Indakan traces Filipino native roots as it features the diversity and richness of Filipino culture and tradition. The performers from the group category will take its audience around the country as they embody Filipino cultural dance and celebration through their performances that shall bring different Philippine festivals from the streets to the stage. 
Pebrero 23
Sampaksaan sa K'wentong Bayan : Pambansang Kumperensiya ng K'wentong Bayan
UP NISMED
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Ang Sampaksaan ay may pangunahing layunin na mailahad ang kasaysayan at kabuluhan ng mga k'wentong bayan at kaugnayan ng mga mga ito sa pagbuo ng pambansang kamalayan. Layunin din ng kumperensiya ang makalikha ng dalumat hinggil sa k'wentong bayan at mga mito gamit ang balangakas at/o modelong interdisiplinaryo. Ang kumperensiya ay libre at bukas para sa lahat. Ang mga dadalo ay tatanggap ng Certificate of Participation. 
Pebrero 23 
3rd UP Gawad Tandang Sora Recognition of Outstanding Social Development Practitioners or Social Development Organizations
Bulwagang Tandang Sora
9 a.m.- 12 p.m.
The College of Social Work and Community Development bestows Gawad Tandang Sora to outstanding social development practitioners or social development organizations that excelled in social work, community development, or women and development. 
The award is named after Melchora "Tandang Sora" Aquino, the "Mother of the Philippine Revolution," whose life and deeds represent love of country and service to her fellow Filipinos. The awardee, like Tandang Sora, will be honored for a life of devotion to country and service to the Filipino people as a social development practitioner or as a social development organization. 
Pebrero 23
Aral at Aksyon : A Forum on CSWCD Alumni in Action
Bulwagang Tandang Sora
1-5 p.m.
The UP CSWCD Alumni Association will lead the forum that will showcase its outstanding alumni in various fields. In celebration of the College days, its alumni in government agencies, communities, local government units, NGOs and academe will share their insights to the CSWCD community. 
Pebrero 24
CSWCD Service Awards
Recognition of Faculty, REPS and Administrative Staff for their Loyalty and Service to the College/University
Bulwagang Tandang Sora
9 a.m.-12 p.m.
Part of the annual celebration of the College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD) days is the recognition of its faculty members, Research Extension and Professional Staff (REPS) and administrative staff for their loyalty and service to the College/University. For 2017, College-based student organizations will also be acknowledged for their support and service to the students and CSWCD's activities for the entire year. 
Pebrero 25-26
Ang Unang Aswang ni Rody Vera
Tampok ang UP Dance Company
Arki Amphitheater
7 p.m.
Bahagi ng pagdiriwang ng UP Diliman Month 2017 ang pagtatanghal ng orihinal na akda ni Rody Vera, Ang Unang Aswang, sa pamamagitan ng sayaw at galaw. Ito ay sa koryograpiya at direksiyon ni JM Cabling at sa pamamatnubay ni Prop. Angela Baguilat na parehong mula sa UPD Kolehiyo ng Musika. 
Ang UP Dance Company, isa sa mga pitong opisyal na organisasyong pangtanghal ng UPD, ang siyang naatasang magbigay buhay sa dula.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 3, Blg 2 (3 copies)
column7:
Marso 1
CSWCD Golden Anniversary Lecture Series
What is the Cutting Edge of Women and Development Studies?
Bulwagang Tandang Sora
1-5 p.m.
As part of its year-long golden anniversary celebration, the College of Social Work and Community Development will be conducting lecture series featuring the expertise of its faculty from the Department of Women and Development Studies (DWDS). With its pioneering programs in women and gender studies in the Asia-Pacific region, the DWDS will provide relevant discussions on its academic program. 
Marso 1 
Call for Nominations for the UP Gawad Plaridel 2017
The UP College of Mass Communication (UP CMC) is now accepting nominations for the annual UP Gawad Plaridel which recognizes Filipino media practitioners who have excelled in any of the media (print, radio, film, television and new media) and have performed with the highest level of professional integrity in the interest of public service. For 2017, the 13th UP Gawad Plaridel will be given to an outstanding television practitioner. 
Marso 1-5 
Si Faust at ang Dalumat ng Kasamaan : Isang Adaptasyon ng Akda ni Goethe
Wilfrido Ma. Guerrero Theater, Palma Hall
Weekday shows at 7 p.m.
Weekend shows at 10 a.m. and 3 p.m.
For its 41st Theater Season, Dulaang UP presents Rody Vera's adaptation of Faust. The play opens in heaven where the devil, Mephisto, wagers with God that he can corrupt God's most virtuous servant Faust. What follows in the play is a series of events that questions the preconceived notions of justice, rationality and morality. Neil Sese and Jack Yabut alternate in the title role of Faust while Paolo O'Hara and Mailes Kanapi as Mephisto. Karen Gaerlan plays the innocent and pious Gretchen. The play is directed by Jose Estrella with an artistic team led by Sir Anril Tiatco, Jem Javier and Bryan Viray (dramaturgy), Barbie Tan-Tiongco (lights), Carlo Pagunaling (costume), Ed Lacson (set), JM Cabling (choreography) and Jon Lazam (sounds).
Marso 2
Dulaang Engineering 
UP Film Institute
6-11 p.m.
The event showcases the talent in theater production of the Engineering students through play performances that promote responsiveness on current trends and issues in the college, the university and the country. 
Marso 3
Singkwenta! Samu't Saring Musika
Abelardo Hall Auditorium
UP College of Music
6 p.m. 
In celebration of 50 years of excellence and service, the Music Education Department of the UP College of Music invites everyone to its anniversary concert "Singkwenta! Samu't Saring Musika." The concert highlights the resident groups of the department such as UP Rondalla, Musika Sophia Recorder Ensemble, Guitar Guild, JMEG Chorale, Cuerdas Comparsa String Ensemble with guest performers Profs. Mauricia Borromeo and Leticia Del Valle, Taytay Youth Philharmonic Orchestra, Talayan Children's Rondalla, NCCA Rondalla and the QMC Golden Nightingale Senior Citizen's Chorale. 
Marso 3
Miss Engineering
University Theater
Miss Engineering is the most awaited night event of Engineering Week. Male students from the College of Engineering, representing their organizations, compete in a grand pageant composed of photoshoots, video shoots, charity event, seminars, workshops, rehearsals and the pageant night. Each candidate will also portray a specific Philippine goddess or deity, while carrying the theme "Self-Empowerment."
Marso 4
Call for submission of entries to The 2017 Amelia Lapeña-Bonifacio Literary Contest
This long running literary competition in English is open to all bonafide students of the University enrolled in the second semester AY 2016-17 excluding faculty members of the Department of English and Comparative Literature. 
Marso 4-5
Ang Unang Aswang ni Rody Vera Tampok ang UP Dance Company
Arki Amphitheater
7 p.m.
Ang Unang Aswang ay isang pagtatanghal ng orihinal na akda ni Rody Vera sa pamamagitan ng sayat at galaw ng UP Dance Company, isa sa mga pitong opisyal na organisasyong pangtanghal ng UPD. Ito ay sa saliw ng koreograpiya at direksiyon ni JM Cabling at sa pamamatnubay ni Prop. Angela Baguilat na parehong mula sa UPD Kolehiyo ng Musika. 
Marso 4
Engineering Week Afterparty
Cities Events Place, 30 Sgt. Esguerra Ave. Diliman, Quezon City
7 p.m. 
This is the awards night of the Engineering Week 2017, as well as the afterparty of the week-long event with the theme "Sinulog" or street party. 
The Engineering community is invited to attend a night full of enjoyment and celebration of Engineering Week 2017. A short program will start the event to be followed by the awarding of the Top 10 and special awards by popular demand. The night will be capped with a party. 
Marso 10-27
Flat to Functional 
An exhibition of flat-pack, self-assembly furniture
Foyer, UP Fine Arts Gallery, Bartlett Hall 
6 p.m.
The one-man exhibition by industrial designer and UP College of Fine Arts faculty member Tito de la Peña takes advantage of the digital design workflow and explores the use of the flat-pack model as a key design method. The show features recent furniture design prototypes for everyday use. Flat-pack furniture design offers practical solutions for people in need of affordable and almost ready-to-use goods. Through digital design development and production, it can now be implemented more quickly and at significantly less cost, providing people with more economical options for home furnishing needs. These products can be efficiently deployed to communities in disaster-affected areas and other offsite places. 
Marso 10-27
Reconfigurations
A sculpture exhibition by UP College of Fine Arts faculty
Corredor, UP Fine Arts Gallery, Bartlett Hall 
6 p.m.
This group exhibition of recent works by Roberto Feleo, Toym Imao, Paul Quiaño and Manolo Sicat explores the reinterpretation of figurative sculpture. It revisits the foundations of three-dimensional figuration as points for further creative exploration of the human form. The featured artists belong to different generations of sculptors and represent a diverse range of themes, media and practices in this field. All four are faculty members of the UP College of Fine Arts, handling core and major courses in materials and techniques in sculpture. A panel discussion wih the artists is scheduled on March 24, from 1 to 4 p.m. 
Marso 12-19
56th UP National Writers Workshop
BP International Makiling, Los Baños, Laguna
8 a.m.-5 p.m.
The UP National Writers Workshop is an annual workshop catering to writers who have published or are on the brink of publishing a book, or have won an award. The 2017 fellows  are Arbeen Acuña (Fiction, Filipino), Christa De La Cruz (Poetry, Filipino), Zeno Denolo (Fiction, Filipino), Rowena Festin (Fiction, Filipino), Rogene Gonzales (Poetry, Filipino), Arvin Mangohig (Poetry, English), Arnie Mejia (Creative Non Fiction, English), Paolo Enrico Melendez (Creative Non Fiction, English), Charisse-Fuschia Paderna (Poetry, English), Wilfredo Pascual (Creative Non Fiction, English), Karren Renz Seña (Fiction, English), and Alvin Ursua (Poetry, Filipino). The works of these 12 fellows will be discussed and critiqued during a week-long series of workshop sessions, aimed at helping them further hone their craft. 
Marso 14
38th Gabriel A. Bernardo Memorial Lecture
UPD Main Library Lobby
8:30 a.m. 
The UP School of Library and Information Studies will be holding the 38th Gabriel A. Bernardo Memorial Lecture, with Prof. Iris Thiele Isip-Tan of the UP Manila Medical Informatics Unit as the guest speaker. Isip-Tan will be discussing the current state of health informatics in the Philippines and the role of librarians in health informatics. 
Marso 20-24
Seminar Workshop : The Islamic Procedure in Inheritance and Family Cases at the Republic of the Philippines
Microtel, UP-Ayala Technohub
Commonwealth Avenue, Quezon City
8:30 a.m. - 8 p.m. 
The UP Institute of Islamic Studies will be hosting 17 Justices from the Judiciary Department of the Kingdom of Thailand for a seminar workshop on Islamic Procedure in Inheritance and Family Cases. One of the highlights of the program is a courtesy visit to selected government offices including the Court of Appeals of the Philippine Judiciary. The event is in collaboration with the Research Association for Islamic Social Sciences, Inc. (RAIS), and the Office of the Judiciary in Thailand. 
Marso 21-24
The B.I.G. CSSP Career Fair
Build, Innovate, Generate : Pursue your passion, Serve the Nation!
Palma Hall Lobby
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The College of Social Sciences and Philosophy (CSSP) is holding the 6th annual CSSP Career Fair. Invited in this event are different non-government organizations, corporations, schools transitioning to the K-12 curriculum to offer CSSP majors and all UP Diliman students with socially relevant jobs and careers in line with their own courses-to emphasize the vital role both CSSP and UPD students play as nation-builders. A career talk by CSSP alumni will also be held on March 22 at the Palma Hall Room 400. 
Marso 21
Comparative Public Administration : Thailand and Philippines
UP NCPAG Case Room
1-4 p.m.
The UP National College of Public Administration and Governance (NCPAG) is holding a lecture on the comparison of public administration in Thailand and Philippines. Guest speaker is Dean Sida Sonsri. The talk is part of the lecture series featuring people who have been instrumental, or who have made significant contributons, in the advancement of the study of public administration in the Philippines and abroad and/or advocates of accountability, transparency, efficiency and responsible practice of public service and governance. 
Marso 21
CSWCD Golden Anniversary Lecture Series
Transformative and Transnational Social Work Practice
Bulwagang Tandang Sora
1-5 p.m.
As part of its year-long golden anniversary celebration, the College of Social Work and Community Development will be conducting lecture series featuring the expertise of its faculty from the Department of Social Work (DSW). Recognized by the Commission on Higher Education as a Center of Excellence in Social Work, the DSW will be engaging its audience to an interesting topic on "Transformative and Transnational Social Work Practice."
Marso 25
Microbiology : Redefining Lives through Research, Development and Community Extension : A Cluster 2 Symposium
Institute of Biology Auditorium 
The symposium targets to bring together researchers, scientists, faculty members, college students, entrepreneurs and even microbe enthusiasts in the fields of agriculture, environmental sciences, biotechnology, industrial microbiology and other related fields to communicate current research priorities and progress in these fields, and to identify new research approaches. It particularly aims to present the status of microbiology in the fields of agriculture, aquatic, environment and industry. 
Marso 25
Ihudyat! 2017
UP Amphitheater 
4 p.m.
Ihudyat! 2017 is a festival of the finest marching bands of the Philippines. The Festival will feature the Citizen's Brigade Band of Dasmariñas Cavite, University of Luzon Drum & Bugle Corps, Philippine Air Force Band, Philippine Army Band, Philippine Coast Guard Band, Philippine Marines (Navy Fleet) Drum & Bugle Corps, and the UP ROTC Symphonic Band. 
Marso 31
Talasalitaan 2017 : Ang Wikang Filipino sa Pagsulong ng Karapatan ng Kababaihan
UP College of Law
9-11 a.m.
Ang Talasalitaan ay isang regular na forum na isinasagawa ng Sentro ng Wikang Filipino (SWF)- UP Diliman na nagtatampok ng iba't-ibang paksa na may kaugnayan sa pananaliksik at pagtuturo ng wikang Filipino sa iba't ibang disiplina para sa lalo pang pagpapaunlad at pagsulong ng Palisi sa Wika ng Unibersidad ng Pilipinas. Ngayong Marso, magiging paksa ng Talasalitaan ang Ang Wikang Filipino sa Pagsulong ng Karapatan ng Kababaihan. Kasamang tagapagtaguyod ng SWF-UP Diliman ang iba't ibang organisasyon sa loob at labas ng Unibersidad tulad ng Gabriela Women's Partylist, UP Center for Women and Gender Studies, UP Diliman Gender Office at UP Portia Sorority ng UP College of Law.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 3, Blg 4 (4 copies)
column7:
Ang Paglalakbay ng Pagpapaunlad ng Pamayanan 
CSWCD Golden Lecture Series
Bulwagang Tandang Sora
2-8 p.m.
As part of the 50th Anniversary Celebration of the College of Social Work and Community Development, the Department of Community Development and the Community Development Society of the Philippines present "Ang Paglalakbay ng Pagpapaunlad ng Pamayanan." Professors Elmer Ferrer and Maureen Pagaduan together with Prof. Emeritus Ka Lito Manalili, through a lecture, will share their experiences and expertise in the field of Community Development. A cultural and solidarity night floows right after the lecture. 
Mayo 6
AGORA 2017 : Arkichange
Melchor Hall Theater
1-4 p.m.
An annual event of the Architecture Student Council (ASC) since 2015, Agora serves as an avenue where peolle can exchange ideas through a multi-perspective view on Architecture and its related fields. This year, Agora focuses on how the present and future professionals in the field of architecture could own up their vital role and be able to contribute to the positive changes in our country. Representatives from the government, private architecture practitioners, private developers, non-government organizations and the academe will join the panel discussion for a holistic approach on the said topic. 
Mayo 8
TAMPISAW
Huling HirEAT sa Tag-init
Alonso Hall, College of Home Economics
8 a.m. - 4 p.m.
Food Science (FS) 178 Product Fair is an annual event organized by the 4th year BSt Food Technology students under the supervision of the FS 178 Faculty-in-Charge from the Department of Food Science and Nutrition, College of Home Economics. The event showcases various food products prototypes that were developed using High Impact Technology Solutions (HITS) equipment at the UP Diliman DOST Food Innovation Facility.
Mayo 8-12
HARAYA 2017
College of Architecture Complex 
8 a.m.-7 p.m.
Haraya is an annual exposition of the UP Diliman College of Architecture. It showcases the outstanding body of works of its students, faculty and research associates, complimented by a series of activities geared towards the promotion of a collaborative environment between design students and professionals. The event is an open-house activity wherein everyone is welcome to view the students' works. This grand exhibit is accompanied by different activities per day that are open to the public as well; such as presentation of best theses, presentation of faculty research, architecture and landscape architecture workshops, talks on career advancement, contests and social nights. 
Mayo 8-22
Multiple Modalities
An exhibition by the FA 222 graduate class under Prof. Josephine Turalba
Bulwagan ng Dangal, Main Hall
UP Main Library
Six artists from UP Diliman Master of Fine arts (MFA) program, in their FA222 : Studies and Exploration of Contemporary Methods and Approaches to Art Production class, explore non-habitual approaches to art production within their individual praxis in their show entitled Multifocal Modalities. The exhibit's highlights the artist's experimentations on various techniques, processes, media and modalities in production. 
Mayo 12-27
Still/Moving
An exhibition by the FA 215 class under Prof. Katti Sta. Ana
Bulwagan ng Dangal, Atelyer
UP Main Library
The exhibition presents the end-of-semester output of four MFA students, namely, Weena Espardinez, Titat Ledesma, Annie Pacaña-Lumbao, and Manolo Sicat as they tackle problems in the creation of space in art making in FA 215 under Prof. Katti Sta. Ana.
Mayo 15-19
CSSP-NSTP : Naglilingkod sa KAPPwa, Naglilingkod sa Bayan! 
Isang Linggong Eksibit
1/F, Palma Hall Lobby
8 a.m.-5 p.m.
Ang isang linggong eksibit na ito ay maglalaman ng mga larawan, poster at iba pang exhibit material na magtatanghal sa mga paglilingkod na ginawa ng mga mag-aaral ng National Service Training Program (NSTP) sa College of Social Sciences and Philosophy (CSSP) ngayong Ikalawang Semester, Akademikong Taon (AT) 2016-2017. Katatampukan ito ng pagbubukas ng eksibit mula sa unang araw at patimpalak para sa Natatanging Community Engagement Poster; at Natatanging Community Engagement Photo para sa mga klase sa umaga at hapon. Ang Parangal ay igagawad sa Programa ng Pagtatapos ng mga Mag-aaral ng NSTP sa CSSP. 
Mayo 15
CSSP-NSTP : Naglilingkod sa KAPPwa, Naglilingkod sa Bayan! 
Programa ng Pagtatapos ng Mag-aaral ng NSTP sa CSSP 2016-2017
1/F Palma Hall Lobby
9 a.m. - 12 p.m. at 2-5 p.m.
Ito ay programa na naglalayong kilalanin ang matagumpay na pagtatapos ngayong Ikalawang Semestre Akademikong Taon 2016-2017 ng mga mag-aaral ng NSTP sa CSSP. Nakapaloob sa programa ang pagpapalabas ng video presentations tungkol sa paglilingkod-komunidad ng bawat klase; pagkilala at pasasalamat sa mga katuwang na organisasyon/komunidad sa pagsasakatuparan ng mga inisyatibo ng bawat klase sa CSSP-NSTP; paggagawad ng mga ispesyal na pagkilala sa mga klase; at ang mainit na pagtatanggap sa mga nagtapos bilang mga bagong kasapi ng National Service Reserve Corps. 
Mayo 17
Social Development : Defining the Terrain, Exploring Pathways 
2017 CSWCD Golden Anniversary Conference
Bulwagang Tandang Sora, College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD)
9 a.m.-5 p.m.
In line with the 50th Anniversary Celebration of the CSWCD, the Social Development Program is conducting a conference entitled "Social Development : Defining the Terrain, Exploring Pathways" to look at Social Development as an academic discipline. The Conference aims to provide academicians, practitioners, policy makers and students a venue for sharing, understanding and networking knowledge and commitment towards disciplinal enhancement of social development. 
Mayo 23
Bulawan : Konsiyerto para sa mga Iskolar ng Bayan
CSWCD Concert Series
Conspiracy Garden Cafe
Visayas Avenue, Quezon City
8:30-11:30 p.m.
Bulawan : Konsiyerto para sa mga Iskolar ng Bayan is a kick-off cultural program designed to raise funds for the talented and deserving scholars of CSWCD. TUBAW Music Collective and other rising bands from the UP Diliman will perform. The soft launch for the grand concert scheduled on October 2017 will start on May 19 through an online campaign that will feature its scholars and various support groups. 
Mayo 24
EEE Technology Fair 2017 
UP Professional Schools
Bonifacio Global City
8 a.m. - 6 p.m.
The UP Electrical and Electronics Engineering Institute is hosting the first EEE Technology Fair 2017, with the theme, "Enhanced Industry-Academe Collaboration to Support a Culture of Innovation." The event aims to engage state universities and colleges, government agencies and industry partners in advancing the Electrical, Electronics and Computer Engineering fields in the Philippines by setting the platform for collaboration. The Institute will showcase its research projects and will recognize the faculty, researchers and students in their efforts to make a difference for the Philippines. Invited inudstry partners and government agencies will also lay down the mechanisms for research collaboration and other forms of partnership. 
Mayo 24-28
Ika-10 Palihang Rogelio Sicat
Taunang Pambansang Palihan sa Malikhaing Pagsulat
Leyte Park Resort Hotel, Tacloban City
Ang taunang Pambansang Palihan sa Malikhaing Pagsulat na ito ay itinataguyod ng Departamento ng Filipino at Panitikan ng Pilipinas ng UP Diliman, sa pakikipagtulungan ng Opisina ng Pangulo ng UP, Opisina ng Tsanselor ng UP Diliman, UP Tacloban, Sentro ng Wikang Filipino, City Mayor's Office ng Tacloban City at ni Bb. Karla Estrada.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 3, Blg 5 (5 copies)
column7:
Hunyo 1- Agosto 10
A Man and His Relations
A retrospective exhibition on the life and works of national Artist for Visual Arts Cesar Legaspi
Main Hall, Bulwagan ng Dangal 
The exhibition will feature artworks of Legaspi from the University Art Collection, works of Legaspi's peers from the Thirteen Moderns and the neo-realists, and archival materials. Cesar Legaspi was a pioneer of the neo-realist genre in painting; a prominent member of the Thirteen Moderns and is widely known for his contributions in Philippine Modern Art. He was awarded National Artist for Visual Arts in 1990. Legaspi graduated from the UP College of Fine Arts in 1936. The Bulwagan ng Dangal UP Heritage Museum endeavors to introduce Legaspi's art to a younger generation of UP students. 
Hunyo 1-8
UPCFA Studio Arts Thesis Exhibition
UP College of Fine Arts
The Department of Studio Arts of the UP College of Fine Arts (CFA) will conduct a public viewing of thesis works by their graduating batch of students, ranging from paintings, sculptures, installation, video art and drawings. Thesis advisees of Professors Ambie Abaño, Benjamin Cabangis, Roberto Feleo, Marc Malto, Jonathan Olazo and Riza Romero will be exhibiting their art works at the UP Fine Arts Gallery (Corredor and Foyer), Amorsolo Hall, Theory Rooms, Bathalaman and RGEP Rooms. 
Hunyo 1 at 14; Hulyo 4
Gender Sensitivity Training 
Para sa mga guro, REPS, kawaning administratibo at kawaning agency-hired ng UP Diliman
Seminar Room, Diliman Gender Office (DGO)
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Bilang pagpapatibay at pagtiyak na ang UP Diliman ay nananatiling makataong Kampus na may hustisyang pangkasarian, malaya sa diskriminasyon at pang-aabuso, isinasagawa ang Gender Sensitivity Training (GST) para sa mga guro, REPS (Research, Extension and Professional Staff), kawaning administratibo, mga security guard at janitorial workers. Bukas ito para sa mga empleyadong permanente, UP contractual at non-UP contractual. 
Hunyo 2
UP COE Undergraduate Project Competition 2017
Showcasing UPD engineering's best undergraduate research projects
Melchor Hall, UP College of Engineering Main lobby (demonstrations and exhibits) 5th floor, P&G Room (presentations and judging) 2-2:30 p.m. (demonstrations and exhibits) 2:30-5 p.m. (presentations and judging) Senior undergraduate students compete and vie for this year's best engineering undergraduate research project, as distinguished judges from the private sector, government, industry and academia share their expertise in deciding who deserves the grand prize of P50,000. 
Hunyo 6-31
LangueLounge : Forgetory by Jose Tence Ruiz
Atelyer, Bulwagan ng Dangal, Main Library
The exhibit is a site-specific installation that is a meditation on the cult of glib and overwhelming information over truth. It works with image and meaning in relation to contemporary life and society. High backed chairs lined with red velvet are related to the image of high and honorable status, of luxury and comfort that is desired and possessed. Upon closer inspection, these honorable chairs turn out to be electric chairs, and what seems to be an opulent and elegant thing is an object of torture. The work calls for closer scrutiny on what is seen and heard, an encouragement to move out of places of comfort, conformity and complacency. LangueLounge : Forgetory was initially made for and exhibited at the ARTFair Philippines 2017 and was subsequently installed at the Far Eastern University as a center piece in Hudyat, an exhibition decrying the state of extra judicial killings and asserting the principle of human dignity. This third installation is a new iteration from previous exhibitions. 
Hunyo 7
A new look at Philippine export performance : a firm-level approach 
Rm. 303, Encarnacion Hall, School of Economics 4-5 p.m.
This paper introduces a rich and novel database of the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) containing the universe of export and import transactions of all firms from 1991-2012, merged with all the manufacturing firm surveys since 1996. A new set of stylized facts is presented, pertaining to the drivers of export growth and patterns of trade, and including the dimensions of geographical location, size and firm ownership in the analysis. New firm typologies are developed, as the behaviour of individual exporting and importing firms can now be traced not only in terms of the entry, exit and survival, but also re-entry, permanent exit, and continuous or resilient survival. 
Hunyo 16-Hulyo 7
Once More
Corredor, UP Fine Arts Gallery, Bartlett Hall 
6 p.m. (Opening)
The exhibition brings together a batch of UP College of Fine Arts alumni from two decades ago, whose members have gone on to pursue full or part time art practice, education and other related fields. Participating artists are Kaye Anne Alba, Carina Altomonte, Erin Asuncion-Dionisio, Billy Atienza, Martin Caoile, Kathy Cuisia-Wu, Kim Dacanay-Mendoza, Patricia Perez Eustaquio, Pusongbughaw, Otto Hernandez, Jonathan Galicano, Philipp Ines, Lisa Ito, Kim Landicho, Liby Limoso, Lea Zoraina Lim, Elena Malgapo-Domingo, Josue Mangrobang Jr., Robby Mananquil, Winston Martinez, Jerry Morada, Bon Mujeres, Maria Rita Cecilia Pangilinan-Arenas, Chris Paulmino, Jaspher Penuliar, Eufemio Rasco IV, Alan Rapsing, Li-anne Roa-Patricio, Ria Suaverdez-Romero, Jiyas Suministrado-Morales, Riza Romero, Dave Salazar, Chelle Serrano-Tagle, Brian Sergio, Rodel Tapaya, Maan Villanueva dn MM Yu. 
Hunyo 19
[Linguistics Brown Bag] HOKAGLISH : The New Language of the Filipino-Chinese
AVR (PH 207)
College of Social Sciences and Philosophy 
9-11 a.m.
Wilkinson Gonzales, MA Candidate of the National University of Singapore will talk about the unique linguistic features of Hokaglish, i.e., Hokkien, Tagalog and English, also referred to as Salamstam (-oe) 'mixed speech' or Lanang (-oe) 'our people speech', a post-colonial Philippine contact language spoken generally in Metro Manila by local-born ethnic Chinese. 
Hunyo 25
Ika-106 Pangkalahatang Pagtatapos 
Ampiteatro ng Unibersidad
7 a.m.
Sa taunang Pangkalahatang Pagtatapos, iginagawad ng Unibersidad ang titulo sa mga mag-aaral na nagtapos ng kani-kanilang programa sa di-gradwado at gradwadong antas. Ito ay iba pa sa mga pagtatapos na ginagawa ng bawat kolehiyo o linangan ng UP Diliman. Ito ang ikatlong pagkakataon na gagawin ang Pagtatapos sa buwan ng Hunyo mula ng ilipat ang simula ng pasukan sa Agosto noong 2014. 
Hunyo 29
'Walking Korea' by Arirang Yurangdan
Multimedia Room (PH 400)
College of Social Sciences and Philosophy
1-5 p.m.
The workshop brings Arirang Yurangdan's concept of 'Walking Korea' which showcases Korean culture such as the Korean traditional music Gukak, calligraphy and traditional dance. As the Korean Culture Diplomatic Delegation, they travel around the world to share their culture and discover other cultures.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 3, Blg 6 (3 copies)
column7:
Hulyo 1-7
Tubig at Kalikasan : 6th Solo Exhibit of Prof. Johnny T. Reynoso
Foyer, UP Fine Arts Gallery 
Bartlett Hall
This 6th solo exhibition of UP College of Fine Arts (UPCFA) faculty member and photographer prof. Johnny T. Reynoso features his recent series of color photographs portraying the theme of water and nature : underscoring the connections and harmony between this vital element and the natural life that surrounds us. Reynoso's body of work often highlights the presence of nature and its vivid colors. This evident in his two coffee table books featuring his portfolio of photographs, titled "Colors of Nature, Water of Life" and "In Retrospect : Water of Life 2" released in 2014 and 2016, respectively. Reynoso is an Associate Professor at the UPCFA Department of Visual Communication. He started teaching in the college in 1987 on courses in Photography, Visual Design, Production Methods, Editorial Design, Graphic Workshop, Screenprinting, Research Methods and Graphic Thesis. 
Hulyo 3-7
9th Training Course on Road Safety Audit
National Center for Transportation Studies (NCTS)
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The training, organized by NCTS, will use existing course materials and resources to maximize hands-on experiences and ensure internalization and retention of the concepts being presented. It provides an overview on principles and fundamentals of road safety, statistics/trends, causes and factors of crashes, road safety audit concepts, accident analysis and various road safety facilities. Basics of geometric design of highway sections and their applications together with intersection design elements are also presented. Target participants are municipal/city planners, municipal/city engineers, traffic engineers, transportation planners, and local and national government personnel involved in transportation and related functions. 
Hulyo 4-27
(tuwing Martes, Huwebes at Sabado)
Gender Sensitivity Training
Seminar Room, Diliman Gender Office 
Benton Hall
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Bilang pagpapatibay at pagtiyak na ang UP Diliman ay nananatiling makataong kampus na may hustisyang pangkasarian, malaya sa diskriminasyon at pang-aabuso, isinasagawa ang Gender Sensitivity Training (GST) para sa mga guro, REPS, kawaning administratibo, mga security at janitorial personnel. Bukas ito para sa mga empleyadong permanente, UP contractual at non-UP contractual. 
Hulyo 4-15
(Martes, Huwebes at Sabado)
Basic Vector Graphics Composition Workshop
Bartlett Hall
UP College of Fine Arts (UPCFA)
1-5 p.m.
The UPCFA will be opening slots for a 6-day workshop in Basic Vector Graphics Composition, to be facilitated by Studio Arts professor Riza Romero. Workshop schedules will be from 1 to 5 p.m. on July 4, 6, 8, 11, 13, and 15 (Tuesday, Thursdays and Saturdays). Vector graphics composition uses geometric primitives such as points, lines, curves and polygons based on mathematics to represent images in computer graphics. The workshop aims to introduce participants to vector-based graphic environment to give them an understanding of how mathematical equations are used to create images. 
Hulyo 5
Freshmen Orientation Program 2017 : Breed of new blood 
UP Film Center
12-4 p.m.
Parent's Orientation Program : a pledge of support and allegiance
School of Economics Auditorium
12 - 4 p.m.
These programs aim to orient the freshmen and their parents with various university programs and services. 
Hulyo 8-22
(tuwing Sabado)
Drawing and Painting Workshops
Bartlett Hall
UP College of Fine Arts (UPCFA)
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Facilitated by Prof. Jamel Obnamia, the 3-day workshop emphasizes basic drawing skills in the student's choice of painting media (watercolor on paper, acrylic on canvas or oil on canvas). 
Hulyo 15
Call for papers for the International Conference Marking the Centennial of Philippine Anthropology
The conference aims to generate critical discussions that focus on institutional memories, historiography and analyses of issues and concerns in research, teaching and practice of anthropology in the Philippines in the last 100 years. Students, researchers and practitioners in anthropology and related disciplines are invited to submit paper and panel proposals that cover any of the following themes : Theory, Practice, Pedagogy and Ethics in Philippine Anthropology; Institutional Memories and Anthropological Traditions; Ancestors of Philippine Anthropology; Identity, Culture, Folklore and Heritage; Anthropology and Philippine Society; and New Directions for Philippine Anthropology. 
Hulyo 21
Call for papers for the 2nd Colleges and Universities Public Service Conference
The conference aims to provide a platform to effectively harness teaching and research for extension and public service, to provide a venue for scholarly discussions on lessons and best practices for continuous improvement, and to document innovative initiatives that highlight higher education institutions' social responsibility and compassion. The conference is open to all disciplines with community engagements and public service initiatives. Programs with themes like service learning; student engagement; public service monitoring and reporting systems; knowledge-driven volunteerism; linkages of the university with communities, civil society, industries and government; and impact measurements of engagements are very welcome. 
Hulyo 23-Agosto 6
(tuwing Linggo)
Basic or Advanced Drawing and Painting Workshop
Bartlett Hall 
UP College of Fine Arts (UPCFA)
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
Facilitated by Prof. Yasmin Almonte, the 3-day workshop offers participants the chance to hone their skills in basic drawing and their choice of painting formats (portrait, still life, landscape, figure drawing or abstract art). Almonte is a summa cum laude graduate of the Universitgy of Northern Iowa's Master of Arts degree program and a magna cum laude graduate of the UPCFA's Painting program. 
Hulyo 31
Call for nomination for the 2nd Gawad Pangulo : Award for Excellence in Public Service
The Award seeks to recognize outstanding public service initiatives of various UP System units that exhibit compassion (malasakit) to the different publics that they serve. Nominations should belong to the five clusters : Arts and Letters and Communications; Science and Technology; Social Sciences, Management, and Law; Health and Allied Sciences; and Agriculture, Forestry, Fishery and Natural Resources Management.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 3, Blg 7 (4 copies)
column7:
Agosto 7
Freshman Welcome Assembly 
University Theater
1-5 p.m.
The program aims to welcome UPD freshmen by introducing them to their respective Deans as well as University Officials. 
Agosto 14-18
19th Traffic Administration Course
National Center for Transportation Studies Bldg. 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The training gives an overview of what transportation planning is all about and its interaction with land use, introduction to traffic engineering and management. Basics of traffic impact assessment including rationale required analysis and components of the transportation system are also presented. Different traffic measures employed locally and internationally will be discussed as well as basic traffic safety information and education programs. Field work activity including data collection will be experienced by the participants for a more hands-on learning. Participants will also understand specific institutional issues and the possible legal arrangements in setting up the traffic regulatory unit including laws related therein. 
Agosto 15 - Setyembre 15
Call for papers : Bodies in/and Asian Theatres
The Joint 2018 Asian Theatre Working Group Colloquium and International Federation for Theatre Research Regional Conference (IFTR-Asia) attempts to answer these general questions : What do we mean when we talk about bodies in Asian theatres and performances? What do we mean when we talk about Asian bodies in different performances outside the region? How does theatre affect the way we think about the bodies of Asians?
Agosto 17-18
Sinelaysay : A Documentary Film Showcase
Room 400, Palma Hall
10 a.m. - 4 p.m.
For six years now, the Third World Studies Center (TWSC) of the College of Social Sciences and Philosophy, UP Diliman and the Department of Political Science of Universite de Montreal produced documentaries that tell the stories of the lives and struggles of Filipinos at the unmarked margins of society. This year's showcase will premiere two new documentaries. "Leaving Bodeng" documents the last days of the about-to-be-evicted occupants of the iconic White Building in Phnom Penh, while "Pag-baylo : Losing the Land to Solar Farms" brings to light the deft maneuvers by big landowners to convert vast tracts of lands that are meant for distribution to its tenants into solar farms. 
Agosto 18
Work-Life Balance IV
Seminar Rm. 
UP Diliman Gender Office (UPDGO)
9 a.m. - 4 p.m.
Layunin ng Work - Life Balance IV na itaguyod ang malay sa kasariang talastasan sa pamamagitan ng mga impormal na lunan. Nag-oorganisa ang UPDGO ng mga aktibidad na naglalayong magbahagi ng kaalaman at kamulatan tungkol sa iba't ibang isyung pangkasarian. May libreng talakayan, sine, hilot at meryenda para sa mga kawani, REPS, guro at mag-aaral ng UPD. 
Agosto 18
Department of Linguistics Research Colloquium
Palma Hall Audio - Visual Room (Rm. 207) 
9 a.m. [Undergraduate papers]
1 p.m. [Graduate papers]
The annual Research Colloquium serves as a venue for presenting findings of recent research projects done by graduate and undergraduate students of Linguistics to the general public, in order to forge collaborations among students, faculty and scholars of linguistics, language studies, as well as other related disciplines. 
Agosto 18 - Setyembre 1
Dissident Vicinities
Main Hall and Atelyer
Bulwagan ng Dangal
4 p.m.
The exhibition touches on how cultural work and production contributed to various arenas of struggle, from traditional venues to subaltern zone. It features works by Karl Castro, Nathalie Dagmang, Boy Dominguez and Leonilo Doloricon in collaboration with Tom Estrera, Voltaire Guray, Renz Lee, Henrielle Pagkaliwangan, Melvin Pollero, Iggy Rodriguez, Sining Lila, Aldrein Silanga, Pablo Baen Santos, Archie Oclos, Renan Ortiz, UGATLahi Artists Collective and Bagong Alyansang Makabayan. The exhibition is part of Condition Report, a collaborative art project with emerging young curators from Japan and Southeast Asia, and is supported by the Japan Foundation Asia Center. Responding to the theme "What is Southeast Asia?", the project consists of four collaborative exhibitions between January to March 2017 in Manila, Jakarta, Kuala Lumpur and Bangkok and 14 local exhibitions in 12 cities for the second half of 2017. Lisa Ito-Tapang curates the exhibit. 
Agosto 22
Nuclear Energy Forum
Considerations and Essential Information
Novotel, Cubao, Quezon City
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The forum will discuss the plan of the present Philippine government to adopt or consider nuclear energy as potential solution in providing sustainable electric energy in the country. Its advantages such as the relatively competitive levelized cost of energy, low greenhouse gas and criteria air pollution footprint, and base load contribution will be presented. Concerns in using this energy source such as the complexity of the technology, multiple dimensions to be copnsidered, i.e. safety, security, safeguard as well as management of radioactive waste will also be tackled. The forum is organized by the National Engineering Center, the Engineering Research and Development for Technology (ERDT) and the UP Engineering Research and Development Foundation, Inc. (UPERDFI).
Agosto 22
Special Lecture : Dr. Naonori Nagaya 
Palma Hall Audio-Visual Room (Rm. 207)
10-11:30 a.m. The event is free and open to the public. 
Agosto 22-25
Public Administration in Changing Times : New Norms and Sustainable Reform
Marco Polo Hotel, Ortigas Avenue, Pasig City
In celebration of the 65th anniversary of the National College of Public Administration and Governance, the College is holding an international conference on global issues, such as climate change, fiscal crises, human rights violations, international crimes, growing influence of supra-national institutions, disruptive technology, 'populist nationalism' and changes in regional dynamics (i.e. Brexit), among others. 
Agosto 24-25
Mga Susing Salita : Pambansang Seminar sa Pagbuo ng Diskurso sa Konseptong Filipino
Awditoryum ng Kolehiyo ng Edukasyon 
8 a.m.
Bilang bahagi ng pagdiriwang ng Buwan ng Wika 2017 sa UP Diliman (UPD), malugod na iniimbitahan ang mga guro, mag-aaral, manunulat at mananaliksik na dumalo at makiisa sa pambansang seminar. Sa pangunguna ito ng Sentro ng Wikang Filipino - UPD at pakikpagtulungan ng UP Departamento ng Filipino at Panitikan ng Pilipinas ng Kolehiyo ng Arte at Literatura, UP Departamento ng Linggwistiks ng Kolehiyo ng Agham Panlipunan at Pilosopiya, at UP Dibisyon ng Araling Wika ng Kolehiyo ng Edukasyon. Ito ang kauna-unahang pagkakataon para sa isang pambansang seminar na magtatampok sa pagbuo ng diskurso na hango sa konseptong Filipino. Gamit ang isang susing salita sa anumang wika sa Pilipinas, layunin ng seminar na paunlarin ang inisyal na mga ideya at konseptong nakapaloob sa piniling salita. 
Agosto 24
The Linguist Talks : Dr. Viveca V. Hernandez
Palma Hall Audio-Visual Room (Rm. 207) 
9 a.m. 
This brownbag session highlights the experiences of one of the pillars of the UP Department of Linguistics, Dr. Viveca V. Hernandez, as a fieldworker in various ethnolinguistic communities around the Philippines. Dr. Hernandez will share the issues, challenges, and rewards of actual field activities done for linguistics and cultural studies purposes, for the benefit of the future generation of field researchers. 
Agosto 24
Nanay talks
Palma Hall Audio-Visual Room (Rm. 207)
1-4 p.m.
This event invited scholars from the Department of Linguistics and the Department of Anthropology, who are also mothers, to share their experiences in rearing their child during their developmental and formative years. This roundtable discussion aims to gain insights into the language acquisition and learning of children, from the perspective of linguist/anthropologist-mothers. 
Agosto 25
CSWCD Grand 50th Anniversary
College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD)
9 a.m.-5 p.m.
The celebration of CSWCD's Grand 50th Anniversary will have a number of activities like Bicycle Campus Tour (9-10:30 a.m.), launching of Philippine Journal of Social Development 2017 Volume 9 (11 a.m.-12 p.m.), Lunch (12-1 p.m.) and Fellowship Time : songs, dances, games, and raffle (1-4 p.m.)
Agosto 26
CSWCD @50 Grand Reunion and Alumni Homecoming 
CSWCD Grounds
3:30 - 10 p.m.
Join the College of Social Work and Community Development (CSWCD) in a night of song, dance and memories as they recall the 50 years of CSWCD in a Grand Reunion and Alumni Homecoming. 
The College will recognize its Jubilarians and other graduates, and present the premiere of the CSWCD @50 video. 
Agosto 28
Linguistics in Movies
Palma Hall Audio-Visual Room (Rm. 207)
9 a.m. - 3 p.m.
A public screening of movies that highlight themes related to languages and linguistics. The event also serves as a venue for movie enthusiasts and linguistics and language studies scholars in order to engage in discussions on their disciplines and how they are relevant to and manifested in popular culture. 
Agosto 29
Peminismong Babaylan
Nilalayon ng porum na talakayin ang lakas at kapangyarihan ng kababaihan sa perspektibo ng kalinangang Pilipino. Iniuugat sa mga babaylan ang taal na kapangyarihan ng mga Pilipina at ang mataas na pagpapahalaga sa kababaihan sa taal na pamayanang Pilipino. Tampok ang tagapagsalita na si Prop. Ma. Fe Buenaventura-Mangahas. 
Agosto 30
Notes from the Field
Palma Hall Audio-Visual Room (Rm. 207)
9 a.m.
In this symposium, students of the recently conducted linguistic fieldwork will share their experiences in undertaking field activities for social science research. The presenters will present the research background, problems encountered and handled, and other pertinent notes in doing field research in their assigned sites outside the campus. 
Agosto 30
Linguistic Mapping and Language Documentation
Palma Hall Audio-Visual Room (Rm. 207)
This event presents the final outputs of the students of Linguistics 125 (Introduction to Field Methods) and Linguistics 121 (Linguistic Survey of the Philippines), under the supervision of faculty members of the Department of Lingustics, in collaboration with the Department of Geography. This serves as a platform where the presenters get to share with the public the results of their research on field activities and linguistic surveys through the soft launching of an interactive website where the visitors would be able to check out the languages spoken in specific localities in the country, alongside a brief profile of each of these languages. 
Agosto 31
