Asian Culture Fest : Writing Systems
Room 400, Palma Hall
10 a.m. - 4 p.m. 
The festival showcases the different writing systems of selected Asian languages in order to gain appreciation of how linguistic forms are expressed in a system other than the more commonly used Roman letters. This forum/workshop invites Asian language students, teachers and enthusiasts to experience the traditional and contemporary writing methods and techniques in the respective languages.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 3, Blg 8
column7:
Setyembre 2, 9 at 16
Drawing and Painting Workshop
Bartlett Hall, College of Fine Arts
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Learn the basic or advance techniques of drawing and painting landscapes, still life, portraiture, figure and abstracts through the study of the elements of art and principles of design. Prof. Yasmin Almonte, a summa cum laude graduate of the University of Northern Iowa's Master of Arts degree program and a magna cum laude graduate of the UP College of Fine Arts' Painting program, facilitates the workshop. 
Setyembre 2-23
Rituals of Invasion and Resistance / Norberto Roldan
Vargas Museum, Ground Floor (GF) Galleries
6-9 p.m. Opening Reception
UP Vargas Museum, in cooperation with Silverlens, opens Norberto Roldan's exhibition titled "Rituals of Invasion and Resistance" at the GF Galleries of the museum. Roldan engages viewers with his past installations that reflect on belief systems, that are either religious, political or personal. 
Setyembre 2-23
The Arrow of Time in the Heart of the Sun / Ian Fabro
Vargas Museum, 3F Galleries
6-9 p.m. Opening Reception
The exhibit focuses on Ian Fabro's skillful appropriation of various media to convey the intricacies of abstraction and the depiction of the human form. The bloodstained body and the suffering flesh hint at the internal struggle of humanity and the triumph over evil. The human is pinned downed literally and figuratively, awaiting redemption and enlightenment.
Setyembre 3, 10 at 17
Drawing and Painting Workshop
Bartlett Hall, College of Fine Arts
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Learn the basic or advance techniques of drawing and painting landscapes, still life, portraiture, figure and abstracts through the study of the elements of art and principles of design. Prof. Yasmin Almonte, a summa cum laude graduate of the University of Northern Iowa's Master of Arts degree program and a magna cum laude graduate of the UP College of Fine Arts' Painting program, facilitates the workshop. 
Setyembre 4 - 25
(tuwing Lunes)
Gender Sensitivity Training
Seminar Room, UP Diliman Gender Office
1-5 p.m.
Bilang pagpapatibay at pagtiyak na ang UP Diliman ay nananatiling makataong kampus na may hustisyang pangkasarian, malaya sa diskriminasyon at pang-aabuso, isinasagawa ang Gender Sensitivity Training (GST) para sa mga mag-aaral ng UP Diliman bilang bahagi ng organization accreditation sa taong 2017.
Setyembre 9, 16 at 23
Drawing and Painting Workshop for Beginners
Bartlett Hall, College of Fine Arts 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
Facilitated by Asst. Prof. Jamel Joseph A. Obnamia, the 3-day workshop emphasizes basic drawing skills in the student's choice of painting media (watercolor on paper, acrylic on canvas or oil on canvas).
September 11 at 18
Lakad Gunita
First Quarter Storm and Diliman Commune Memory Walk in UP Diliman
Commune Memory Walk in UP Diliman
Palma Hall (former AS) Lobby
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The First Quarter Storm of 1970 and the Diliman Commune of 1971 occured as a reaction to the worsening economic conditions of the time. These events displayed the response of a dissatisfied people who chose activism and militancy amidst threats of political persecution from the Marcos administration. Looking at the narrative and legacy of these events is crucial in producing a better understanding of the historical circumstances that ultimately led to the declaration of Martial Law in 1972. To celebrate the legacy of these student-led protests, the UP Department of History will conduct a memory walk which will revisit the key landmarks of activism within the UP Diliman campus. This is in cooperation with the College of Social Sciences and Philosophy (CSSP) and the Office for Initiatives in Culture and the Arts (OICA). 
Setyembre 19-22
Training on Participatory Monitoring and Evaluation of Development Projects
Seminar Room, College of Social Work and Community Development
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The training course aims to enhance the understanding capabilities of participants in developing appropriate participatory monitoring and evaluation systems for their respective community projects and programs. The training is initiated and organized by the Research and Extension for Development Office of the UP College of Social Work and Community Development. 
Setyembre 26-28
Batas Militar 2017 : Mga Hamon sa Demokrasya
A seminar-workshop for teachers in basic and higher education institutions
National Institute for Science and Mathematics Education Development (NISMED) Auditorium
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
In commemoration of the declaration of Martial Law in September 1972, the UP College of Social Sciences and Philosophy (CSSP) will hold a seminar-workshop aimed at helping improve the teaching of history, social studies and the social sciences (particularly on the issue of martial law), in various levels of education. This is in cooperation with the History Departments of Ateneo de Manila University and De La Salle University, with the support of the National Historical Commission of the Philippines and the Office for Initiatives in Culture and the Arts (OICA) of UP Diliman. 
Setyembre 26-29
2nd Emission Inventory for Smaller Cities Training 
National Center for Transportation Studies (NCTS) Building 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The training aims to identify the emission sources in the participants' respective cities; develop an understanding of how computation of emission loading from different sources is conducted; and design a work plan for the establishment of emission inventories in their city.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 3, Blg 11 (6 copies)
column7:
Disyembre 1-2
UP Anthropology @ 100 : International Conference Marking the Centennial of Philippine Anthropology
UP National Institute for Science and Mathematics Education Development (NISMED) 
8 a.m.
The Conference marks the culmination of the 3-year celebration of the UP Deparment of Anthropology of its 100 years of existence beginning in 1914 when general anthropology was taught at UP. In 1917, one of the earliest departments of anthropology in Asia was established in the country with Professor Henry Otley Beyer as the founding Chair. 
Although special attention is given to the institutional memory of the UP Department of Anthropology, the conference also brings together the various narratives of anthropological institutions in the country and presents different researches on the Philippines. The Conference celebrates, documents and interprets the legacy of Philippine anthropology cutting across geographies, academic traditions, personalities, and historical periods. 
Disyembre 1
Session Avenue
Kick off celebration of Philippine radio's 100th year 
Carillon Plaza
7 p.m.
Isang on-ground music at cultural event na kung saan magsasama-sama ang mga home grown talent ng UP. Itatampok ang iba't ibang indie bands, solo artists, spoken word artists, manunula at iba pa. Magkakaroon ng open mic jamming session kung saan maari ring magtanghal ang mga tagapanood. 
Disyembre 2
Parangal sa mga Guro at Kawani ng UPIS
UPIS Gymnasium
7 a.m. - 2 p.m.
The event is a day of honoring and fellowship between the past and present UP Integrated School (UPIS) faculty, staff, students and alumni. This year, aside from the thanksgiving program and the special tribute for the retired UPIS faculty members, there will be a wellness fair that features free consultations with medical practitioners, nutrition consultants, wellness experts, massage services, and other health and wellness booths. 
Disyembre 4-5
Gender Sensitivity Training para sa OVCAA
Seminar Room
UP Diliman Gender Office (UPDGO)
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Nagpapatuloy ang Gender Sensitivity Training (GST) ng Office of the Vice Chancellor for Academic Affairs ngayong Disyembre bilang pagtalima sa Memorandum No. MLT-17-188 na inilabas ng Opisina ng Tsanselor hinggil sa GST. Tatalakayin sa GST ang ibayong pagsulong ng karapatan at kagalingan ng lahat ng kasarian, at ang oryentasyon ng bagong Anti-Sexual Harassment Code ng Unibersidad ng Pilipinas. 
Disyembre 4-5
Problems and Opportunities in Achieving Competitiveness 
A series of talks reflecting on the NIMBB curriculum for the next 30 years
NIMBB, National Science Complex
Disyembre 4, 9:30 a.m. - 12 p.m.
Disyembre 5, 11:30 a.m. - 4 p.m.
The National Institute of Molecular Biology and Biotechnology (NIMBB) has been producing excellent talent over the last 30 years. What lies ahead for the next 30 years? This series of talks aims to identify the key strengths, weaknesses, opportunities and threats that could inform a new curriculum. Invited speakers include Dr. William Padolina, President of the National Academy of Science and Technology, Dr. Luis Sison of the UP Technology Transfer and Business Development Office; and Dr. Gonzalo Serafica, Entrepreneur and Consultant to the United States Agency for International Development - Science, Technology, Research and Innovation for Development (USAID STRIDE). 
Disyembre 5-8
Mandatory Continuing Legal Education (MCLE)
UP Law Center
8 a.m. 
Continuing legal education is required of members of the Integrated Bar of the Philippines (IBP) to ensure that throughout their career they keep abreast with law and jurisprudence, maintain the ethics of the profession and enhance the standards of the practice of law. The UP Institute for the Administration of Justice actually formulates and conducts MCLE for IBP members to hone their professional competence. 
Disyembre 5
The Rohingya Conundrum : Its Significance for International Studies
Seminar Room, UP Asian Center
2-4 p.m. 
This lecture will focus on three fundamental issues : the Rohingya conundrum, possible solutions and its implications in international studies. The conflict between Rohingya, a Muslim minority in Rakhine state in western Myanmar (Burma), and the Rakhine Buddhists has been an international concern since 2012 when violence first erupted in recent years. The protracted violence is a threat not only to Myanmar's internal peace and stability but also for the country's relations with the international community, particularly its immediate neighbor Bangladesh and the Muslim-majority countries in Southeast Asia, such as Malaysia and Indonesia, that have expressed concerns over the Rohingyas' treatment in the past. 
Disyembre 6-8
Module D : Predictive Analysis
National Engineering Center (NEC)
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
This course surveys the different algorithms for data mining specifically : classification, regression, feature selection, association and clustering algorithms for business analytics. Learn to utilize a data warehouse and the various data mining algorithms to aid in business decisions. Learn concepts such as problem types, data structure requirements, over and under fitting, and how to evaluate a business analytics model. Advantages and disadvantages of methods are discussed. 
Disyembre 6-8
15th Asian Symposium on Medicinal Plants, Spices and Other Natural Products 22nd Natural Products Society of the Philippines Convention
Intel Center of Science Innovation 
UP National Institute of Physics
8 a.m.
ASOMPS (Asian Symposium on Medicinal Plants and Spices) is a scientific meeting which aims to strengthen and promote collaboration and cooperation between Asian  scientists in the fields of chemistry, pharmacology, pharmacy, biochemistry, botany and biotechnology of natural products to improve regional research potential and infrastructure in the region. The first symposium (ASOMPS I) was held in Peshawar, Pakistan in 1960. It was in ASOMPS VII held in Manila in 1992 that the conference title was modified to Asian Symposium on Medicinal Plants, Spices and Other Natural Products which recognized the extent of biodiversity from which natural products can be further developed. 
Disyembre 6-8
ASEANnale 2017 : 1st Film and Multimedia Competition and Exhibition
2nd International Symposium on ASEAN Studies
UP Asian Center
The competition encourages ASEAN higher education students to produce creative works in film and multimedia that are the narratives of their culture and personal experiences. The competition also gives honor to filmmakers and multimedia producers whose creative storytelling explores a range of human experiences that echoes the festival's theme "Capturing the spirit of ASEAN in the Digital Times." There are three sub-themes for the film and multimedia competition - Diaspora, Democracy and Disaster. 
Disyembre 7-9
Unpacking Asia : Emerging Issues in Politics, Security, and Foreign Policy
A forum and exhibit
Japan Hall, UP Asian Center
1-8:30 p.m. (forum, Dec. 7) 
8 a.m. - 6:30 p.m. (exhibit, Dec 8-9)
The forum aims to promote awareness of Asian politics, and build constructive and informed dialogue on the various issues, perspectives, and methods used by states, security practitioners and diplomats in dealing with emerging challenges of the region. Twenty-one graduate students of Prof. Aileen Baviera will present their research findings via posters. The poster presentations are grouped into three panels : Security issues in the Asia-Pacific; International Relations of Southeast Asia; and Emerging Issues in Asian International Relations. Various facets of ASEAN and China's expanding role in Southeast Asia will be tackled, as well as the role of digital technology and cyber security in the region's domestic and international politics. 
Disyembre 7-8
Handel's Messiah
Abelardo Hall Auditorium
6:30 p.m. 
The UP College of Music presents George Frideric Handel's "Messiah."
The annual concert features UP Concert Chorus, UP Singing Ambassadors, UP ARCO, UP Cherubim and Seraphim, UP Staff Choral Society, Dawani Women's Choir, Musika Sophia, Afro Latin Ensemble, UP Orchestra, Villancico Vocal Ensemble, MUPC 1-10 Chorus Classes of Prof. Janet Aracama and Dr. Beverly Shangkuan Cheng. Featured soloists are : Ervin Lumauag, tenor; Avelino Guande, Baritone; Stefanie Quintin, soprano; and Kyle Tingzon, countertenor..
Disyembre 8
Work-Life Balance V (WLB V)
Conference Room, UP Diliman Gender Office (UPDGO)/Office of Anti-Sexual Harassment 
9 a.m. - 4 p.m. 
Ang gawaing ito ay bilang pakikiisa sa 18-Day Campaign Against Violence Against Women. Layunin ng WLB V na itaguyod ang malay sa kasariang talastasan sa pamamagitan ng mga impormal na lunan para sa kaalaman at kamulatan tungkol sa iba't ibang isyung pangkasarian. Magkakaroon ng libreng hilot, meryenda at talakayan para sa mga mag-aaral, guro, kawani at REPS ng UPD. 
Disyembre 8
Big Data for Development (BD4D)
A public lecture by Sriganesh Lokanathan
Case Room, UP National College of Public Administration and Governance (NCPAG)
2-4 p.m. 
Lokanathan will talk about the potentials and challenges of big data for development, sharing examples from LIRNEasia's researches in Sri Lanka and Bangladesh. He will cover a range of topics like modelling of disease propagation, transportation and traffic, socio-economic activity and wellbeing, land use mapping, data access, privacy, marginalization, capacity requirements, and government engagement. The event is a joint initiative of LIRNEasia and the Center for Local and Regional Governance of UP NCPAG.
Disyembre 8 - Enero 27, 2018
Place of Region in the Contemporary 
Initial project of the Philippine Contemporary Art Network (PCAN)
UP Vargas Museum
6 p.m. (Opening reception) 
UP Vargas Museum opens the first project of the Philippine Contemporary Art Network built through three nodes in a network : Knowledge Production and Circulation; Exhibition and Curatorial Analysis; and Public Engagerment and Artistic Formation. The project endeavors to activate a network to coordinate a range of interventions in contemporary art in the Philippines and to cast a sharper profile for it on an inter-local and trans-regional scale. Place of Region in the Contemporary was made possible through the support of Sen. Loren Legarda. 
Disyembre 9
UPIS General Alumni Homecoming 2017
UPIS High School Grounds
3 p.m. - 12 a.m. (Dec. 10)
The University of the Philippines Integrated School (UPIS) will be commemorating its 101st anniversary as the unified institution of the UP High School founded in 1916, the UP Elementary School in 1936 and the UP Preparatory School in 1954. The 101st Grand Alumni Homecoming will welcome back to the institution more than 1,000 alumni who will be treated to a fun celebration night led by the Golden Jubilarians - UP High and UP Prep Class of 1967 and hosted by Silver Jubilarians - UPIS Class of 1992. 
Disyembre 15
"UP Diliman : Paaralan, Palaruan"
UPD Parada ng mga Parol 2017
Academic Oval 
5 p.m.
Paligsahan ito ng mga pinakamagandang parol ng mga administratibo at akademikong yunit ayon sa temang "UP Diliman : Paaralan, Palaruan." Ipaparada ang mga parol paikot sa Academic Oval at magtatapos sa isang palatuntunan sa Amphitheater. Ito rin ay tatampukan ng fireworks display. 
Disyembre 15
Maskipaps : The Crossover
UP Sunken Garden
9 p.m.
Isang pangkomunidad na pagdiriwang ng UP sa pangunguna ng Beta Epsilon na gaganapin pagkatapos ng Lantern Parade. Magtatanghal sa nasabing music festival ang iba't ibang banda at mga disk jockey.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 4, Blg 1
column7:
Pebrero 1, 8, 15 & 22
Taludturan : Poetry of the Body
Palma Hall Lobby, Melchor Hall Lobby, Shopping Center, CMC Lobby
3 p.m.
Taludturan : Poetry of the Body is a 4-part public performance project which includes dance, movement and performance pieces lasting for around 10 minutes in selected venues around the UP Campus every Thursday of February. Each part explores the embodiment of the encounter and engagement with beauty, love, desire, revolt and death. It will be part of Kat(h)awan : UP Diliman Festival of Culture and the Arts 2018. The title "taludturan" means "stanza" specifically of a poem but it also evokes the craft of verse-making, recitation (turan) and movement. Indeed, some of the performances were inspired by poetic texts about the body. The performance, however, need not be secondary to a text as it can be the act of writing the poem and the poem itself. 
Pebrero 1
Guangxi (Connections)
Abelardo Hall Auditorium
7:30 p.m.
As the final activity for the commemoration of National Artist for Music Jose Maceda's 100th birth year/centenary, a special concert highlighting Maceda's three works that cover his whole career as a composer will be staged. The closing concert will involve around 80 musicians from the UP College of Music and Guangxi Arts University Traditional Orchestra of Taiwan/China to present Maceda's music and learn from one another about the various musical traditions of Asia. 
Indie Pa 'Ko Ready III
Barrel House, Arayat St., Diamond Subdivision 
Balibago, Angeles City, Pampanga
6-9 p.m. 
The event is an annual music gig that promotes Original Pilipino Music and the local indie scene. Presented by the UP Musician's Guild, "Indie Pa 'Ko Ready III" will feature Psychomanagement and Chinita Phenomemon from UP Clark; Binge, Forestille and Mt. Lewis PH from Angeles City; and Sleepersecond and Goodbye Yesterday Hello Today (GYHT) from Manila. 
Pebrero 3 - Marso 31
Living Architecture with special projects by Junyee and Indy Paredes
1F Galleries, Vargas Museum
Opening reception : 3 p.m.
"Living Architecture" raises awareness of the relationship between architecture and the natural, social and cultural life of which it is part and to stimulate the creation of buildings that support these life processes. The exhibition does so by presenting projects from all over the world, which consciously relate to nature and to all human needs and thereby bring this attitude to an artistic expression. The 2018 exhibition at the Vargas Museum commissioned by Iona Stichting Foundation (Netherlands) is expanded with the site-specific works of Filipino artists Junyee and Indy Paredes. "Living Architecture" is the main presentation of the 2018 Arkitektura Festival. 
Pebrero 6-9
Workshop on Historical Demography : Preparing & Analyzing Archival Data
UP Population Institute Seminar Room
3/F Palma Hall
8:30 a.m. - 5 p.m.
This 4-day workshop aims to gather local and international researchers in an interdisciplinary conversation to address the practical issues and concerns in the study of the different population histories of Philippine localities. The primary aim is to orient and capacitate workshop participants with the theories, methodologies, historical sources and new techniques in digital technology towards the advancement of local historical demography. Another is to coordinate the different and disparate research efforts across organizations with the Population Institute of the UPD College of Social Sciences and Philosophy, providing institutional leadership to standardize the methods being applied in the different studies under the Social History of the Philippine Population Initiative. The workshop is supported by the UP Office of the Vice Chancellor for Research and Development, the UP Office of the Vice President for Academic Affairs and the Demographic Research and Development Foundation. 
Pebrero 6
Ms. UP Clark 2018 Coronation Night 
Teresa Hall, Angeles City, Pampanga 
6-9 p.m.
"Ms. UP Clark 2018," formerly known as Mr. and Ms. UP, is an annual event hosted by the UP Association of Economics Students (AES) of UP Clark. This year's theme, "Ethnic : Unravel the Beauty of the Past," aims to showcase the rich culture of our ethnic tribes and to encourage Filipinos to help in their preservation. Contestants will be working hand in hand with the contest organizers in implementing a project that will benefit the ethnic community. 
Pebrero 9
Seremonya ng Pagbubukas : Karanasan ng Kat(h)awan 
UP Amphitheater 
5:30 p.m.
Bilang panimula ng "UP Diliman Festival of Culture and the Arts 2018," tatalakayin ang katawan bilang paksa ng iba't ibang diskurso tulad ng pagnanasa, kaluwalhatian, "masculine," "female" at "queer gaze" sa pamamagitan ng mga sayaw, "spoken word poetry," at dramatikong pagbasa ng katha ni Gilda Cordero-Fernando. Tampok sa palatuntunan sina Rochelle Pangilinan ng Sexbomb, Masculados Does, Kare Adea, Dzeli Del Mundo at Orlando Oribiana. 
Pebrero 10
Asian Culture Fest 2018
Palma Hall Parking Lot
9 a.m. - 4 p.m.
The UP Department of Linguistics, with the support of The Japan Foundation Manila, is holding the Asian Culture Fest 2018. The event aims to introduce Asian cultures to Asian language students and the general public through games, food, costumes, dance, and other activities. The Department's language classes and student organizations will each set up a booth. 
Pebrero 11
COEXIST : Help Rebuild Libraries and Communities 2018 
Color Buddy Fun Run
UP Academic Oval (Start)/Melchor Hall Finish) 4:30 - 5 a.m. (Registration and Assembly) 6 a.m. (Start of run) A fundraising activity for the benefit of the UP College of Engineering Library's outreach program which would support the rehabilitation of war-torn libraries in Marawi City. It also intends to promote the Library's I Love My Library Campaign to raise awareness on the importance of involving the community within the libraries. 
Pebrero 13-15
ISKOLOR : Iba-ibang Kulay, Lahat Pantay-pantay
UPIS Days 2018
UPIS 7-12 Building
7 a.m. - 8 p.m. 
Ito ang taunang school fair na naglalayong maitanghal ang husay at talento ng mga mag-aaral sa pamamagitan ng iba't ibang gawain, gayundin ang mapagtibay ang ugnayan ng bawat miyembro ng paaralan. Batay sa adbokasiya ng tema ng pagdiriwang ngayong taon, pagtutuunan ang nga kulay bilang simbolo ng pagkakaisa at pagrespeto sa kapwa sa kabila ng magkakaibang pinagmulan, kasarian, paniniwala, relihiyon at iba pang aspeto. Sa mga nagnanais dumalo at makibahagi, may fair tickets na maaaring mabili sa mga nakasaad na petsa.
Pebrero 13-28
(Com)modified Bodies in the Philippines : An Installation Exhibit
Asian Center
The indoor exhibit includes "the history of the beautiful in the Philippines," mundane rituals of everyday life, body modifications in the country and documentary films on body technologies. As each portrait tells their personal story, the gallery contemplates on motivations, processes, and beliefs around modification of the human anatomy : physiological and physical. Body alterations test the body's natural boundaries of how much pain it can endure. Each person's submission to pain and eventually relish the pleasure of their transformation is overwhelmed by the desire to make the body malleable to the shape of the ideal self. While each portrait unpacks a particular narrative, their altered bodies also complicate and interrogate body aesthetics and identity making. 
Pebrero 13
The Chemical Youth Project
3 p.m. Conference
6 p.m. Film showing
Based on a research collaboration of the Department of Anthropology, UP Diliman and the University of Amsterdam, it focuses on body sites and body technologies where the body is perceived as the target of social engineering, advertising and marketing. The event features the launching of the ethnographic study on body technologies conducted in the port areas of Southern Luzon and Indonesia and the premiere of the documentary film "Sweet Medicine."
Pebrero 13-17
UP Fair 2018
Sa Tugtog Mo'y Buhay
UP Sunken Garden
6 p.m.
The biggest annual student-initiated activity in UP Diliman, this year's theme is "Highlighting Resilience of the Filipino Spirit Through Progressive Change." The fair is a 5-day musical feast featuring established and budding local bands. 
Pebrero 14
Panawagan para sa Papel
Iniimbitahan ng Kolehiyo ng Arte at Literatura (KAL) ang mga gradwadong mag-aaral ng kolehiyo na magpasa ng abstrak sa ika-3 KAL Kolokyum ng Gradwadong Mag-aaral na gaganapin sa Abr. 7, 2018. Malawak ang saklaw na paksa ng kolokyum at hinihikayat ang kontribusyon mula sa iba't ibang larangan ng pag-aaral sa arte at humanidades. Ang pangunahing layunin ng kolokyum ay maglaan ng lugar sa mga gradwadong mag-aaral ng KAL upang magtipun-tipon at makipagpalitan ng kaalaman at karanasan patungkol sa mga partikular na disiplina na interes at mas malawak na komunidad ng gradwadong mag-aaral ng kolehiyo. 
Pebrero 15
Moving care between Japan and The Philippines : Policy, Theory and Experience 
A Forum and Roundtable Discussion
Seminar ROom, UP Asian Center
12:30-4:30 p.m.
This will discuss the relation between migration, regarded as the most intense source of social, cultural and historical linkage between Japan and the Philippines; and care, as one of the key aspects through which recent labor and family migration between Japan and the Philippines may be examined. The forum-roundtable will gather and highlight various perspectives from academics, government officials, migration brorkers and non-governmental organizations in order to examine care as a cultural ideology, a social and migration policy and a framework for evaluating shifts (and continuities) in Japan-Philippine relations. Organized by UP Asian Center, it is free and open to the public, but seating is on a first-come, first-served basis. 
Pebrero 15-Marso 17
Beyond Myself
Exhibition on Filipino Migrants' Investments in Philippine Futures
3F Galleries, Vargas Museum
Opening reception : 3 p.m.
This feature works by Natalie Dagmang, Guhit Kulay HK and migrant workers in Hong Kong through the Voices of Women Media, with cooperative efforts from Scalabrini Migration Center Philippines, University of Hong Kong and Keele University. Co-presented by the Curating Development program of Goldsmiths, University of London, this explores various understandings of the idea of value and investment in moving and shifting contexts : value of people, images, objects and money in trans-border contexts. Specifically, it draws on and curates visual images produced mainly through a series of participatory art workshops with Filipinos living and working in London and Hong Kong. 
Pebrero 15
UPD One Billion Rising 2018
Quezon Hall
3 - 6 p.m.
Bumangon, lumaban, magkaisa!
Muling magsasama-sama ang komunidad ng UP Diliman para sa taunang kampanyang "One Billion RIsing (OBR)." Ang OBR ay ang pambansang kampanya para labanan ang karahasan, partikular sa mga kababaihan. Sa ikalimang taon ng OBR sa UP Diliman, tangan ng komunidad ang tema ng pagbangon, paglaban at pagkakaisa. Ang OBR ay hudyat ng bisperas ng Buwan ng Kababaihan. Iniimbitahan ng Diliman Gender Office na lumahok ang lahat ng mga mag-aaral, guro, REPS, kawaning administratibo, pati na rin ang iba pang organisasyon at sektor na nasa loob ng UP Campus. 
Pebrero 17
28th Rockstruck Battle of the Bands
Ihawash, Angeles City, Pampanga
5 p.m.
An interschool battle of the bands held annually by the UP Psychology Society (PsychSoc), it gathers amateur bands from UP Clark and other universities in Central Luzon universities, mostly in Pampanga. Ten bands will battle it out, on a night of fun and rock and roll, with songs from the '80s. Each band will play two songs : one song is in line with the theme and a song of their choice. Part of the proceeds will go to UP PsychSoc's outreach programs. 
Pebrero 19-23
2nd Training Course on Road Works Safety
National Center for Transportation Studies (NCTS) Building
8 a.m.- 5 p.m.
The UP NCTS, in cooperation with the Department of Public Works and Highways, offers this to provide a uniform approach to worksite traffic management through the use of standard signs and layouts that can be applied the most road work situations. Target participants are municipal/city and transportation planners, municipal/city and traffic engineers, and local and national government personnel involved in transportation and related functions. This is open to the public on a first-come, first-served basis. 
Pebrero 19
Patalasan
UPDEPP Campus, Clarkfield, Pampanga
1-4 p.m. 
The event is an annual inter high school quiz bee that aims to foster social awareness, teamwork and friendly competition among local high schools, and promote UP Diliman - Extension Program in Pampanga (UPDEPP) and its various degree programs to the brightest students in the region. Hosted by Sigma Kappa Pi Fraternity and Sigma Delta Pi Sorority UPDEPP Chapter, the quiz bee is open to the public. 
Pebrero 20-23
Bodies in/and Asian Theatres
Conference 2018
Asian Theatre Working Group
GT-Toyota Auditorium 
UP Diliman
On the occasion of "Kat(h)awan" [a contraction of katha (creative creation) and katawan (body)] or the celebration of the UP Diliman Month 2018 vis-a-vis the National Arts Month in the Philippines, "Bodies in/and Asian Theatres" invokes the Asian bodies and creative and critical entities. The international conference is envisioned to contribute to the study of the body, particularly its functions and placements in the different performances in the Asian region. This is in context of its peoples' experiences, colonization, decolonization and globalization, albeit different modalities. The event is organized by the Asian Theatre Working Group, International Federation for Theatre Research (IFTR), UP Diliman, UPD Office for Initiatives in Culture and the Arts, College of Arts and Letters, Asian Center, Department of Speech Communication and Theatre Arts, UP Diliman Information Office and UP Theater Complex. 
Pebrero 21 - Marso 11
Ang Dalagita'y 'sang Bagay na Di-buo
Wilfrido Ma. Guerrero Theater
2/F Palma Hall
Weekday (Wed-Fri) shows 7 p.m.
Weekend shows 10 a.m. and 3 p.m. 
Dulaang UP brings to the Philippine stage Eimear McBride's haunting novel "A Girl is a Half-formed Thing" (Goldsmith Prize Award 2013) billed as "Ang Dalagita'y 'sang Bagay Na Di-Buo." Translated by Rody Vera, the play is based on Annie Ryan's stage adaptation of the critically-acclaimed novel, originally staged by The Corn Exchange Theatre Company in 2014. "Ang Dalagita'y 'sang Bagay Na Di-buo" tells in fragmented, poetic and partly ambiguous language, the tragic life of a Girl, who has been repeatedly abused by her uncle, who loses her beloved brother, grapples with the wicked religiosity of her mother and increasingly finds herself in that corner of her life where no option is left. "Ang Dalagita'y 'sang Bagay na Di-buo" is a harrowing piece that lays bare in no delicate terms, the appalling iniquity of sexual abuse and power.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 4, Blg 3 (3 copies)
column7:
Abril 10
La Fashionista 
Nature Couture : Connecting with nature to change the future
Teresa Hall, Uni-chef's Catering
Angeles City, Pampanga
6 - 9 p.m.
La Fashionista is the annual fashion competition of the UP Clark Business Management Society where students can explore their talents infashion. This year's La Fashionista is about nature-based designs that are environment-friendly and promote connectivity and awareness to save the planet. Competing student designers will have their clothes modeled by their fellow students. 
Abril 11-13
Worker's Institute on Labor Laws (WILL) Class 171
Isabelo Delos Reyes Auditorium
UP School of Labor and Industrial Relations (SOLAIR)
9 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The seminar will enable the participants to : acquire basic knowledge about labor laws and legislation of the country; apply the legal processes and techniques in their respective workplaces; and understand and share experiences and practices involving labor laws and industrial relations. 
Abril 11 - Mayo 14
Lawas
Site-specific Installation Exhibit
Lawas is about three site-specific installations that examine representations of the body and shifting modes of perception. Featured artists are Agnes Arellano, Lee Paje and Mark Justiniani. Arellano's "Project Pleiades 2," cast stone goddesses, four of seven of the open star cluster most visible to the naked eye at night will be situated in an area between UP Vargas Museum and UP Faculty Center; while Paje's "Pagpamulak," stylized sculptures meant to mimic the body's intimate parts will be installed at the corner of Roxas Avenue and TM Kalaw St., Justiniani's "Oasis" is a structure likened to a navel, an invisible umbilical cord between heaven and earth. It will be constructed at the UP Lagoon area across UP Faculty Center. 
Abril 12
Understanding population heterogeneity in demographic and human capital dynamics : How does population heterogeneity matter for the future population outlook? A public lecture by Dr. Samir K.C.
Palma Hall Room 1314-1316
10 a.m. - 12 p.m. 
Age and gender differentials are the two fundamental sources of population heterogeneity within a country that are considered in almost all demographic analysis. Recent researches demonstrated the importance of education (attainment) as a third significant source of population heterogeneity. Based on a series of publications and modeling studies, Dr. Samir K.C. and colleagues have also shown the significance of place of residence (urban or rural) and administrative regions as important sources of observable population heterogeneity. Samir K.C. is a professor and leader at the Human Capital and Development Pillar at the Asian Demographic Research Institute, Shanghai University, China. 
Abril 12-14
ISKOPRENEUR 2018
UP Diliman Extension Program in Pampanga (UPDEPP) Clarkfield, Pampanga
"Iskopreneur," an annual bazaar organized by the Business Management seniors of UPDEPP, aims to develop the entrepreneurial skills of the students through the application of business management concepts in manning a physical store in a bazaar setting. This year's theme is "Iskoprenotes : Playing a Harmonious Rhythm of Unity in Diversity." The students will embody the characteristics of the diverse genre of music through their costumes and booth aesthetics. The theme intends to send a message of "unity in diversity." Despite of the divisiveness in race, culture, religion, gender and sexual orientation, people can work together towards one goal. The 3-day event will be filled with fun activities such as quiz bees, concerts and various competitions related to music. The event is open to the public. 
Abril 16 - 20
Inclusive Tourism in the City
Room 203, UP School of Labor and Industrial Relations (SOLAIR)
8:30 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The 5-day workshop focuses on exploring the potential and challenges of inclusive development in tourism. It also aims to share ideas across a wide-range of disciplinary perspectives. Thematic discussions per day covers perspectives on : community and urban space; human rights and employment; culture; marginalized community; and the future. The workshop is a collaborative effort between the University of Strathclyde (United Kingdom) and UP Asian Institute of Tourism, together with the College of Social Sciences and Philosophy, College of Social Work and Community Development, SOLAIR and School of Urban Planning and Development. 
Abril 17
Musika ng mga Filipino sa Radyo sa Panahon ng Kolonyalismong Amerikano 
A public lecture-concert of Prof. Elizabeth L. Enriquez
Television Studio B, Media Center 
College of Mass Communication
6-8 p.m.
Naging mahalagang kasangkapan ang radyo upang mapalaganap ng mga Amerikano ang wikang Ingles at musikang kanluranin noong panahon ng kolonyalismong Amerikano sa Pilipinas. Ngunit nang buksan nila ang mga unang himpilan ng radyo sa bansa noong dekada 1920 at 1930, kinailangan nila ang mga mang-aawit at musikerong Filipino upang awitin at tugtugin ang mga himig nila sa himpapawid. Ito ang naging daan upang maisingit ng mga Filipino ang sariling awit at maging ang sariling wika sa kanilang mga programa sa radyo. Kaya bagama't naging popular ang jazz at iba pang banyagang musika sa pamamagitan ng radyo, pumailanlang rin ang kundiman at iba pang musikang maituturing na sariling atin, at maging mga bagong himig Filipino na may bahid ng kanluraning musika. May mag pagkakataon na sumahimpapawid ang ilang awiting taglay ang paglaban sa kolonyal na kondisyon. 
Abril 26 - 28
Traffic Management Course for Traffic Law Enforcers (Level 1) 
National Center for Transportation Studies (NCTS) Building
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The course provides training for personnel who are required to enforce traffic laws and regulations, allows enforcers to understand their role as a traffic enforcer and understand the different laws and regulations they are going to enforce. The program also covers the standard traffic signs and pavement markings - their meaning, and when and where to install them. Enforcers are also taught how to direct and control traffic in an intersection; administer traffic citation ticket and investigation; and recording and reporting of traffic incident. Target participants are traffic law enforcers and private traffic crash investigators, engineers, lawyers, safety officers and local and national government personnel involved in traffic management. Minimum degree requirement for participants is college level. 
Abril 27
University Student Council (USC) Elections / Halalan UPD 2018
HalalanUPD2018 is the selection of leaders of elective positions in the University Student Council (USC). The elections aims to uphold the autonomous, democratic and representative character of the USC. The University Student Electoral Board is composed of the Vice Chancellor for Student Affairs as ex-officio Chairperson, two faculty members and two students. 
Abril 30
13th Philippine Linguistics Congress Call for Abstracts
The Philippine Linguistics Congress focuses on the most current advances in the study of Philippine languages, and on the application of linguistic theory to other disciplines in the Philippines. The conference also looks into contemporary studies between Philippine languages and foreign languages. Abstracts dealing with theoretical, applied or experimental linguistic study on any Philippine languages are invited for presentation at the conference. Other topics include but are not limited to : Structural linguistics; Diachronic linguistics; Dialectology and Typology; Language documentation; Language education and learning; Language planning; Sociolinguistics; Psycholinguistics; Ethnolinguistics; Translation studies; Lexicography; Clinical linguistics; and Philosophy of language.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 4, Blg 5 (2 copies)
column7:
Hunyo 1, 4-8
FAMAS Rising at Cine Adarna
Year's best with the country's oldest existing award-giving body
Cine Adarna, UPFI Film Center
2:30 p.m., 5 p.m. and 7:30 p.m.
The UP Film Institute presents a selection of full-length, short and documentary features nominated for the Filipino Academy of Movie Arts and Sciences (FAMAS) Awards in time for the 66th Gabi ng Parangal honors night to be held on June 10 at The Theater, Solaire. 
Hunyo 2 - Agosto 9
Paralegal Training Program
14th Foundation Course
Malcolm Hall
Tuesdays and Thursdays (5-8 p.m.)
Saturdays (9 a.m.- 12 p.m.)
The Paralegal Training Program is an extension service and training program of the UP Law Center which aims to lay the foundation for the development and professionalization of paralegal work in the country. The Program was designed to provide participants with basic and practical knowledge of substantial legal concepts pertaining to government; the judicial system; civil, criminal and quasi-judicial procedure; jurisdiction of local government units; ethical standards of judges, lawyers and civil servants; and the rules, regulation and procedures of various administrative agencies. 
Hunyo 2
World Bicycle Day
"REQUITED" Screenings
Celebrating the first World Bicycle Day at the UPFI Film Center
Videotheque, UPFI Film Center
1 p.m., 3 p.m. and 5:30 p.m. 
In celebration of the first official International World Bicycle Day this coming June 3, the UP Film Institute is screening "Requited" directed by Nerissa Picadizo. The film aims to put a spotlight on the long-distance biking culture of our country as seen through the characters of Matt (Jake Cuenca) and Sandy (Anna Luna) as they travel from Manila to majestic locations like Mt. Pinatubo Lake, Monasterio de Tarlac and Capas National Shrine. There will be a Q&A session with the director after every screening. 
Hunyo 4 - 15
DOST PHIL-LIDAR 1 Program and DILG LiDAR - GIS and Flood Disaster Management Training for Local Government Units (LGUs)
Geodetic Engineering Theater, 4th Floor, Melchor Hall
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) and the Department of Science and Technology (DOST) tapped the Phil-LIDAR Program to disseminate Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) products and to utilize and apply knowledge on open source geospatial processing tools, and come up with effective local government plans to more municipalities. Selected LGUs were asked to send representatives from their local DILG, Planning, Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) and Engineering Offices. DOST and DILG Regional Offices were also invited. Covering 17 regions of the country, the training aims to expand the reach and power of GIS and LiDAR products in developing more proactive and responsive DRRM plans for the LGUs. 
Hunyo 4 - 8
TEC 2018
Teaching Effectiveness Courses
Teodoro Room, 3rd Floor, UP ISSI
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The TEC is a 5-day (40 hours) experiental course organized by the Office of the Advancement for Teaching (under the Office of the Vice Chancellor for Academic Affairs) leading to the design, development, try-out and revision of teaching plans. The activities are highly structured and cumulatively sequenced to achieve the intended learning outcomes (e.g., design of an effective teaching plan). The course is open to regular faculty, lecturers, teaching assistants and teaching fellows. Priority is given to new faculty members but more experienced faculty members who wish to be updated on teaching approaches may also join. The course is free of charge but pre-registration is required. Last minute/walk-in participants will not be entertained. 
Hunyo 7
Refugees and Immigration in the US
Public lectures by Dr. Claire Angelique Nolasco and Dr. Daniel Braaten
Room 207, Palma Hall
9 a.m. - 12 p.m. 
As the world faces a global refugee crisis and political debates take place over levels of immigration and immigration policy, two public lectures on refugees and immigration in the United States (US) are co-organized by the UP Department of Political Science and the UP Center for Integrative and Development Studies (CIDS) Program on Social and Political Change. Nolasco's lecture gives an analysis of US federal court jurisprudence to determine the legal rights of unaccompanied alien children in various stages of immigration enforcement proceedings. The lecture by Braaten explores how state capacity, state politics and the religious composition of federal states explain refugee resettlement patterns within the US. 
Hunyo 13
Performance : Daloy Dance Company
A collateral event in line with Lawas Public Art
Vargas Museum grounds (near CAL Lawn)
6 p.m.
Dancers wearing animal masks will follow a trail around Agnes Arellano's work Pleiades through dance movements. Led by artistic director Ea Torrado, this performance by Daloy Dance Company is a collateral event in line with Lawas Public Art. This event is free to the public. Lawas is the public art component of the UP Diliman Festival of Culture and the Arts 2018. It features the works of Agnes Arellano, Mark Justiniani and Lee Paje at the CAL Lawn, Lagoon and Vargas Museum Sculpture Garden, respectively. Lawas may be viewed until August 2018. 
Hunyo 14
Pagtatapos 2018
UP Film Center
2 p.m.
Ang UP Integrated School (UPIS) ay magdiriwang ng Pagtatapos 2018 na may temang "Iskong Mahal ang Bayan, Manindigan para sa Katotohanan." Sa seremonyang ito, kikilalanin ang mga unang mag-aaral ng UPIS na magsisipagtapos sa ilalim ng K-12 Program ng Department of Education. 
Hunyo 20
AGHAMBAYAN
A DOST-UP Science and Technology and Innovation Festival 
Philippine International Convention Center, Pasay City
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
AGHAMBAYAN celebrates the scientific projects and accomplishments of the University of the Philippines (UP) that impact the seemingly ordinary challenges of daily living. The Department of Science and Technology (DOST) supports UP in these endeavors. Through this festival, UP and DOST hope to raise public awareness of the vaue of science and its role in improving the quality of life of ordinary Filipinos. There will be an exhibition of scientific works classified into four clusters : health and well-being, environment, food, and human resource and job creation. There will also be a conference with short panel discussions and breakout sessions that would expound on the policy implications, utilization and uses of scientific research and innovations that benefit the public. 
Hunyo 21-22
Microsoft Excel Training : Intermediate Training Course
CSRC Computer Lab 1 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The UP Computational Science Research Center (CSRC) is conducting a two-day training on Microsoft Excel. Target participants are those who want to familiarize themselves with  Excel's terminology, screen components and most used functions, which are beneficial in making reports. 
Hunyo 24
Ika-107 Pangkalahatang Pagtatapos
UP Amphitheater
7 a.m. 
Sa taunang Pangkalahatang Pagtatapos, iginagawad ng Unibersidad ang titulo sa mga mag-aaral na nagtapos ng kani-kanilang programa sa di-gradwado at gradwadong antas. Ito ay iba pa sa mga pagtatapos na isinasagawa ng bawat kolehiyo o linangan ng UP Diliman. Ang tema sa taong ito ay "Paglayang Minamahal." Ang pangunahing tagapagsalita ay si Senator Loren Legarda ng gagawaran ng titulong Doktor ng Batas (honoris causa). Ito ang ikaapat na pagkakataon na gagawin ang Pagtatapos sa buwan ng Hunyo mula ng ilipat ang pagsisimula ng pasukan sa Agosto noong 2014. 
Hunyo 29
Chopin : Before and Beyond
Abelardo Hall Auditorium
7 p.m. 
Featuring international concert pianist Albert Tiu in an evening of Chopin classics, the concert is the last offering of the Abelardo Hall Concert Series 1 of the UP College of Music.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 4, Blg 6 (6 copies)
column7:
Hulyo 2, 6-7
FAMAS Rising at Cine Adarna
Year's best with the country's oldest existing award-giving body
Cine Adarna, UPFI Film Center
2:30, 5 and 7 p.m. 
The UP Film Institute presents an extended screening of short and documentary features nominated for the 2018 Filipino Academy of Movie Arts and Sciences (FAMAS) Awards led by eventual winners : Best Documentary Film "Yield" directed by Victor Delotavo Tagaro and Toshihiko Uriu, Best Short Film "Hilom" by P.R. Patindol, and joint winners of the Grand Jury Prize for Short Films "Aliens Ata" directed by Glenn Barit and "Dory" by Beverly Ramos. FAMAS is an organization of prize-winning writers and movie columnists established in 1952. It is the oldest existing film industry award-giving body in the Philippines. 
Hulyo 3 at 16
Iran's Film Festival
Videotheque, UPFI FIlm Center
1:30, 4 p.m. and 6:30 p.m.
The Cultural Section, Embassy of the Islamic Republic of Iran-Manila in partnership with the UP Film Institute proudly presents Iran's FIlm Festival. 
Hulyo 3 - 27
Tumba Tumba Children's Museum of Philippine Art
CANVAS - The Center for Art, New Ventures, and Sustainable Development
1F and 3F Galleries, UP Vargas Museum 
Tue - Sat, 9 a.m. - 5 p.m. except holidays
The exhibit is CANVAS' proof of concept that transforms the Vargas Museum into a study of an actual operating children's museum complete with a museum shop. Three new CANVAS books for children, two of which written by Genaro Gojo Cruz and Eugene Evasco, were unveiled through the art of Arvi, Fetalvero, Jared Yokte and Jojo Barja. "Abubot," a special show, features art works by Aze Ong, Sergio Bumatay III, Art Sanchez, Renz Baluyot, Liza Flores and more. Interactive installations by Elmer Borlongan, Pam Yan Santos and Daniel dela Cruz, two murals by Archie Oclos and a rainbow installation and a scale model presentation of the future site of the children's museum by the architectural firm Arkisens are also showcase in the exhibition. 
Hulyo 3-27
Bridges : Marianao Ponce & Jorge Vargas
In cooperation with Museo ng Baliwag
3F Sensorium, UP Vargas Museum 
Tue-Sat, 9 a.m. - 5 p.m. except holidays
The exhibition explores the political roles of the two diplomats, Mariano Ponce and Jorge Vargas, during tumultuous times as the Philippines built its sense of nationhood and sought its independence in light of different forces amidst newly established governments. The exhibit engages the audience through its interactive and tactile activities in order to draw the youth to the relevance of history and the creation of a collective identity. This exhibition is part of the year-long programs of the Municipality of Baliwag and Museo ng Baliwag for the 100th Death Anniversary of Mariano Ponce. 
Hulyo 5
CineManunuri Film Festival
Videotheque, UPFI Film Center
1:30 and 4 p.m.
Manunuri ng Pelikulang Pilipino, Cinema Centenario together with the UP Film Institute present a selection of nominated and awarded films of the 41st Gawad Urian Awards. Featured at the Videotheque are the following : 1:30 p.m. "Sorry for the Inconvenience" directed by Carl Adrian Chavez, "Gikan sa Ngitngit Nga Kinailadman" by Kiri Dalena, "Empyreus" by Dennis Hubag, "Redempsyon" by Edmund Telmo and "Caramel Child" by Kim Timan; 4 p.m. "Han-ayan" by JL Burgos. 
Hulyo 9
Burning Issues in Korea, China, and Malaysia : Alternatives and Prospects for People's Movements 
UP CIDS Conference Hall, Lower Ground Floor
Ang Bahay ng Alumni
1:30 - 5 p.m. 
The event will examine current burning issues in Asia such as the Belt and Road Initiative, Chinese President XI Jinping's expanded powers, the US-North Korea summit and denuclearization in the Korean Peninsula and the return of Mahathir as Malaysia's leader. The discussion will be led by Asian Regional Exchange for New Alternatives (ARENA) fellows Lee Jung Ok, Ph.D. (Catholic University of Daegu, South Korea), Lau Kin Chi, Ph. D. (Lingnan University, Hong Kong), Margaret Jade Sit Tsui, Ph.D. (Southwest University, Chongqing, People's Republic of China) and Andrew Aeria, Ph.D. (Universiti Malaysia Sarawak, Malaysia). 
Hulyo 16 - Agosto 11
Linescapes : Aesthesizing Chaos of Urban Infrastructure
MFA degree exhibition by Annie Dennise Pacaña Lumbao
Bulwagan ng Dangal, University Heritage Museum
6 p.m. (opening)
Linescapes is an MFA degree show that explores one's urban experience through art, technology, contemporary dance and counter-cartography. Here, the city and its chaos are expressed through photography, line drawing, moving image and new media, mapping connectivity. An introductory event is set on July 3 to 5, from 6 to 9 p.m. where a projection+performance entitled "Waiting" will be held at the UP Gateway commuter shed along the University Avenue. A Lost Frames screening+talk is also scheduled on August 10 at the Bulwagan ng Dangal at 3 p.m., coinciding with the artist's talk. 
Hulyo 17-20, 23-28 at 30-31
TOFARM Film Festival
Videotheque, UPFI Film Center
1:30, 4 and 6:30 p.m.
The UP Film Institute presents the full showcase of features from the first (2016) and last year's edition of the TOFARM Film Festival, the only advocacy-driven film festival in the country today, with entries showcasing the lives, journeys, trials and triumphs of the Filipino farmer. The first edition's line-up include "Free Range" directed by Dennis Marasigan, "Kakampi" by Vic Acedillo Jr., "Pauwi Na" by Paolo Villaluna, "Paglipay" by Zig Dulay, "Pilapil" by Johnny Nadela and "Pitong Kabang Palay" by Mariel Cariaga. The 2017 edition is comprised of "Baklad" by Topel Lee, "High Tide" by Tara Illenberger, "Instalado" by Jason Paul Laxamana, "Kamunggai" by Vic Acedillo Jr., "Sinandomeng" by Byron Bryant and "What Home Feels Like" by Joseph Abello.
Hulyo 20, 28; Agosto 4, 11, 18
Digital Video Production Workshop
UPFI Media Center Bldg. Film Studio 
1-6 p.m.
To be facilitated by award-winning director Milo Paz, the short course will discuss and apply pre-production, production and post-production management as well as the technical and creative functions of a digital video production crew. Paz's documentaries and short features were all recipients of the Gawad CCP para sa Alternatibong Video at Pelikula. He previously worked as an executive producer and director for GMA 7 and now regularly produces videos for NGOSs, government agencies and private institutions on important issues and advocacies. 
Hulyo 28; Agosto 4, 11, 18 (4 na Sabado)
Film Acting Workshop
UPFI Media Center Bldg. Room B4 
1-6 p.m.
The course is designed to equip workshop participants with a fundamental understanding of the art of acting as applied to digital material such as film, TV, commercials and other digital content. The workshop is to be facilitated by TV and film character actor Rolando Inocencio. He teaches theater and film subjects at the Meridian International College and conducts workshops for Octoarts and Regal Films, among others. He has taken acting workshops under Ana Valdes-Lim, Noa Rotem, Kiara Pipino (Michael Chekov), John Maloney (Viewpoints) and Ron Burrows of Stella Adler Studio.

column3:
Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 4, Blg 7 (6 copies)
column7:
Agosto 1 at 2 
All Features from TOFARM Film Festival
Full showcase from the festival's year 1 and 2 editions
Videotheque, UPFI Film Center 
The UP Film Institute (UPFI) presents the full showcase of features from the first (2016) and last year's edition of the TOFARM Film Festival, the only advocacy-driven film festival in the country today, with entries showcasing the lives, journeys, trials and triumphs of the Filipino farmer.
Agosto 3
Francesco Conte's "Filipinos in the Eyes of an Italian"
Panel discussion, project presentation, film screening and open forum on Filipino migrant workers in Italy
Conference Hall
UP Center for Integrative and Development Studies (CIDS)
2 p.m. 
In this project, Francesco Conte depicts an in-depth portrait of Filipinos in Italy. The interviews in the film focused on Pinoys answering questions about what pushed them to move abroad and the challenges and fulfillments of living in Italy. This project will also contextualize and discuss the situation of Filipinos in Italy. 
Agosto 3 at 4
School Opening Special with Slashed-Price Admission 
Videotheque, UPFI Film Center
Enjoy full-length films at half the usual price at the campus cinema. Featured are the following : action-drama-historical Cinco De Mayo (La Batalla) directed by Rafa Lara; suspense-thriller Backstabbing for Beginners directed by Per Fly; and suspense-horror June directed by L. Gustavo Cooper. 
Agosto 4 - Oktubre 20 (12 Sabado)
Management Development Program
Isabelo Delos Reyes Auditorium
School of Labor and Industrial Relations (SOLAIR)
9 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The program is a short and comprehensive course to learn basic managerial and leadership skills in the application of technologies in management of organizations and enterprises. It is designed for cooperatives, non-government organizations, trade unions and similar organization like small and medium enterprises. 
Agosto 6-10
Training Course on Road Works Safety
Toyota Training Room, UP NCTS Building 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
The UP National Center for Transportation Studies (NCTS), in cooperation with the Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH), will offer Road Works Safety Course to provide a uniform approach to worksite traffic management by using the Road Works Safety Manual developed by DPWH. The program will train the participants to be competent in planning, organizing, directing and managing the flow of traffic through road works with the minimum delay and risk to other users. 
Agosto 7
Linguistics Month 2018
In celebration of its 96th founding anniversary, the UP Department of Lingustics has prepared a roster of activities for the whole month of August that will showcase current research on the burning issues in Philippine languages and linguistics. The month-long celebration will kick off with a Department Welcome Assembly on Aug. 7, to be attended by Linguistics freshies, as well as new shiftee and transferee students. The annual Student Research Colloquium and Faculty Research Colloquium will be held on Aug. 14 and Aug. 17, respectively. On Aug. 18, the Department will hold the Asian Culture Fest, which will highlight the textile traditions of ethnolinguistic groups in the Philippines and in other Asian countries. The second installment of the Nanay Talks has invited experts that would talk about raising multilingual children, and will happen on Aug. 23. The Linguistics Month 2018 will conclude via the relaunch of The Archive, the Department's journal devoted to the study of Philippine languages and dialects, which will happen on Aug. 25. 
Agosto 8
Book Launching of 'Twentieth Century Philippine Political Thinkers : Selected Readings' Edited by Jorge V. Tigno
2nd floor lobby, Palma Hall
3 p.m. 
The book is a collection of readings from 13 Filipino political thinkers of the 20th century. The thinkers include Maximo M. Kalaw, Onofre D. Corpuz, Pura Villanueva Kalaw, Ricardo R. Pascual, Remigio E. Agpalo, Felipe Agoncillo, Pedro L. Baldoria, Estrella D. Solidum, Jose P. Laurel, Renato Constantino, Claro M. Recto, Cesar A. Majul and Jose Maria Sison. A short essay written by Department of Political Science fauclty members introduces each thinker and reading. The contributors are Athena Lydia Casambre, Miriam Coronel-Ferrer, Perlita M. Frago-Marasigan, Jan Robert R. Go, Herman Joseph S. Kraft, Ruth Lusterio-Rico, Jaime B. Naval, Jalton G. Taguibao, Jorge V. Tigno and Jean Paul L. Zialcita. 
Agosto 10 at 11
IAM4ME Digital Summit and Festival
Cine Adarna, UPFI Film Center
The event aims to gather students of mass communication, media, arts, marketing, educaiton and psychology. The activities are : Digital Intelligence Summit (with digital citizenship, privacy management and digital footprint, critical thinking and fake new discernment, cyberbullying and digital empathy as topics); Screenings of advocacy movies; and YAEEHA DEMIC Awards (starting at 7:30 a.m.) on Aug. 11) to give recognition to exemplar digital educators, mentors, influencers and content. 
Agosto 10 - Oktubre 6
Ties of History : Art in Southeast Asia
Vargas Museum (UP Diliman), Metropolitan Museum of Manila and Yuchengco Museum (Makati City) 
5-9 p.m. (Opening Reception) 
The Philippines organizes an exhibition of contemporary art in Southeast Asia in Manila to mark the 50th year of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN). The exhibit gathers 10 artists to represent the member-states of the organization. Taken from the ASEAN Declaration in 1967, the phrase "ties of history" may allude to the blessings and burdens of being together and being different in a region that is thought to be shared. The exhibition opens on Aug. 8 (Metropolitan Museum of Manila), Aug. 9 (Yuchengco Museum) and Aug. 10 (Vargas Museum) and will run until Oct. 6. The project is presented by the National Commission for Culture and the Arts through the Dalubhasaan Para sa Edukasyon sa Sining at Kultura with the support of the Office of Senator Loren Legarda. 
Agosto 14
Student Research Colloquium
Palma Hall AVR (PH 207) 
1-4 p.m.
The colloquium is an annual Department event that aims to provide a venue for undergraduate and graduate students to share the results of their research while in their respective programs. For this year, the Colloquium will showcase the research projects of students in Linguistics 125 [Introduction to Field Methods] and Linguistics 225 [Field Methods in Linguistics], who did their fieldwork in the different ethnolinguistic communities in Baguio City during Midyea 2018. A multimedia exhibit that documents the field activities of the classes will also be launched. The Student Research Colloquium is part of the month-long celebration of Linguistics Month 2018, commemorating the Department's 96th founding anniversary. The event is free and open to the public.  
Agosto 15
Application deadline : Gemino H. Abad Seminar-Workshop on Teaching Literature 
Training teachers in junior and senior high school in discussing literary texts that are now being used in textbooks for our classrooms
The seminar-workshop aims to help establish a more standardized basis for literary discussion and analysis, as well as in choosing which works in both English and Filipino are most appropriate for inclusion in the school textbooks. In a bigger sense, the seminar-workshop aims to aid teachers in better educating the K-12 junior and senior high school students. The free 3-day workshop will be on Sept. 20-22, 2018. 
Agosto 15-18
Japanese Film Festival at UP Film Institute
EIGASAI 2018 : A Celebration of Collaboration and Diversity!
Cine Adarna, UPFI Film Center
12:30 p.m., 2:45 p.m., 5 p.m. and 7:15 p.m.
Now on its 21st edition, the Japanese Film Festival in the Philippines presented by The Japan Foundation, Manila in partnership with the UP Film Institute (UPFI), will showcase full-feature films of diverse genres selected for more than 100 screenings across five major cities nationwide. Films to be screened include : Chihayafuru Part 1-3, Tori Girl, When the Curtain Rises, Tora-san of Goto, Honnouji Hoterl, Of Love & Law, Survival Family, ReLIFE, Memoirs of a Murderer, Rudolf the Black cat, Let's Go, JETS! from Small Town Girls to U.S. Champions?! and Seven Samurai. 
Agosto 17
"Talastasan sa Kasaysayan : Ang Pampublikong Kasaysayan at Kamalayang Pangkasaysayan."
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The one-day conference on public history is in congruence with this year's History Month celebration with an overall theme "Kasaysayan : Tuklasin, Mahalin, Palaganapin.' Recognizing public history as an emerging field in the discipline, the conference primarily aims to provide an avenue for sharing and updating of knowledge on public history and its importance. Meanwhile, the more specific objectives are the following : to conduct a preliminary assessment of the state of public history in the Philippines; to identify the significance of public history in raising historical awareness and consciousness; and to understand the role of the public history in nation-building and in the fight against negative historical revisionism and disinformation. Aside from holding a one-day public history conference, other major events include the launch of the UP Department of History's website and a walking history tour (Lakad-Gunita). A forum on Martial Law will highlight this year's celebration of History Month (Buwan ng Kasaysayan 2018). 
Agosto 17
Faculty Research Colloquium
Palma Hall AVR (PH 207) 
The annual Faculty Research Colloquium serves as a platform for the members of the faculty to share with the wider public their current research on Philippine languages. It aims to establish and strengthen networks of scholars of lingustics as well as other disciplines to improve their papers or form new research projects in Philippine languages and cultures. This year, the Department will showcase linguistic papers presented during the recently held Southeast Asian Linguistics Society Conference in Taiwan. The Faculty Research Colloquium is part of the month-long celebration of Linguistics Month 2018, commemorating the Department's 96th founding anniversary. 
Agosto 18
Asian Culture Fest
Palma Hall AVR (PH 207) 
9 a.m. - 4 p.m.
As part of their celebration of the Linguistics Month 2018, the Department will hold the Asian Culture Fest. The festival is an annual event showcasing the cultures of the different ethnolinguistic groups in the Philippines as well as the other Asian countries. This year's installment of the festival focuses on Philippine and Asian textile traditions. The main speakers for the event are Professor Emeritus Norma A. Respicio and Dr. Marilyn R. Canta. Respicio will talk about shared textile cultures among Philippine ethnolinguistic groups, while Canta will deliver a lecture on the connection between India and the Philippines in terms of textile tradition. Other invited exhibitors from Japan, Korea, Indonesia and organizations in the country that create indigenous woven products will also participate in the whole-day festival. 
Agosto 18
2018 U.P. General Alumni Homecoming
"Mga Haligi ng Lahi" 
Ang Bahay ng Alumni
3 p.m.
The UP Alumni Association (UPAA) will honor Distinguished Alumni Awardees as shining examples of "Mga Haligi ng Lahi" for having best exemplified the national university's ideals of service, excellence and leadership - with their outstanding accomplishments and services rendered to their communities and the nation which have brought glory to the Alma Mater. The homecoming will likewise feature the Jubilarians - members of Class 1958 (Diamond), Class of 1968 (Golden), Class of 1978 (Ruby), and Class of 1993 (Silver), who are preparing colorful and entertaining presentations. 
Agosto 20 at Setyembre 3
2018 Orientation for New UP Diliman Faculty
National Center for Transportation Studies (NCTS) Toyota Room 
8 a.m. - 4 p.m.
The Office of the Vice Chancellor for Academic Affairs (OVCAA), with the Office for the Advancement of Teaching (OAT), will be conducting an orientation program, which will cover academic and administrative policies and programs that UP Diliman faculty need to familiarize themselves with. While priority is given to new faculty members, other faculty members and faculty teaching associate/teaching fellows could be accomodated depending on the availability of slots. OAT, the coordinating office for the event, is requesting academic units to please send the list of suggested participants thorugh ovcaa.oatdiliman@up.edu.ph (Attention : Ms. Daizy Agao)
Agosto 23-25
10th International Conference on Teacher Education
Seda Vertis North, Quezon City
The international conference is a biennial event organized by the UPD College of Education. The theme for this year is "Educational Equity through inclusion."
This year's conference underscores the role of education as an agent for genuine social cohesion and equity which requires breaking barriers and narrowing gaps between and among learners, families, schools and other communities. It advances the view that inclusive education is all-embracing, and that it "is a comprehensive process of which the diverse needs of all learners are addressed and responded to, regardless of their social, economic, cultural, linguistic, physical, or other contexts" (UNESCO, 2013). The theme problematizes how inclusive education may be fostered through teacher education and professional development. 
Agosto 23
Nanay Talks : Raising Multilingual Children
Palma Hall AVR (PH 207)
1-4 p.m.
Nanay Talks is a roundtable discussion on topics regarding language acquisition and learning, particularly through the eyes of mothers who are also linguistics and/or language teachers. This year, the Nanay Talks has invited faculty members from the language departments within UP Diliman to talk about issues on raising multilingual children. Nanay Talks : Raising Multilingual Children is part of the month-long celebration of Linguistics Month 2018, commemorating the Department's 96th founding anniversary. 
Agosto 25
Juan Balucas at ang Bayabas
National Heroes' Day feature
Cine Adarna, UPFI Film Center
4 p.m.
Inspired by the popular Juan Tamad tales that Filipino children grew up with and loved, the film is about a young boy's journey to save his fathe who has been mistaken to be Juan Tamad and is turned into stone by the mythical creature Ibong Adarna. The film is top-billed by Nourish Icon Lapuz, Archie Alemania, Rolando T. Inocencio, Peter Vallejos, Gabby Padilla, Brooke Chantelle Estillore, Ynahbel Moran, Randy De Silva, Rainier Morales and Bernard Laxa under the direction of Franco G. Arce. The film is packed with educational and cultural values as it conveys that 'Filipinos are not lazy' as its overall statement. It has been rate "G" (General Audience) by the Movie and Television Review and Classification Board (MTRCB). 
Agosto 29-31
Worker's Institute on Labor Laws (WILL) Class 173
Isabelo Delos Reyes Auditorium
School of Labor and Industrial Relations (SOLAIR)
9 a.m. - 5 p.m.
The seminar will enable the participants to acquire basic knowledge about labor laws and legislation of the country; apply the legal processes and techniques in their respective workplaces; and understand and share experiences and practices involving labor laws and industrial relations. 
Agosto 29
UP Gawad Plaridel 2018
Awarding and Lecture
UP Film Center
2 p.m. 
The UP Gawad Plaridel is the sole award in UP given to outstanding media practitioners given to outstanding Filipino media practitioners who have excelled in media (journalism, broadcast-radio and television, film and new media) and have performed with the highest level of professional integrity in the interest of public service. This year's UP Gawad Plaridel recipient is Jessica Soho, a veteran broadcast journalist. She is cited for her pioneering work in broadcast investigative journalism and for her insightful and courageous coverage of Philippine and world news. She holds the distinction of being the most awarded broadcast journalist in Philippine history with 253 local awards and 32 international awards to date. She is the first Filipina recipient of the highly acclaimed George Foster Peabody Award for her documentaries on the illegal trade of kidneys and the harrowing plight of Filipino boxers and is also a 6-time recipient of the "Most Trusted News Presenter" award of Readers Digest Asia. 
Agosto 30 -31
Curiosity, Adventure & Love
National Heroes'Day feature
Videotheque, UPFI Film Center
August 30, 4 p.m.
August 31, 7 p.m.
The documentary film weaves together three threads : the life of a 104-year-old American orphan who journeys to the Philippines alone in 1933 at age 18; the history of the country that she has witnessed and made her home over the last century; and the insights and wisdom on life of a woman whose indomitable spirit has evoked inspiration and awe for the thousands who have known her. Sitting alone in her 250-year-old Spanish colonial home, Jessie Lichauco's poetic soul draws comfort from the ebb and flow of the mighty river outside her window and protection from an ancient Banyan Tree while sharing stories of a life guided by fate, and where her innate sense of adventure, curiosity and compassion for others knows no boundaries. 
Agosto 30
Secret Reunion
Korean Arthouse Feature
Videotheque, UPFI Film Center
6:30 p.m.
UP Korea Research Center, in partnership with UP Film Institute, will hold its 2nd Korean Arthouse Feature with a film entitled "Secret Reunion" (2010) directed by Hyun Jang. It is a story about a brotherhood between North Korean secret agent Ji-won and National Intelligence Service officer Han-gyu. In the midst of the ongoing peace talks on the Korean Peninsula, the film will make the viewers think about realistic yet humanistic pictures in this divided country. 
Agosto 31
Honor : The Legacy of Jose Abad Santos
National Heroes' Day feature
Cine Adarna, UPFI Film Center
5 p.m.
A full-length documentary biopic about the life and legacy of Filipino hero of World War II Chief Justice Jose Abad Santos. The story is weaved together through interviews with his descendants, top World II Philippine historians, official documents, and excerpts from articles written by Abad Santos's son, Pepito, who was with his father through his last days.

column3:
"Ugnayan
Unibersidad ng Pilipinas Diliman. Tomo 4, Blg 8 (4 copies)"
column7:
Setyembre 3
Gender Sensitivity Training
Para sa College of Home Economics 
Seminar Room
UP Diliman Gender Office
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
Bilang pagpapatibay at pagtiyak na ang UP Diliman ay nananatiling makataong kampus na may hustisyang pangkasarian, malaya sa diskriminasyon at pang-aabuso, isinasagawa ang Gender Sensitivity Training (GST) para sa mga guro, REPS, kawaning administratibo, mga security at janitorial personnel sa loob at labas ng kampus ng UP Diliman. Bukas ito para sa mga empleyadong permanente, UP contractual at non-UP contractual.
Setyembre 3 at 10
Gender Sensitivity Orientation
Para sa Student
Organization Accreditation
Vinzons Rooftop
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Upang patibayin ang adbokasiya ng makataong kampus na may hustisyang pangkasarian, malaya sa diskriminasyon at pang-aabuso, at palawakin ang diskurso ng usaping pangkasarian sa hanay ng mga mag-aaral, isasagawa ang Gender Sensitivity Orientation (GSO) para sa mga organisasyong pangmag-aaral ng UP Diliman. 
Setyembre 6-8
Lib eFair 2018
Connecting to Research Discoveries
Main Library Lobby, Gonzalez Hall
8 a.m. - 5 p.m. 
A venue to gain insight into the improvement of academics and research in the UP community through online library resources, the University Library showcases trade booths of online databases, lectures on research productivity and exhibits on information literacy in conjunction with the UNESCO Literacy Day every September 8. This event is free and open to the public. Librarians from other organizations are also encouraged to attend. 
Setyembre 7
Philippine Music Suite : TUGMA, PADAYON and UP Dance Company 
Abelardo Hall
6 p.m.
The performance is a restaging of the Philippine Music Suite featuring the music and dances of the Cordillera, the urban lowlands and Mindanao. This was first performed for the Guangxi ASEAN Music Festival at Guangxi Arts University in Guangxi, Nanning, China held from May 28 - June 3, 2018. 
Setyembre 10
Gender Sensitivity Training
Seminar Room
UP Diliman Gender Office 
8 a.m. - 5 p.m.
Bilang pagpapatibay at pagtiyak na ang UP Diliman ay nananatiling makataong kampus na may hustisyang pangkasarian, malaya sa diskriminasyon at pang-aabuso, isinasagawa ang Gender Sensitivity Training (GST) para sa mga guro, REPS, kawaning administratibo, mga security at janitorial personnel sa loob at labas ng kampus ng UP Diliman. Bukas ito para sa mga empleyadong permanente, UP contractual at non-UP contractual. 
Setyembre 21
Exploratory workshop on "Islam in Southeast Asia"
Publication opportunities for the BRILL's International Journal of Islam in Asia and Palgrave Macmillan's Islam in Southeast Asia book series. 
UP National Institute for Science and Mathematics Education Development (NISMED) 
8 a.m. - 5:30 p.m.
The international exploratory workshop on "Islam in Southeast Asia" concentrates on publishing selected peer-reviewed academic and policy works that focuses on theoretical, methodological, empirical, religious, spiritual and critical studies of Islam, including mundane praxes and day-to-day realities of Muslims (and their relations to non-Muslims) in Southeast Asia. It is organized and sponsored by the UP CIDS Islamic Studies Program, UP Office of International Linkages, UP Institute of Islamic Studies (UP IIS), Philippine International Studies Organization and UP IIS Graduate Students Organization. 
Setyembre 21
Ginoong Maria
Premiere screening at UPFI
Cine Adarna, UP Film Institute (UPFI) 
7 p.m.
"Ginoong Maria," directed by Jon Red, is a black comedy inspired by true events. It is a fictional gangster tale and a parody of key figures in the current political arena. Dindo, now a family man, is being recruited back to a syndicate involved in the lucrative business of killing. He is torn between a personal ambition and his loved ones - wife and daughter. These imagined characters are set against the background of a more surreal scenario : a world of extrajudicial killings, fake news, misogynistic and corrupt leaders and fanatic followers. A curse that is yet to be broken. A nightmare that never sleeps. Everything is political. And everything is personal. 
Setyembre 22 at 23
UPIS Leadership Camp
UP Integrated School (UPIS) Building
7 a.m.
The UPIS Leadership Camp is a 2-day camp for the recently elected officers of different organizations in UPIS. The camp aims to hone aspiring leaders and serving officials through lectures, discussions, workshops and mini competitions that target various skills essential to student leadership. This year, it focuses on two of the five UPIS core values-love and peace. It builds up from last year's camp which revolved around truth and justice, two other UPIS core values. 
Setyembre 22
The role of the State, Education, Community Outreach, Responsible Media, and Religio-Cultural Dialogues in Countering Violent Extremism (CVE)
UP National Institute for Science and Mathematics Educaiton Development (NISMED)
8 a.m. - 5:30 p.m. 
The conference theme seeks to understand the ontologies, epistemologies, theories, studies, narratives, motivations, causes, effects and impacts of violent extremism, including exploring approaches, frameworks, solutions and mechanisms to counter it by utilizing the imperatives and significance of the state, education, sustainable community outreach programs, highly principled and responsible media, and inter/intra-faith or religio-cultural dialogues that promote religious pluralism, multiculturalism, respect for diversity, acceptance and tolerance. It is organized and sponsored by the UP CIDS Islamic Studies Program, UP Office of International Linkages, UP Institute of Islamic studies (UP IIS), Philippine International Studies Organization and UP IIS Graduate Students Organization. 
Setyembre 24-26
KOTO TRANSFORMATION : Workshop and Concert of Transcultural Koto music and Artists
Abelardo Hall, UP College of Music
Workshops and a concert with Tugtugang Musika Asyatika (TUGMA) and the Composition and Theory Department of the UP College of Music with Koto artists based in Australia, Thailand and Singapore. 
Setyembre 26 
HIV 101 and Screening
